## PAGE 00198: (2026x Refresh1) Redefinitions Utility Class

- page_id: `303989557`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989557/2026x+Refresh1+Redefinitions+Utility+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:41.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In KerML, *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Redefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html)* is a specialized [*Subsetting*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html)relationship that allows a feature in a subtype to replace, rather than merely narrow, a feature inherited from a supertype.

Unlike plain subsetting, a redefining feature takes the place of the inherited one within its owning type: the original feature is effectively hidden, and its constraints are transferred to the redefining feature.

**Getting Redefined**

[*getRedefined*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#getRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))returns all redefined features in the inheritance hierarchy. 
[*isRedefined*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#isRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))checks whether a feature redefines another.

```java
redefinedFeatures = Redefinitions.getRedefined(sportEVMass);
// → [EV::mass, Vehicle::mass]

// Check if a feature redefines another
boolean redefines = Redefinitions.isRedefined(sportEVMass, evMass);]]>
```

**Setting Redefined**

[*setRedefined*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#setRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))replaces all existing *ownedRedefinition* relationships on the redefining feature with a single new one pointing to the given feature.

Passing *null* removes all redefinitions, leaving the feature without any explicit redefinition.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In KerML, <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Redefinition</a></em> is a specialized <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html"><em>Subsetting</em> </a>relationship that allows a feature in a subtype to replace, rather than merely narrow, a feature inherited from a supertype.</p><p>Unlike plain subsetting, a redefining feature takes the place of the inherited one within its owning type: the original feature is effectively hidden, and its constraints are transferred to the redefining feature.</p><p><strong>Getting Redefined</strong></p><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#getRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>getRedefined</em> </a>returns all redefined features in the inheritance hierarchy.<br /><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#isRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>isRedefined</em> </a>checks whether a feature redefines another.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2df584da-003c-4729-81e6-b3d933d34955"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Redefinitions;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature;

List<Feature> redefinedFeatures = Redefinitions.getRedefined(sportEVMass);
// → [EV::mass, Vehicle::mass]

// Check if a feature redefines another
boolean redefines = Redefinitions.isRedefined(sportEVMass, evMass);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Setting Redefined</strong></p><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#setRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>setRedefined</em> </a>replaces all existing <em>ownedRedefinition</em> relationships on the redefining feature with a single new one pointing to the given feature.</p><p>Passing <em>null</em> removes all redefinitions, leaving the feature without any explicit redefinition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="505ba6a8-70a7-4cd2-9343-7ee5f82343ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Redefinitions;

// Make EV::mass redefine Vehicle::mass (replaces any existing)
Redefinitions.setRedefined(evMass, vehicleMass);

// Remove all redefinitions from a feature
Redefinitions.setRedefined(evMass, null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00199: (2026x Refresh1) Refactoring model elements

- page_id: `303989446`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989446/2026x+Refresh1+Refactoring+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To refactor an element in a model, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring***class.

###### **Example #1: Converting an element to an interface**

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Replacing an element with another element**

```java

```

###### **Example #3: Extract refactoring**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting***class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget*. Invoke the refactoring with *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()*. Review refactoring results by inspecting *ExtractSource* and *ExtractTarget*.

```java
allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]>
```

###### **Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring**

The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(**[Element](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html))***method.

```java

```

###### **Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection<**Element>, Element)* to move *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and all connected *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)* to a new owner.

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\refactor*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To refactor an element in a model, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring</em><em> </em>class.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #1: Converting an element to an interface</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="996e118e-32cf-4ec3-84a3-d1a0befe5030"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToConvert = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Convert");
 
    // Converts the element to an interface.
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(Interface.class);
 
    // Preserves the old element ID for the new element. 
    info.setPreserveElementID(true);
    Element conversionTarget = Refactoring.Converting.convert(elementToConvert, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>Example #2: Replacing an element with another element</strong></h4><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd05f199-c8c8-46d9-9379-1f52b2421087"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToReplace = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Replace");
 
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(elementToReplace.getClassType());
    info.setConvertOnlyIncomingReferences(true);
 
    Refactoring.Replacing.replace(element, elementToReplace, info);   
    
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><h4><strong>Example #3: Extract refactoring</strong></h4><p>Use the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget</em>. Invoke the refactoring with <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()</em>. Review refactoring results by inspecting <em>ExtractSource</em> and <em>ExtractTarget</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b25ad79-2609-464f-a3f6-e20f4e785c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Creates an extract refactor manager.
    ExtractManager extractManager = Refactoring.Extracting.createExtractManager(symbols);
    if (extractManager != null)
    {
        Project project = ...;
        // A session has to be started before refactoring. 
        SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
        sessionManager.createSession(project, "Extract Refactor Symbols");
 
        // We may control the extract refactor result by modifying extract target. 
        ExtractTarget extractTarget = extractManager.getExtractTarget();
 
        // Create a namespace to which we are going to refactor. 
        Project project = Project.getProject(symbols[0]);
        Package package1 = project.getElementsFactory().createPackageInstance();
        package1.setOwner(project.getPrimaryModel());


        // Set the namespace to which the extract result should go. 
        extractTarget.setTargetNamespace(package1);
 
        // Choose target diagram type from allowed diagram types if the default type does not suite.
        List<String>  allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List<? extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring</strong></h4><p>The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a>)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1967114d-d7fe-43aa-b363-92527bbea717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     // We have an arbitrary element or symbol which represents a relationship. 
    BaseElement baseElement = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    // Relationship reversing should be wrapped with session create/close calls. 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Reverse relation");  
 
    // Reverse the relationship. 
    Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(baseElement);
 
    // Close the session. 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner</strong></h4><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection&lt;</em><em>Element&gt;, Element)</em> to move <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and all connected <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)</em> to a new owner.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\refactor</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00200: (2026x Refresh1) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager

- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code

```text

```

In general, a new execution engine is registered at <Plugin Class>.init().

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ac1e768-d1a9-4cdc-9d40-cb4dd54f9240"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[SimulationManager.registerEngine(new MyEngineDescriptor());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In general, a new execution engine is registered at &lt;Plugin Class&gt;.init().</p>
````

## PAGE 00201: (2026x Refresh1) Registering custom hyperlink handler

- page_id: `303989121`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989121/2026x+Refresh1+Registering+custom+hyperlink+handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:20.514+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Managing navigation in model > (2026x Refresh1) Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-)* to add a custom handler.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customhyperlink

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-">)</a></em> to add a custom handler.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customhyperlink</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00202: (2026x Refresh1) Registering custom symbol renderer provider

- page_id: `303989178`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989178/2026x+Refresh1+Registering+custom+symbol+renderer+provider
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.461+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the *SlotRenderer* for slot symbols, the *PackageRenderer* for package symbols, and the *DependencyRenderer* for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager*.

****

**Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class**

```java

```

**Step 2. Registering RendererProvider**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the <em>SlotRenderer</em> for slot symbols, the <em>PackageRenderer</em> for package symbols, and the <em>DependencyRenderer</em> for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager</em>.</p></div><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class</strong> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd8b2e2c-d3fa-4500-9c26-1299c2ac27e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     /**
    * A custom renderers provider.
    */
    class RendererProvider implements PresentationElementRendererProvider
    {
        private SlotRenderer slotRenderer;
        private DependencyRenderer dependencyRenderer;
        private PackageRenderer packageRenderer;
 
        RendererProvider()
        {
            slotRenderer = new SlotRenderer(); 
            dependencyRenderer= new DependencyRenderer(); 
            packageRenderer = new PackageRenderer();
        }
 
        public PresentationElementRenderer getRenderer(PresentationElement presentationElement)
        {
            if (presentationElement instanceof SlotView || presentationElement instanceof SlotListElementView)
            {
                // A slot renderer 
                return slotRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof DependencyView)
            {
                // dependency renderer 
                return dependencyRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof PackageView)
            {
                // package renderer 
                return packageRenderer;
            }
 
            return null;
       }
    };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Step 2. Registering RendererProvider</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4088bf97-912e-4d4b-8ae4-9d5390636717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementRendererManager.getInstance().addProvider(new RendererProvider());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989565 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00203: (2026x Refresh1) Relationship-Backed Properties

- page_id: `303989565`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989565/2026x+Refresh1+Relationship-Backed+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:42.384+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2 Open API, not every property behaves like a simple field you set and forget. Some properties are **backed by model relationships** and act as convenient shortcuts for making structurally correct changes to the model.

A good example is `[Element.setOwner()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))`. It may look like you’re just assigning a parent, but in reality the API creates or updates the proper ownership relationship (for example, an `[OwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html)`) that makes the model valid.

#### **The core idea**

SysML v2 is fundamentally **relationship-driven**.

The model is not just a collection of objects with stored attributes. Instead, it is built from **explicit relationships between elements**:

- ownership
- typing
- subsetting
- redefinition
- connections

These relationships are not hidden—they are **first-class model elements**.

The Open API reflects this design. What looks like a simple property is often just a **view or shortcut over those relationships**.

#### **How to think about it**

A helpful mental model:

You are not setting values — you are **asking the API to reshape the model correctly**.

So when you call:

```java

```

you are effectively saying:

“Make this package owned by this namespace in a valid SysML v2 way.”

The API then:

- creates or updates the appropriate relationship element
- ensures consistency of the model
- updates any dependent or opposite links

Similarly:

- `getOwner()` does not read a stored field
- it resolves the owner from existing relationships

#### **Why this exists**

This approach comes directly from how the **SysML v2 / KerML standard is designed**:

- relationships are explicit and central
- model validity depends on correct structure
- many concepts are expressed through relationship elements rather than primitive fields

The Open API gives you a **simplified, safe layer** on top of that.

Without it, you would need to manually create and wire relationship elements yourself, which is error-prone and verbose.

#### **What actually happens under the hood**

When working with these properties, the API may:

- create relationship elements (e.g., memberships, feature typings)
- reattach or move existing relationships
- remove relationships when values are cleared
- update related or opposite properties automatically

So even though the code looks simple, the change is **structural, not just data-level**.

#### **Seeing the relationships in the model**

If you want to better understand what’s happening internally, you can inspect these relationship elements directly in the UI.

In the **Containment Tree**:

1. Open the tree view
2. Uncheck “Essential Elements”

You will then see additional elements such as:

- owning memberships
- feature memberships
- other relationship objects

These are the actual elements that back the “simple” properties in the API.

This is often very helpful when:

- debugging unexpected behavior
- learning how the model is structured
- working with lower-level APIs

#### **When to use them**

For most use cases, you should **prefer these properties** because they:

- make code shorter and more readable
- ensure model correctness automatically
- hide low-level complexity

Just keep in mind that they are:

- not simple fields
- but high-level modeling operations

#### **Summary**

Relationship-backed properties are a convenience layer in the SysML v2 Open API. They let you work with the model in a natural way, while the API manages the underlying relationship elements required by the SysML v2 standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In SysML v2 Open API, not every property behaves like a simple field you set and forget. Some properties are <strong>backed by model relationships</strong> and act as convenient shortcuts for making structurally correct changes to the model.</p><p>A good example is <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">Element.setOwner()</a></code>. It may look like you’re just assigning a parent, but in reality the API creates or updates the proper ownership relationship (for example, an <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html">OwningMembership</a></code>) that makes the model valid.</p><h2><strong>The core idea</strong></h2><p>SysML v2 is fundamentally <strong>relationship-driven</strong>.</p><p>The model is not just a collection of objects with stored attributes. Instead, it is built from <strong>explicit relationships between elements</strong>:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2d0e2616-69e6-42e0-91e4-2e2f706118b9">ownership</li><li data-uuid="6f71d5ab-890f-4fe0-adb1-a618bc277bb1">typing</li><li data-uuid="d9b27df5-2479-42f9-94db-9da6b726d2b5">subsetting</li><li data-uuid="702005c3-03f9-4f27-b4ff-b54fab05533f">redefinition</li><li data-uuid="165264e3-b1f7-4229-80d7-bfc8fda0e3df">connections</li></ul><p>These relationships are not hidden—they are <strong>first-class model elements</strong>.</p><p>The Open API reflects this design. ## PAGE 00204: (2026x Refresh1) Reshaping shape elements

- page_id: `303989336`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989336/2026x+Refresh1+Reshaping+shape+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:31.246+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change bounds for the existing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement*, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(**ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)*:

```java

```

If shape has the *Autosize*****property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change bounds for the existing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em>, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(</em><em>ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3986e906-8a93-4bdf-a29d-21b2689561be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Rectangle newBounds = new Rectangle(100,100,80,50);
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().reshapeShapeElement(element, newBounds);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>Every shape element has a preferred size. The shape size cannot be smaller than the preferred size. If you will try to set smaller bounds, these bounds will be increased to the preferred size.</div><p>If shape has the <em>Autosize<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </strong></em>property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20c1f6d-3f0d-462a-adf0-f8b0691eec4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989614 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00205: (2026x Refresh1) Resource dependent plugin

- page_id: `303989614`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989614/2026x+Refresh1+Resource+dependent+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:45.533+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins
- labels: ['is-plugin-required', 'get-plugin-name', 'get-plugin-version']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. 
To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin* interface.

*ResourceDependentPlugin* has three special methods:

- a isPluginRequired [*(*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*Project)* method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return true, if a given project uses resources from the plugin.
- a *getPluginName()* method should return a plugin name.
- a *getPluginVersion()*** method should return a version of the plugin.

The following example illustrates an implementation of *ResourceDependentPlugin*:

```java

```

This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project "*my_profile_filename*". The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin</em> interface.</p><p><em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em> has three special methods:</p><ul><li>a <em>isPluginRequired</em><em><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-"><em>(</em></a><em>Project)</em></em> method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">true,</em> if a given project uses resources from the plugin.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginName()</em></span> method should return a plugin name. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginVersion()</em><em> </em></span>method should return a version of the plugin. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following example illustrates an implementation of <em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f908de-266e-4022-a6a8-91416580f831"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package myplugin;
import ...
public class MyPlugin extends Plugin implements ResourceDependentPlugin
{
   @Override
   public void init()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean close()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean isSupported()
   {...}
 
   @Override
   public boolean isPluginRequired(Project project)
   {
      return ProjectUtilities.findAttachedProjectByName(project, "my_profile_filename") != null;
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginName()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getName();
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginVersion()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getVersion();
   }
}


]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project &quot;<em>my_profile_filename</em>&quot;. The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989585 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00206: (2026x Refresh1) Resource manager descriptor file

- page_id: `303989585`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989585/2026x+Refresh1+Resource+manager+descriptor+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:43.554+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file.

For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file naming

The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is*MDR_<type>_<plugin name>_<plugin id>_descriptor.xml.*For example, *MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm**l*.

All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file location

The file location is*<modeling tool installation directory>\data\resourcemanager*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample

This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.

You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “*type*“ value.

```xml
Enterprise
    Architect
    Standard
    Professional Java
    Professional C++
    Professional C#
    Professional ArcStyler
    Reader
    OptimalJ
 
    
        
        
        
        
        
        
        

    
]]>
```

The following table describes the terms used in the sample:

| Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | A unique plugin id. id is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at http://www.nomagic.com/support.html. |
| name | A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources. |
| product | A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type. |
| type | A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template. |
| version resource | This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a version internal value multiplied by 10. version resource should be a number. |
| version internal | This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. version internal should be a number. |
| version human | A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The version human number may consist of numbers and/or words. |
| edition | Supported program editions. |
| installation | installation includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.Do not use "*.*" ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if "samples/*.*" is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. |
| critical | If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e6be16-6307-406c-919c-b44b0ffc07f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file naming</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_&lt;type&gt;_&lt;plugin name&gt;_&lt;plugin id&gt;_descriptor.xml.  </em>For example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">l</em>.</p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f1661a8-55a6-42e9-9e03-aab847223bd8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>  All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file location</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The file location is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\data\resourcemanager</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">.</span></p><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.</span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “</span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">type</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">“ value.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dc3b1dc-2ac2-4410-93a8-813a41720afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<resourceDescriptor 
    critical="false" 
    date="2007-12-24+02:00" 
    description="My Plug-in"
    homePage="http://www.nomagic.com" 
    id="3001"
    mdVersionMax="higher" 
    mdVersionMin="15.0" 
    name="CustomPlugin" 
    product="CustomPlugin" 
    type="Plugin">
 
    <version human="1.0 beta" internal="1" resource="10"/>
    <provider name="No Magic" />
 
    <edition>Enterprise</edition>
    <edition>Architect</edition>
    <edition>Standard</edition>
    <edition>Professional Java</edition>
    <edition>Professional C++</edition>
    <edition>Professional C#</edition>
    <edition>Professional ArcStyler</edition>
    <edition>Reader</edition>
    <edition>OptimalJ</edition>
 
    <installation>
        <file from="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" to="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" />
        <file from="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" to="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" />
        <file from="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" to="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" />
        <file from="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" to="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" />
        <file from="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" to="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" />
        <file from="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" to="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" />
        <file from="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" to="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" />

    </installation>
</resourceDescriptor>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following table describes the terms used in the sample:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>id</strong></p></td><td><p>A unique plugin id. <em>id </em>is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at <a href="http://www.nomagic.com/support.html">http://www.nomagic.com/support.html</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>name</strong></p></td><td><p>A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>product</strong></p></td><td><p>A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>type</strong></p></td><td><p>A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version resource</strong></p></td><td><p>This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a <em>version internal </em>value multiplied by 10. <em>version resource </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version internal</strong></p></td><td><p>This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. <em>version internal </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version human</strong></p></td><td><p>A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">version human </em>number may consist of numbers and/or words.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>edition</strong></p></td><td><p>Supported program editions.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>installation</strong></p></td><td><p><em>installation </em>includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="227c4851-c138-46de-84f3-55f8f05f6997"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not use &quot;*.*&quot; ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if &quot;samples/*.*&quot; is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>critical</strong></td><td colspan="1">If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00207: (2026x Refresh1) REST API Change Log

- page_id: `303989502`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989502/2026x+Refresh1+REST+API+Change+Log
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Changed data types

###### arrayOfUuid

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/workspaces
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/admin/usergroups
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### arrayOfString

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### osmc/admin/config/put

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/config/{key}

###### osmc/admin/roles/roleID/userGroups/post

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups

###### osmc/admin/license/apply/put

Parameter “port” removed.

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/license/apply

##### Changed endpoints

###### /osmc/admin/license/query/{licenseServer}

###### GET

Query parameters added:

| Name | Type | Remark |
| --- | --- | --- |
| licenseFramework | String | The licensing framework (DSLS or FlexNet) |
| licensingMode | String | The licensing mode (ORGANIZATION_DEFINED or CUSTOM, for DSLS only) |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989462 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00208: (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs

- page_id: `303989462`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989462/2026x+Refresh1+REST+APIs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.350+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud provides a set of REST APIs for server management. REST APIs are essential properties in Teamwork Cloud that allow you to query data about user accounts, roles, projects, and that provide access and basic operations for the resources. REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.

The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs inboth machine-readable and human-readable formats. Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at [https://osmc.nomagic.com](https://osmc.nomagic.com/). The API document is also bundled with Teamwork Cloud installation. You can access it from https://<Teamwork Cloud IP>:8111/osmc/swagger.

Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. It allows logging in and calling the server from a web browser. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.</p><p>The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs in<span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> both machine-readable and human-readable formats.</span> Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/">https://osmc.nomagic.com</a>. You can access it from <span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);">https://&lt;Teamwork Cloud IP&gt;:8111/osmc/swagger</span>. </p><p>Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="808393af-a8e7-4889-a3c1-4d4c7eeabe89" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989155 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00210: (2026x Refresh1) Retrieving meta information about element

- page_id: `303989155`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989155/2026x+Refresh1+Retrieving+meta+information+about+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:23.683+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every Element is identified by it's "metaclass". Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class*).

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()* to retrieve the element metaclass.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes* for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every Element is identified by it's &quot;metaclass&quot;. Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em>). </p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()</em> to retrieve the element metaclass.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes</em> for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989493 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00211: (2026x Refresh1) Reusing Teamwork Cloud session

- page_id: `303989493`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989493/2026x+Refresh1+Reusing+Teamwork+Cloud+session
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.032+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is **very** important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.

The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.

The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.

```text
users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]>
```

The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the **cookie.txt** file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.

The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is <strong>very</strong> important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.</p><p>The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.</p><p>The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="154a6874-c10f-4833-bd14-899a0068625c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
# Script to export a unique list of email address for all users in Teamwork Cloud into a singe CSV list (email_list.csv)
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
#
# 
SERVER=127.0.0.1
RESTBASEURL=https://$SERVER:8111/osmc
USERURL=${RESTBASEURL}/admin/users
LOGINURL=${RESTBASEURL}/login
LOGOUTURL=${RESTBASEURL}/logout
username=Administrator
password=Administrator
rm -f email*.*
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt --insecure -u $username:$password $LOGINURL
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL" -H "accept: application/json" > users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the <strong>cookie.txt</strong> file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.</p><p>The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcf44a99-0ec2-4a02-8871-c1632d46fd92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Script to import users from CSV file into Teamwork Cloud
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
# Format: non-quoted CSV, no headers
#  userLogin,userPassword,fullUserName,departmentName,emailAddress
#
# Usage:
# PowerShell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ImportUsersFromCSV.ps1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
Add-Type @"
  using System.Net;
  using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;
  public class TrustAllCertificatesPolicy : ICertificatePolicy 
  {
     public bool CheckValidationResult
     (
       ServicePoint srvPoint, X509Certificate certificate
       ,
       WebRequest request, int certificateProblem
     ) 
     {
       return true;
     }
  }
"@
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CheckCertificateRevocationList = $false;
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback += { $true; };
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CertificatePolicy = New-Object TrustAllCertificatesPolicy
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12
$Server = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Name or IP address'
$Admin = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Administrator User'
$Passwd = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Administrator Password'
$Userfile = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the full path name of the file containing the users'
$RESTBASEURL = "https://"+$Server+":8111/osmc/"
$ADDUSERURL = $RESTBASEURL + "admin/users"
$LOGINURL = $RESTBASEURL   + "login"
$LOGOUTURL = $RESTBASEURL + "logout"
$pair="$($Admin):$($Passwd)"
$bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($pair)
$base64 = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes)
$basicAuthValue = "Basic $base64"
$headers = @{ Authorization = $basicAuthValue }
$RestError = $null
Try {
$response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$LOGINURL" -Method Post -Headers $headers -SessionVariable 'Session'
} Catch {
	$RestError = $_
}
foreach($line in [System.IO.File]::ReadLines($Userfile))
{
                  $login,$password,$username,$department,$email = $line.split(',').trim()
                  
                  $JSON=@"
                  {
                                             "userName": "$login",
                                             "password": "$password",
                                             "otherAttributes": {
                                                            "mobile": "",
                                                            "name": "$username",
                                                            "department": "$department",
                                                            "email": "$email"
                                             },
                                             "enabled": true
                              }
"@
                              $response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$ADDUSERURL" -Method Post -Body $JSON -ContentType "application/json"  -WebSession $Session
}
 
$response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$LOGOUTURL" –Method Get -WebSession $Session]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988956 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00212: (2026x Refresh1) Running action with progress

- page_id: `303988956`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988956/2026x+Refresh1+Running+action+with+progress
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:10.085+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.

There are two options to execute these actions:

- a foreground task - use com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress or com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner
- a background task - use com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\progressstatus*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Multi-threading' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.</p><p>There are two options to execute these actions:</p><ul><li>a foreground task - use <em>com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner</em></li><li>a background task - use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation direc</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">tory&gt;\openapi\examples\progressstatus</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74b88a6d-75d0-4f9c-9077-d7b780313a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Multi-threading" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989123 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00213: (2026x Refresh1) Running programs in batch mode

- page_id: `303989123`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989123/2026x+Refresh1+Running+programs+in+batch+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:20.714+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are cases when custom task must be executed in the batch (command line) mode. For example, if you want to run MagicDraw or other modeling tool application, open some project, execute code generation and exit application.

API provides two ways to run the tool in the batch mode depending on the required functionality.

1. Functionality is provided by an application core. You need to extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine class.
2. Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction interface.

****You can find the code examples in

1. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\*imagegenerator* . It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.
2. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin . Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.

The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. The graphical environment is required.

For more information on the launching batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

For more information on creation of batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Implementing command line launchers' space=''].

More information is available in javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You need to extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em> class.</li><li>Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em> interface.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ol><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\<em>imagegenerator</em></em>. <ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5478fe15-51af-4122-aaa4-1a0111f53781" /></h3><h3>Performing the forward engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code generation.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c8fe96a-5d37-4773-afd7-e09335dc20e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet javaGenerationSet = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #2. Adding model elements to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="142f730a-857e-439e-91e1-e2d9f727c166"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();
 
 	// create a new element
 	ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
 	Class classA = ef.createClassInstance();
 	classA.setName("ClassA");
	classA.setOwner(project.getModel());
	List<BaseElement> modelsForSample = new ArrayList<BaseElement>();
	modelsForSample.add(classA);
	javaGenerationSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(modelsForSample);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Step #3. Performing the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> generation</strong></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a7d6f5-8cf4-4795-960a-8fc7e1d2d6d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    CodeEngineeringManager.generate(javaGenerationSet);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Performing the reverse engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code reverse.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9933ea61-dd6a-4c46-b658-9eef350f9f35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;// e.g C:\myworkingPackage
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet ces = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Here the null dialect is used for the java language, because java doesn't have any dialect.</p><p><strong>Step #2. Adding the source code to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eccd7ee9-f67e-4c1a-95af-e288323986bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	ces.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(new File(workingDir + File.separator + "test directory")); // starting from C:\myworkingPackage\test directory\]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This sets the given instance of the code engineering set working directory and adds all files from that directory. Set java classpaths for the project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6ba7648-854c-4ea6-a859-f54175d7b845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    String[] claspath = new String[] { path1, path2, path3, path4 }; 
    JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(project, claspath);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #3. Performing the reverse of <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d41202c-94aa-42f4-bf2e-6a98fb396354"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(ces, false);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989338 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: (2026x Refresh1) Script validation rule

- page_id: `303989338`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989338/2026x+Refresh1+Script+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:31.394+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to compile and store them in a classpath. Oftentimes, the code is compact and simple.

###### How to create a a script validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Constraints' space='MT'].
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).
6. Enter the validation code in the selected language.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
