Check their APIs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989360 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00227: (2026x Refresh1) Starting execution

- page_id: `303989360`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989360/2026x+Refresh1+Starting+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:32.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs

- SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession
- SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession

Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows

- The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.
- If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the "isMainSession" flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.
- If the "start" flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12a1efe7-0e1f-4d71-b787-b371ebf298cc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ee8cf1d-fc1b-45b8-96f9-007dadbadfc4">SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21e3b246-3329-4c35-ad35-45db8faa59ca"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9dd29a01-11b3-4d69-a624-de481b5d9573">SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows</p><ul><li>The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="469dc2f0-9737-41d3-a2a0-95d515839ccc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e768531-ccd4-45a4-a498-fc8a90ebe099">If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the &quot;isMainSession&quot; flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>If the &quot;start&quot; flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989429 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: (2026x Refresh1) Starting MagicDraw or other modeling tool as part of another application

- page_id: `303989429`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989429/2026x+Refresh1+Starting+MagicDraw+or+other+modeling+tool+as+part+of+another+application
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.680+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.

In order to do that you must:

- Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath
- Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate start(...) method. (see Javadoc)
- Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM)

For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.</p><p>In order to do that you must:</p><ul><li>Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath</li><li>Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate <em>start(...)</em> method. (see Javadoc)</li><li>Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM) </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Running programs in batch mode" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989372 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00229: (2026x Refresh1) Stopping execution

- page_id: `303989372`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989372/2026x+Refresh1+Stopping+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:33.876+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API

- SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void

Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void</li></ul><p>Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989563 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00230: (2026x Refresh1) Subsettings Utility Class

- page_id: `303989563`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989563/2026x+Refresh1+Subsettings+Utility+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:42.252+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In KerML, *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Subsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html)* expresses that the value set of one feature — the subsetting feature — is a subset of another — the subsetted feature. Every value of the subsetting feature is therefore also a value of the subsetted feature.

*[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html)*is the utility class for programmatic subsetting operations.

#### **Getting Subsettings**

[*getAnyKindSubsetting*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#getAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))returns the features, in the context of a given type, that subset a specified feature. The *AnyKind* variants also take [Redefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html) into account.

```java
subsettors = Subsettings.getAnyKindSubsetting(vehicleParts, electricVehicle);
// → [EV::engines, EV::batteryPacks] if both subset parts in EV's context]]>
```

[isAnyKindSubsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#isAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))checks whether one feature subsets another, directly or through redefinition.

```java

```

#### **Setting Subsetted**

[*setAnyKindSubsetted*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))replaces all existing owned subsetting relationships on a feature with a new one pointing to the specified subsetted feature. Passing *null* removes all explicit subsettings.

```java

```

[*addSubsetted*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))appends a new plain [Subsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html)without removing existing ones.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In KerML, <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Subsetting</a></em> expresses that the value set of one feature — the subsetting feature — is a subset of another — the subsetted feature. Every value of the subsetting feature is therefore also a value of the subsetted feature.</p><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings</a> </em>is the utility class for programmatic subsetting operations.</p><h2><strong>Getting Subsettings</strong></h2><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#getAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)"><em>getAnyKindSubsetting</em> </a>returns the features, in the context of a given type, that subset a specified feature. The <em>AnyKind</em> variants also take <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html">Redefinition</a> into account.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee1eb410-f496-4883-962f-68343cd26786"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature;

// Which features in ElectricVehicle subset Vehicle::parts?
List<Feature> subsettors = Subsettings.getAnyKindSubsetting(vehicleParts, electricVehicle);
// → [EV::engines, EV::batteryPacks] if both subset parts in EV's context]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#isAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">isAnyKindSubsetting </a>checks whether one feature subsets another, directly or through redefinition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71bd710c-bbe4-49c3-81d9-7f7fd8db32bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// Check if a feature subsets another one.
boolean subsets = Subsettings.isAnyKindSubsetting(subsettingFeature, subsettedFeature);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Setting Subsetted</strong></h2><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>setAnyKindSubsetted</em> </a>replaces all existing owned subsetting relationships on a feature with a new one pointing to the specified subsetted feature. Passing <em>null</em> removes all explicit subsettings.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f152b96-c4d7-40aa-a395-6e8bfbf0af9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// Vehicle::engines subsets Vehicle::parts
Subsettings.setAnyKindSubsetted(engines, parts);

// Remove all explicit subsettings
Subsettings.setAnyKindSubsetted(engines, null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>addSubsetted</em> </a>appends a new plain <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html">Subsetting </a>without removing existing ones.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d531ba21-f9be-4f17-ac6b-6e0b060bc9ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// engines already subsets parts — add another subsetting
Subsettings.addSubsetted(engines, motorisedComponents);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989113 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00231: (2026x Refresh1) Supported Changes from UML 2.4.1 to UML 2.5

- page_id: `303989113`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989113/2026x+Refresh1+Supported+Changes+from+UML+2.4.1+to+UML+2.5
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:19.759+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33

##### Metamodel Changes

All the changes are grouped by the categories:

44

###### New property names added

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Classifier::conveyingFlow | - |
| DeployedArtifact::deploymentForArtifact |  |
| PackageableElement::import |  |

###### Properties renamed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| NamedElement::classifierinheritingClassifier | - |
| Classifier::classnestingClass |  |
| ParameterableElement::parameterSubstitutionowningTemplateParameterSubstitution |  |

###### Derivation changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/classifier : Classifier [0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Feature::featuringClassifier, subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | All these properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. |
| InputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Action::/input : InputPin[*]{ordered, readOnly, union, subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| OutputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| NamedElement::/namespace : Namespace[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| DirectedRelationship::/source : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | Both properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| DirectedRelationship::/target : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} |  |
| NamedElement::/clientDependency | The Client Dependency property of a named element has been made automatically derived. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Order changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| AcceptEventAction::result : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The ability to order output pins holding the values received from an event occurrence, has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| Association::/endType : Type [1..*]{ordered, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | The set of classifiers that are used as end types of the association, is no longer ordered. |
| Classifier::/attribute : Property [0..*]{ordered, union, subsets Classifier::feature} | The ability to order the attributes, which are direct properties of a classifier has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw.Though the Attribute property cannot be edited, its value displays the same order of the attributes as is defined in other places of the classifier specification, such as the Owned Attribute property or the Attributes property group. Thus to change the Attribute property value, change the value of a relevant property. |
| ConnectableElement::/end : ConnectorEnd [0..*]{ordered} | A set of connector ends that attach to the particular connectable element, is no longer ordered. |
| DurationObservation::event : NamedElement [1..2]{ordered} | Event elements are now automatically ordered. |
| ReplyAction::♦ replyValue : InputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::input} | The set of input pins can now be ordered. In the Reply Value property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |
| UnmarshallAction::♦result : OutputPin [1..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The set of output pins can now be ordered. In the Result property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |

###### Other changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| LoopNode::♦loopVariable : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Element::ownedElement} | The Loop Variable property has been made composite. |
| OutputPin::loopNode : LoopNode[0..1]{subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Activity::structuredNode : StructuredActivityNode [0..*]{readOnly, subsets Activity::node, Activity::group} | The property has been made read-only in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Properties removed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/default : String[0..1] | The Default property no longer appears in a property specification. The value of the Default Value property will be used instead of the removed property value. |

##### Notation Changes

From now on, the inherited members are denoted with the caret "^" sign.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Profile changes

Standard/system profiles *StandardProfileL2* and *StandardProfileL3* have been merged into a single one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /><h3 class="bold">Metamodel Changes</h3><p>All the changes are grouped by the categories:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" In the <strong>Reply Value</strong> property value cell, click the <ac:image ac:alt="order list values" ac:height="11" ac:width="11"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.nomagic.com/images/noteworthy/180/order_list_values.png" /></ac:image> button to open the dialog for reordering.</td></tr><tr><td>UnmarshallAction::♦result : OutputPin [1..*]{<span>ordered</span>, subsets Action::output}</td><td>The set of output pins can now be ordered. In the <strong>Result</strong> property value cell, click the <ac:image ac:alt="order list values" ac:height="11" ac:width="11"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.nomagic.com/images/noteworthy/180/order_list_values.png" /></ac:image> button to open the dialog for reordering.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="bold">Other changes</h4><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><strong>Property</strong></td><td><strong>Description / Comment</strong></td></tr><tr><td>LoopNode::<span>♦</span>loopVariable : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, <span>subsets Element::ownedElement</span>}</td><td rowspan="2">The <strong>Loop Variable</strong> property has been made composite.</td></tr><tr><td>OutputPin::loopNode : LoopNode[0..1]{<span>subsets Element::owner</span>}</td></tr><tr><td>Activity::structuredNode : StructuredActivityNode [0..*]{<span>readOnly</span>, subsets Activity::node, Activity::group}</td><td>The property has been made read-only in earlier versions of MagicDraw.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="bold">Properties removed</h4><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><strong>Property</strong></td><td><strong>Description / Comment</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Property::/default : String[0..1]</td><td>The <strong>Default</strong> property no longer appears in a property specification. The value of the <strong>Default Value</strong> property will be used instead of the removed property value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><h3 class="heading">Notation Changes</h3><p>From now on, the inherited members are denoted with the caret &quot;^&quot; sign.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="inheritance sign" ac:height="351" ac:width="432"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.nomagic.com/images/noteworthy/180/md/inheritance_sign.png" /></ac:image></p><h3 class="heading">Profile changes</h3><p>Standard/system profiles <em>StandardProfileL2</em> and <em>StandardProfileL3</em> have been merged into a single one.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="profile merged" ac:height="216" ac:width="661"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.nomagic.com/images/noteworthy/180/md/profile_merged.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989129 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00232: (2026x Refresh1) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1

- page_id: `303989129`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989129/2026x+Refresh1+Supported+UML+Specification+Changes+from+Version+2.3+to+2.4.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:21.316+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Supported UML specification changes include:

44

###### Metamodel Changes

| Metamodel Change | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| DATA TYPES ADDED |  |
| Real | Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models. |
| METACLASSES ADDED |  |
| LiteralReal | Specifies the real value. |
| METACLASSES CHANGED |  |
| DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification | The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification. |
| METACLASSES REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionEvent | These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types. |
| CreationEvent |  |
| ReceiveOperationEvent |  |
| ReceiveSignalEvent |  |
| SendOperationEvent |  |
| SendSignalEvent |  |
| PROPERTIES ADDED |  |
| Package::URI:String[0..1] {id} | Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see . |
| Property::isID:Boolean=False | Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see . |
| InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1] | Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction. |
| InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1] | Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction. |
| EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1] | The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier. |
| ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1] | The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1 |
| OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1] | The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more. |
| BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*] | Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more. |
| SUBSETTING CHANGES |  |
| Interface::redefinedInterface {subsets redefinedElement → redefinedClassifier} | An interface now references all the interfaces that are redefined by this interface. |
| SUBSETTING ADDEDNote: These features were already available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |  |
| Duration::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} | Element or elements can be selected or created only under the owned element’s scope. |
| LinkEndData::qualifier:QualifierValue[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| LinkAction::endData:LinkEndData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| CreateLinkAction::endData:LinkEndCreationData[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| DestroyLinkAction::endData:LinkEndDestructionData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeExpression::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValuePin::value:ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| State::deferrableTrigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::edge:ActivityEdge[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValueSpecificationAction::value: ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| AcceptEventAction::trigger:Triger[1..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Stereotype::icon:Image[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeEvent::when:TimeExpression[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Classifier::/feature {subsets Namespace::member} | The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property. |
| Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets NamedElement::memberNamespace} | The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property. |
| Property::owningAssociation {subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | A property now references the owning association of this property. |
| DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES |  |
| DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True | The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty. |
| DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True |  |
| PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public | The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public. |
| MULTIPLICITY CHANGES |  |
| EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1] | The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory. |
| Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2] | ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior. |
| DERIVATION CHANGES |  |
| Constraint::/context → context | The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property. |
| Message::/signature → signature | The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only. |

###### Notation changes

1. If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989200 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00233: (2026x Refresh1) SysML classes for open API

- page_id: `303989200`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989200/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+classes+for+open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.910+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in *<SysML plugin installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

The*com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities* class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.

Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in <em>&lt;SysML plugin installation directory&gt;\openapi\docs</em>.</p><p>The<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities</em> class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.</p><p>Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989594 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00234: (2026x Refresh1) SysML ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `303989594`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989594/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:44.242+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Model Traversal and Visitor Pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor interface extends KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor and SysMLModelVisitor to provide a enhanced implementation of the visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.

###### Key Features

- Hierarchical Traversal : The visitor pattern allows for hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.

SysMLVisitorContext is the standard context object used during traversal. It provides: boolean isAlreadyVisited(int classID); void addToVisited(int classId);. Each model element type provides its own integer identifier. Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation. 
 
For example, you can put some code into visitActionUsage(actionUsage, sysMLVisitorContext) and it will be executed for all subclasses of ActionUsage (AcceptActionUsage, SendActionUsage etc)

```java
{

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]>
```

##### Using the Custom Visitor

Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor interface extends KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor and SysMLModelVisitor to provide a enhanced implementation of the visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.</p><h5>Key Features</h5><ul><li data-uuid="82e41c48-0e43-4af9-bb02-f2d7f3100195"><strong>Hierarchical Traversal</strong>: The visitor pattern allows for hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>SysMLVisitorContext is the standard context object used during traversal. It provides: boolean isAlreadyVisited(int classID); void addToVisited(int classId);. Each model element type provides its own integer identifier. Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.<br /><br />For example, you can put some code into visitActionUsage(actionUsage, sysMLVisitorContext)  and it will be executed for all subclasses of ActionUsage (AcceptActionUsage, SendActionUsage etc)</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="962eff3e-9b60-4917-b4f5-d0592084f833"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLVisitorContext;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.*;

public class CustomSysMLVisitor implements SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor<SysMLVisitorContext> {

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Using the Custom Visitor</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:<br /> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c89bf51-17da-4b91-acbd-f15dd1f23640"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;

public class Main
{
	public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception
	{
		MyCustomVisitor myVisitor = new CustomSysMLVisitor();
		Package root = ..;
		for (Element element : root.getOwnedElement())
		{
			element.accept(myVisitor);
		}
		System.out.println("Traversal complete.");
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989596 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00235: (2026x Refresh1) SysML ModelVisitor

- page_id: `303989596`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989596/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+ModelVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:44.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Model Traversal and Visitor Pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysMLModelVisitor interface extends KerMLModelVisitor to provide a visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner. Every com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element has the accept(AbstractVisitor) method for visiting it. 
 
Key Features :

- Visitor Pattern: The visitor pattern allows for the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.
- Context Management: The visitor context (SysMLVisitorContext) is used to manage the state during traversal, including tracking visited elements and other relevant information.

The SysMLVisitorContext class is used to manage the state during traversal of SysML model elements. It provides methods to track visited elements and other relevant information.

Key Features :

- Tracking Visited Elements: The SysMLVisitorContext class provides methods to track visited elements during traversal.
- Context Management: The class is used to manage the state during traversal, including tracking visited elements and other relevant information. java{

 @Override
 public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
 System.out.println("Visiting AcceptActionUsage: " + element.getName());
 }

 @Override
 public void visitActionDefinition(ActionDefinition element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
 System.out.println("Visiting ActionDefinition: " + element.getName());
 }

 @Override
 public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
 System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
 }

 // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]>

##### Using the Custom Visitor

Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:

```java

```

````html
<p>The SysMLModelVisitor interface extends KerMLModelVisitor to provide a visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner. Every com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element has the accept(AbstractVisitor) method for visiting it.<br /><br />Key Features :</p><ul><li data-uuid="882aef00-c5dd-4fbf-8c84-e7c008bae2b6">Visitor Pattern: The visitor pattern allows for the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.</li><li data-uuid="cc9b7b71-522d-4b1f-9187-3fb0bc89be70">Context Management: The visitor context (SysMLVisitorContext) is used to manage the state during traversal, including tracking visited elements and other relevant information.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The SysMLVisitorContext class is used to manage the state during traversal of SysML model elements. It provides methods to track visited elements and other relevant information.</p><p>Key Features :</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2ac1c85-dca4-47d6-9899-45013493ba50">Tracking Visited Elements: The SysMLVisitorContext class provides methods to track visited elements during traversal.</li><li data-uuid="ce0190e5-fbf0-4060-9b03-f34063cfc8a6">Context Management: The class is used to manage the state during traversal, including tracking visited elements and other relevant information.<br /><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ae17603-9d30-40e1-bee6-954d6026bb50"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLModelVisitor;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLVisitorContext;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.*;

public class CustomSysMLVisitor implements SysMLModelVisitor<SysMLVisitorContext> {

    @Override
    public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
        System.out.println("Visiting AcceptActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitActionDefinition(ActionDefinition element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionDefinition: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3>Using the Custom Visitor</h3><p>Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24a62592-4cba-4ef5-965c-40a360e6c01c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;

public class Main
{
	public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception
	{
		MyCustomVisitor myVisitor = new CustomSysMLVisitor();
		Package root = ..;
		for (Element element : root.getOwnedElement())
		{
			element.accept(myVisitor);
		}
		System.out.println("Traversal complete.");
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989184 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00236: (2026x Refresh1) SysML Profile

- page_id: `303989184`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989184/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.575+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to use this API class.

****

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*STEREOTYPE_NAME*”.

For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.*ID*returns a string of “Id”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.

For example, SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) where "Elem" is the element you want to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) returns *True* if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns *False* otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.</span><em style="text-align: left;">STEREOTYPE_NAME</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">”.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.</span><em>ID</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>returns a string of “Id”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes:</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes  SysMLProfile.Stereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) where &quot;Elem&quot; is the element you want to check.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) returns <em>True</em> if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns <em>False</em> otherwise.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989174 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00237: (2026x Refresh1) SysML Profile API Changes

- page_id: `303989174`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989174/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+Profile+API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.395+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.

The following constants were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = "ConstraintProperty";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = "QuantityKind";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = "definitionURI";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = "description";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = "symbol";

public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = "Unit";

The following methods were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

getConstraintProperty()

getQuantityKind()

getUnit()

isQuantityKind()

isUnit()

isConstraintProperty()

The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.</p><p>The following constants were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = &quot;ConstraintProperty&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = &quot;QuantityKind&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = &quot;definitionURI&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = &quot;description&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = &quot;symbol&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = &quot;Unit&quot;;</p><p>The following methods were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getConstraintProperty()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isConstraintProperty()</p><p>The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989017 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00238: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1

- page_id: `303989017`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989017/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:12.948+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: ['open-api']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard stereotypes in SysML plugin are defined in SysML Profile and MD Customization for SysML Profile. Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html)*and *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html)*, respectively. Each class allows you to:

- Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).
- Get a stereotype element.
- Check if an element is stereotyped.

Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip*

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip*

Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in <modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>**_no_install\openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip*

Extract a .jar file and double-click the*index.html*to open it.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html"> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile </a></em>and <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html">com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</a></em>, respectively. Each class allows you to:</p><ul><li>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).</li><li>Get a stereotype element.</li><li>Check if an element is stereotyped.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip</em></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a03c3705-33fb-422d-a0ec-c8eced569dd6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, <em>&lt;modeling_tool_version_number&gt;_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1134b5c-9d74-4041-ac03-9813137509ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, <em>&lt;modeling_tool_version_number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip</span></em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Extract a .jar file and double-click the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index.html</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> to open it.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Related pages</span></strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="efbbc27d-b595-4074-9030-22a314abc9ed"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide" /></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989216 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00239: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2

- page_id: `303989216`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989216/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.369+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysML v2 implementation is based on the OMG KerML 1.0 and SysML 2.0 metamodels. This documentation does not provide a detailed description of all KerML/SysML metamodel elements and their properties. Instead, the implementation fully aligns with the OMG specifications, which can be found on the Object Management Group website:

- https://www.omg.org/spec/KerML/1.0/About-KerML
- https://www.omg.org/sysml/sysmlv2/

Users are expected to work with interfaces from the following packages:

- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/package-summary.html)
- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/package-summary.html)

Implementation classes (e.g., *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.impl.ElementImpl](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/validation/ElementValidationRuleImpl.html)*) must not be used directly in user code.

The root interface for all model elements is *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)*. 
It extends both:

- [com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html)
- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.model.ModelElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

All model structures are derived from *[Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)* and follow the definitions provided in the OMG KerML and SysML specifications.

All attributes and references defined in the SysML specification are implemented and accessible via standard getter and setter methods.

Typical interaction with the model includes:

- Navigating elements via containment and references
- Accessing properties using getters
- Modifying values using setters within a controlled context

The model has the following concurrency characteristics:

- Read operations are thread-safe
- Write operations are not thread-safe

All write operations must be externally synchronized or executed within the appropriate transaction or session mechanism.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989571 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00240: (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `303989571`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989571/2026x+Refresh1+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:42.756+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working with SysML v2 Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The*[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html)* class class provides utility methods for creating and opening SysML v2 projects in Teamwork Cloud.

#### Project Creation

Creates a new SysML v2 project in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Example

```text

```

##### Notes

- Project is created locally in UPS before being uploaded to Teamwork Cloud
- Project name must be unique in Teamwork Cloud and in UPS
- Throws an exception if the upload fails

#### Project Opening

Opens an existing Teamwork Cloud project using its qualified name.

##### Example

```text

```

##### Notes

- Project must exist in Teamwork Cloud
- Qualified name must match exactly
- Throws an exception if loading fails

#### Summary

| Operation | Method |
| --- | --- |
| Create project | createTWCProject(projectName) |
| Open project | loadTWCProject(projectName) |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;"><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper</a></em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">class <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">class provides utility methods for creating and opening</span></span> SysML v2 projects in Teamwork Cloud.</p><h2>Project Creation</h2><p>Creates a new SysML v2 project in Teamwork Cloud.</p><h3>Example</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53376c73-15a9-4e91-8d76-b015fe316a71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = SysMLProjectHelper.createTWCProject("MyProject"); 
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> is created locally in UPS before being uploaded to Teamwork Cloud</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> name must be unique in Teamwork Cloud and in UPS</li><li>Throws an exception if the upload fails</li></ul><h2>Project Opening</h2><p>Opens an existing Teamwork Cloud project using its qualified name.</p><h3>Example</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b21765d-30e4-4dba-95c7-7362bd2df06e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;

SysMLProjectHelper.loadTWCProject("MyProject");  
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> must exist in Teamwork Cloud</li><li>Qualified name must match exactly</li><li>Throws an exception if loading fails</li></ul><h2>Summary</h2><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Operation</p></th><th><p>Method</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>Create project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#createTWCProject(java.lang.String)">createTWCProject(projectName)</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#loadTWCProject(java.lang.String)">loadTWCProject(projectName)</a></code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989608 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00242: (2026x Refresh1) Testing plugin

- page_id: `303989608`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989608/2026x+Refresh1+Testing+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:45.307+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can test if your plugin sample works by checking described messages or by checking the *magicdraw.log* file.

To check messages

1. Restart MagicDraw (or other modeling tool you are using). On startup, a message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_init.png']
2. Then exit a program. Another message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_close.png']

To check the *magicdraw.log* file

- On the Help menu, click About MagicDraw . Then, in the Environment tab, click the Log File link.
- You can find the*magicdraw.log* file in a configuration file storage:
  - For Windows operating system, it is *C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<MagicDraw version number>*
  - For other operating systems, it is *<user.home>/.magicdraw/<MagicDraw version number>*

After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the *magicdraw.log* file:

```bash

```

Also all plugins and their statuses are listed in the program **Environment****Options** dialog, in the Plugins tab.

Looking at the file is the best way to find problems when the plugin does not work.

On startup, a message should appear:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_My_Plugin_init.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Testing plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Then exit a program. Another message should appear: <br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_My_Plugin_close.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Testing plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p><p>To check the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file</p><hr /><p> </p><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">On the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Help</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> menu, click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">About MagicDraw</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. Then, in the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> tab, click the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Log File</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> link. </span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can find the<em> magicdraw.log</em> file in a configuration file storage:</span><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">For Windows operating system, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For other operating systems, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;user.home&gt;/.magicdraw/&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p>After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea4d7537-8edd-4505-8e99-32eb1b6c723f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[LOAD PLUGINS:
