[com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor@edf730[ id = my.first.plugin, name =|http://Ljava.net.URL/] My First Plugin, provider = Coder, version = 1.0, class = myplugin.MyPlugin, requires api = 1.0, runtime = [Ljava.net.URL;@ff94b1]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Also all plugins and their statuses are listed in the program <strong>Environment</strong><strong>Options</strong> dialog, in the Plugins tab.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a95de164-a7b2-4279-8ee0-86dfa7ab824f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Looking at the file is the best way to find problems when the plugin does not work. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989220 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00243: (2026x Refresh1) Textual Import/Export

- page_id: `303989220`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989220/2026x+Refresh1+Textual+Import+Export
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.456+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML v2 textual notation is a **human-readable, formal syntax** for representing SysML v2 models as text. It provides an alternative to graphical modeling by allowing modelers to define, inspect, and manipulate models using structured language constructs.

The**[SysMLTextualNotationService](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/textual/SysMLTextualNotationService.html)**provides a stateless API for converting SysML v2 models to and from their textual representations.

**1. Import: Text to Model**

Creates model elements from a textual source. Code below parses SysML v2 text and materializes it within anew namespace in the`[ModelElementProject](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/project/ModelElementProject.html)`.

Failures may happen because of syntax/parsing errors, semantic validation issues, or model conflicts within the target project.

```java

```

After successful import in the containment tree we will see the model elements:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**2. Export: Model to Text** 
Code below converts a`[Namespace](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html)`into a SysML v2-compliant string.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SysML v2 textual notation is a <strong>human-readable, formal syntax</strong> for representing SysML v2 models as text. It provides an alternative to graphical modeling by allowing modelers to define, inspect, and manipulate models using structured language constructs.</p><p>The<span> </span><strong class="Yjhzub"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/textual/SysMLTextualNotationService.html">SysMLTextualNotationService</a></strong><span> </span>provides a stateless API for converting SysML v2 models to and from their textual representations.</p><p> <strong class="Yjhzub">1. Import: Text to Model</strong></p><p>Creates model elements from a textual source. Code below p<span class="T286Pc">arses SysML v2 text and materializes it within a<span> new namespace in the </span><code class="o8j0Mc"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/project/ModelElementProject.html">ModelElementProject</a></code>. </span></p><p><span class="T286Pc">Failures may happen because of syntax/parsing errors, semantic validation issues, or model conflicts within the target project.</span><span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a29ba23a-ff2a-451f-99b1-497fa4920be1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Import</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.textual.SysMLTextualNotationService;

ModelElementProject project = ...;

String sysmlText = """
package Example {
    part def Engine {
        attribute power : ScalarValues::Real;
    }
}
""";

SysMLTextualNotationService.importTextual(project, sysmlText);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span class="Yjhzub">After successful import in the containment tree we will see the model elements:</span></p><p><span class="Yjhzub"><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-3-23_16-37-45.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual Import/Export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><br /></p><p><strong class="Yjhzub">2. Export: Model to Text</strong><br /><span class="T286Pc">Code below converts a<span> </span><code class="o8j0Mc"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html">Namespace</a></code><span> </span>into a SysML v2-compliant string.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffe0a810-e20f-4430-9ed0-9e39a5926b4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Export</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.textual.SysMLTextualNotationService;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace;

Namespace namespace = ...;
String text = SysMLTextualNotationService.exportTextual(namespace);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989500 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00244: (2026x Refresh1) Token-based authentication

- page_id: `303989500`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989500/2026x+Refresh1+Token-based+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.

##### Pre-configuring Authentication server

Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to *https://<auth_server_host>:<port>/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration*. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:

```text
:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]>
```

Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the **authserver.properties** file:

1. Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist . This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.
2. Add new client IDs separated with commas: authentication.client.ids . You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept client_id parameters that cannot be found in this list.

There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:

- When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from webappplatform.properties , parameter authentication.client.secret .
- ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property authentication.token.expirity from authserver.properties ), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.

To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request:

```text
]]>
```

##### Authentication with user interaction

The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:

1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (***authorization_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters:

```text
client_id=
response_type=code]]>
```

2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.

3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=authorization_code
code=]]>
```

4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.

5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=]]>
```

##### Authentication without user interaction

To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

- Headers: ]]>

Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password

- Query parameters: ]]>

If your client ID is added into the **authentication.client.permanent**list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter **authentication.permanent.token.expirity**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f592144-074b-424f-be69-8530c09c134b" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.</p><h3>Pre-configuring Authentication server</h3><p>Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to <em>https://&lt;auth_server_host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration</em>. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce45cafd-e140-4db1-8df6-865312107668"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "response_types_supported":["id_token","code"],
    "validation_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the <strong>authserver.properties</strong> file:</p><ol><li>Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</strong>. This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.</li><li>Add new client IDs separated with commas: <strong>authentication.client.ids</strong>. You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept<em> </em><strong>client_id </strong>parameters that cannot be found in this list.</li></ol><p>There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2485283-f574-4483-91f0-c461ad6de081">When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">webappplatform.properties</span></strong>, parameter <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="040a448f-4855-4da6-b43d-7b21c1fbb430">ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property <strong>authentication.token.expirity </strong>from <strong>authserver.properties</strong>), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.</li></ul><p>To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19b3247d-b03e-4724-b739-cb2353ae6439"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <received_id_token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Authentication with user interaction</span></h3><p>The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:</p><p>1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (<em><strong>authorization_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14ce61e5-bd41-436a-9f42-6f6b75a27d22"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
response_type=code]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.</p><p>3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f8bd4f-e472-4799-8623-8255439ecc88"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=authorization_code
code=<code_received_after_user_signs_in>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.</p><p>5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95aee7b0-dc5f-4adf-88ca-f4f957778264"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=<refresh_token_value>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Authentication without user interaction</h3><p>To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Headers: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb2c4bba-b35c-44aa-a2d2-5463ca3ba029"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[X-Auth-Secret: <secret from authentication.client.secret >]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><span>Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password</span></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Query parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66a619-e9f7-47f4-b0d2-073a8a58f310"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[grant_type=client_credentials
client_id=<your_client_id>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p>If your client ID is added into the <strong>authentication.client.permanent </strong>list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter <strong>authentication.permanent.token.expirity</strong>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989506 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00245: (2026x Refresh1) Token-based authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API

- page_id: `303989506`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989506/2026x+Refresh1+Token-based+authentication+sample+for+Teamwork+Cloud+REST+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:48:49.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Teamwork Cloud REST API has an endpoint, which implements token-based authentication described in the [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/(2026x Refresh1) Token-based+authentication) page.

##### Setting up token-based authentication

To set up Teamwork Cloud and Authentication server for token-based authentication using REST API endpoint

1. Set up Authentication server to work with your SAML/SSO, if needed.
2. Open <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf /authserver.properties .
3. Find key**authentication****.client****.ids** and add **,twc****-rest****-api** at the end of the value. Save and close the file. The comma before **twc-rest-api** is a separator.
4. Open <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration /application.conf .
5. Find the esi .auth block and set the server value to your authentication server IP. Save and close the file.
6. Restart the Authentication server and Teamwork Cloud server.

##### Using token-based authentication

To use token-based authentication

1. Open REST API at the following URL: https ://<ip> :8111 /osmc /authen /login on a browser.
2. You should be redirected to the Authentication server login page.
3. Enter your credentials. The browser shows you a token, usually starting with eyJ … . [ATTACHMENT filename='Login Token.png']
4. Copy the token and use it to log on to REST API. The token is used in an Authorization header with the **Token** Type.

For example (using a token with cURL):

```text

```

The result is as follows:

```text
GET /osmc/login HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8111
> User-Agent: curl/7.55.1
> Accept: */*
> Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp
IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6XC9cLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4NTU1XC9hdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiIsImV4cCI6M
TYwNjI5OTc3NywiaWF0IjoxNjA2Mjk4ODc3fQ.bA-
S5hHeSlV8AFoQVzzfIseC3qlmqQoBQREiapHN6I5CcvwetKdSVztWKkssSGjm31Y1zqoULio7_1Ma
mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-
4TCNm8II89ROaEb1tZ5nD84ieRbzJWqrcVTdqU2YfbIUeew5Nir8obkLYgixBXFKWsTHi3jNuoBx3
KcAIyZqL6cjtsCER4wbk4PEEDC57UVsOcsXWr6yvXIoVdJMOiDHo_fJMkgOjDqSyIL-2B21O-Y-GA
>
< HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
< Content-Length: 0
< Content-Type: application/octet-stream
< Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:08:44 GMT
< Accept-Ranges: bytes
< Server: Restlet-Framework/2.2.3
< Vary: Accept-Charset, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language, Accept
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-current-user=Administrator; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-session-id=f40ef933-5461-4058-a1e7-9b8d4021aa8a; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
<
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact]]>
```

##### **Limitations**

This REST API endpoint only displays the ID token. Usually, ID token is not very long-living. You can configure the ID token expiration in *authserver.properties* file (using property **authentication.token.expirity**).

Usually the ID token needs to be refreshed as described in the page [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/(2026x Refresh1) Token-based+authentication). However, this REST API does not display a refresh token, which is needed to refresh the ID token.

As a workaround, long-living ID tokens can be generated by adding **,twc****-rest****-api** to the *authserver.properties* file property **authentication.client.unlimited**. In such case, ID token expiration will be calculated using property **authentication.unlimited.token.expirity**.

Use this feature with caution and make sure that such long-living ID token is adequately protected.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181728 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00246: (2026x Refresh1) Transaction Listeners and Model Changes

- page_id: `314181728`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314181728/2026x+Refresh1+Transaction+Listeners+and+Model+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-06-26T07:42:09.460+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2 Open API, model changes are performed inside a transaction to clearly define the start and end of modification operations.

[TransactionListeners](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html) let you observe those changes and add additional logic before the transaction is completed. This is useful when a change in one part of the model should trigger related updates elsewhere.

#### Transaction lifecycle

A transaction goes through these phases:

- transactionStarted(...) – called once when the transaction begins
- transactionPreCompleted(...) – called before completion and may be invoked multiple times
- transactionCompleted(...) – called once after the transaction is finalized

For details, see [TransactionListener](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html).

The most important phase for reacting to model changes is [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)).

#### Modifying the model during a transaction

The [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)) method receives model changes recorded during the transaction as a collection of [PropertyChangeEvent](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.desktop/java/beans/PropertyChangeEvent.html) instances.

At this point:

- the model has already been updated
- the transaction is still open
- additional model changes can still be applied

You can safely make additional model changes inside [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)).

If a listener makes additional changes, the transaction is re-evaluated and listeners are invoked again with the updated set of changes. This allows implementing follow-up logic within the same transaction.

#### Example: Registering a transaction listener and reacting to element ownership changes

Register transaction listeners through [TransactionManager](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionManager.html). The following example listens for ownership changes on [OwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html) and updates the moved element name.

```text
events, TransactionOptions options)
	{
		processMovedElements(events);
	}

	private static void processMovedElements(Collection events)
	{
		for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
		{
			if (EFeatureNames.ELEMENT__OWNED_RELATIONSHIP.equals(event.getPropertyName())
				&& event.getNewValue() instanceof OwningMembership owningMembership)
			{
				Element element = owningMembership.getOwnedMemberElement();
				if (element != null)
				{
					element.setDeclaredName(element.getDeclaredName() + "_moved");
				}
			}
		}
	}
}, OperationKind.EDITING_EXECUTE);
]]>
```

#### How it works

- the ownership relationship ( OwningMembership ) is updated
- the change is exposed as a PropertyChangeEvent
- the listener inspects the change
- the moved element is retrieved using getOwnedMemberElement()
- an additional update is applied using setDeclaredName(String)

#### Important behavior

- transactionPreCompleted(...) may be called multiple times during a single transaction
- additional changes made by listeners become part of the same transaction
- transactionCompleted(...) is called only after the transaction reaches a stable state
- the model must not be modified in transactionCompleted(...)

#### Things to watch for

Because listeners can modify the model, make sure the logic does not create unintended cycles.

Typical problems include:

- repeatedly updating the same property
- triggering the same condition multiple times

Listener logic should always converge to a stable state.

#### Related APIs

- TransactionListener
- TransactionManager
- OperationKind
- TransactionManagers
- ModelElementProject
- ModelElement
- Element
- TransactionOptions

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In SysML v2 Open API, model changes are performed inside a transaction to clearly define the start and end of modification operations.</p><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html">TransactionListeners</a> let you observe those changes and add additional logic before the transaction is completed. This is useful when a change in one part of the model should trigger related updates elsewhere.</p><h2>Transaction lifecycle</h2><p>A transaction goes through these phases:</p><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionStarted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionStarted(...)</a> – called once when the transaction begins</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a> – called before completion and may be invoked multiple times</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionCompleted(...)</a> – called once after the transaction is finalized</li></ul><p>For details, see <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html">TransactionListener</a>.</p><p>The most important phase for reacting to model changes is <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a>.</p><h2>Modifying the model during a transaction</h2><p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a> method receives model changes recorded during the transaction as a collection of <a href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.desktop/java/beans/PropertyChangeEvent.html">PropertyChangeEvent</a> instances.</p><p>At this point:</p><ul><li>the model has already been updated</li><li>the transaction is still open</li><li>additional model changes can still be applied</li></ul><p>You can safely make additional model changes inside <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a>.</p><p>If a listener makes additional changes, the transaction is re-evaluated and listeners are invoked again with the updated set of changes. This allows implementing follow-up logic within the same transaction.</p><h2>Example: Registering a transaction listener and reacting to element ownership changes</h2><p>Register transaction listeners through <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionManager.html">TransactionManager</a>. The following example listens for ownership changes on <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html">OwningMembership</a> and updates the moved element name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15f57a91-181c-4d61-a5e7-f21516b26d3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionManager;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionManagers;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionListener;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.OwningMembership;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.EFeatureNames;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.OperationKind;

import java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent;
import java.util.Collection;

ModelElementProject project = ...;
TransactionManager transactionManager = TransactionManagers.getTransactionManager(project);

transactionManager.addListener(new TransactionListener()
{
	@Override
	public void transactionPreCompleted(Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events, TransactionOptions options)
	{
		processMovedElements(events);
	}

	private static void processMovedElements(Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events)
	{
		for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
		{
			if (EFeatureNames.ELEMENT__OWNED_RELATIONSHIP.equals(event.getPropertyName())
				&& event.getNewValue() instanceof OwningMembership owningMembership)
			{
				Element element = owningMembership.getOwnedMemberElement();
				if (element != null)
				{
					element.setDeclaredName(element.getDeclaredName() + "_moved");
				}
			}
		}
	}
}, OperationKind.EDITING_EXECUTE);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>How it works</h2><ul><li>the ownership relationship (<a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html">OwningMembership</a>) is updated</li><li>the change is exposed as a <a href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.desktop/java/beans/PropertyChangeEvent.html">PropertyChangeEvent</a></li><li>the listener inspects the change</li><li>the moved element is retrieved using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html#getOwnedMemberElement()">getOwnedMemberElement()</a></li><li>an additional update is applied using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setDeclaredName(java.lang.String)">setDeclaredName(String)</a></li></ul><h2>Important behavior</h2><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a> may be called multiple times during a single transaction</li><li>additional changes made by listeners become part of the same transaction</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionCompleted(...)</a> is called only after the transaction reaches a stable state</li><li>the model must not be modified in <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionCompleted(...)</a></li></ul><h2>Things to watch for</h2><p>Because listeners can modify the model, make sure the logic does not create unintended cycles.</p><p>Typical problems include:</p><ul><li>repeatedly updating the same property</li><li>triggering the same condition multiple times</li></ul><p>Listener logic should always converge to a stable state.</p><h2>Related APIs</h2><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html">TransactionListener</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionManager.html">TransactionManager</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/OperationKind.html">OperationKind</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/TransactionManagers.html">TransactionManagers</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/project/ModelElementProject.html">ModelElementProject</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html">ModelElement</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html">Element</a></li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionOptions.html">TransactionOptions</a></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989409 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00247: (2026x Refresh1) Triggering an Event

- page_id: `303989409`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989409/2026x+Refresh1+Triggering+an+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:36.450+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An execution engine can trigger a given Event by calling the triggerEvent(String event) of an ExecutionEngine. Just like the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods, this method is mostly used in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element).

If the triggerElement(String event) is called, the eventTriggered(String eventID) of all registered engine listeners will be activated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An execution engine can trigger a given Event by calling the triggerEvent(String event) of an ExecutionEngine. Just like the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods, this method is mostly used in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element).</p><p>If the triggerElement(String event) is called, the eventTriggered(String eventID) of all registered engine listeners will be activated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989160 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00248: (2026x Refresh1) UML metamodel changes

- page_id: `303989160`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989160/2026x+Refresh1+UML+metamodel+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:24.115+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two types of changes in UML metamodel API.

**UM****L specification changes**

The UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML.

Most changes are made in UML Templates, Simple Time, Interactions. We suggest to look at UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification for such changes if you see some compile errors.

**UM****L metamodel API implementation changes**

Earlier UML metamodel API used several interface layers for every compatibility level described in the UML specification. All these layers were merged into one using package merging, this is why earlier API had so many interfaces for every metamodel element in a different package. Such structure was very complicated and hardly understandable for new UML users.

MagicDraw version 15.0 and later UML metamodel API provides just one final merged layer. All intermediate layers were dropped from API. This change reduced a UML metamodel API size in few times.

Interfaces that are removed form the following packages:

- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.omg

- com.nomagic.uml2.magicdraw

- com.nomagic.uml2.omg

We left the same package name for merged interfaces like in a previous API version, that is *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.*********. You do not need to make any changes in your code if you were using interfaces from this layer. Otherwise you need to import a statement in your java source files, for example, *import**com.nomagic.uml2.omg**.kernel.Element*to *import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*.

UML metamodel interfaces use Java 5 generics, so it is much easier to understand types of various collections in the metamodel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are two types of changes in UML metamodel API.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">UM</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">L specification changes</strong></p><p>The UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. </p><p>Most changes are made in UML Templates, Simple Time, Interactions. We suggest to look at UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification for such changes if you see some compile errors.</p><p> </p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">UM</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">L metamodel API implementation changes</strong></p><p>Earlier UML metamodel API used several interface layers for every compatibility level described in the UML specification. All these layers were merged into one using package merging, this is why earlier API had so many interfaces for every metamodel element in a different package. Such structure was very complicated and hardly understandable for new UML users. </p><p>MagicDraw version 15.0 and later UML metamodel API provides just one final merged layer. All intermediate layers were dropped from API. This change reduced a UML metamodel API size in few times.</p><p>Interfaces that are removed form the following packages:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.omg</em></li></ul><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.magicdraw</em></li></ul><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.omg</em> </li></ul><p>We left the same package name for merged interfaces like in a previous API version, that is <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.</em><strong><em>** </em></strong>. You do not need to make any changes in your code if you were using interfaces from this layer. Otherwise you need to import a statement in your java source files, for example, <em>import </em><em>com.nomagic.uml2.omg</em><em>.kernel.Element </em>to <em>import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>.</p><p>UML metamodel interfaces use Java 5 generics, so it is much easier to understand types of various collections in the metamodel.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988932 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00249: (2026x Refresh1) UML model implementation in 17.0.1 and up details

- page_id: `303988932`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988932/2026x+Refresh1+UML+model+implementation+in+17.0.1+and+up+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.294+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) UML Model Implementation Using EMF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In version 17.0.1 of MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us), the UML metamodel has only one package - UML, and all UML metadata exist in this package. All UML metadata can be found in *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLPackage*. For example:

```java

```

UML model elements can be created using the EMF factory *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory*. For example:

```java

```

In version 17.0.1, an element created using the EMF factory will be placed in the repository that does not belong to any project. The created object will be automatically added into the project’s repository after it will be added into the container that is in the project’s repository.

All UML model elements are directly or indirectly contained by EMF resources. Resource of an element can be retrieved in the standard way:

```java

```

There is no strict definition how EMF resources are organized in MagicDraw project. It depends on the project type (local or from the Teamwork Cloud server).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In version 17.0.1 of MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us), the UML metamodel has only one package - UML, and all UML metadata exist in this package. All UML metadata can be found in <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLPackage</em>. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db420e1-89f5-41ea-a07a-52ebf32eed6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EAttribute nameAttribute = UMLPackage.Literals.NAMED_ELEMENT__NAME;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>UML model elements can be created using the EMF factory <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory</em>. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2ec01bd-1123-49c3-9259-e771c4cc9c80"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Component component = UMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createComponent();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In version 17.0.1, an element created using the EMF factory will be placed in the repository that does not belong to any project. The created object will be automatically added into the project’s repository after it will be added into the container that is in the project’s repository.</p><p>All UML model elements are directly or indirectly contained by EMF resources. Resource of an element can be retrieved in the standard way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba770e0c-7b9e-4cf1-8f5f-35a04d29e3ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element;
org.eclipse.emf.ecore.resource.Resource resource = element.eResource();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There is no strict definition how EMF resources are organized in MagicDraw project. It depends on the project type (local or from the Teamwork Cloud server). </p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988930 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00250: (2026x Refresh1) UML model implementation in version 17.0 details

- page_id: `303988930`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988930/2026x+Refresh1+UML+model+implementation+in+version+17.0+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.213+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) UML Model Implementation Using EMF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) 17.0 UML metamodel has a nested package structure. UML is a root package that has nested subpackages, for example, classes/mdkernel, components/mdbasiccomponents, mdusecases. Metadata about a specific model element can be found in the package of the element. For example, a UML model element *Property*is from the package *uml/classes/mdkernel*, so metadata about *Property*can be found in the package *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.metadata.MdkernelPackage*.

```java

```

Model elements can be created using the EMF UML factory *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory*: For example:

```java

```

The element will be created in the repository of the active project. UML model elements are not contained by any EMF resource.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The MagicDraw  (or other modeling tool developed by us) 17.0 UML metamodel has a nested package structure. UML is a root package that has nested subpackages, for example, classes/mdkernel, components/mdbasiccomponents, mdusecases. Metadata about a specific model element can be found in the package of the element. For example, a UML model element <em>Property </em>is from the package <em>uml/classes/mdkernel</em>, so metadata about <em>Property </em>can be found in the package <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.metadata.MdkernelPackage</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16b492e9-24a0-4813-919c-268a9a8195c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EAttribute nameAttribute = UMLPackage.Literals.NAMED_ELEMENT__NAME;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Model elements can be created using the EMF UML factory <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory</em>: For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d59b466-59fc-4a77-ab1c-35971a01e854"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Component component = UMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createComponent();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The element will be created in the repository of the active project. UML model elements are not contained by any EMF resource.</p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988928 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00251: (2026x Refresh1) UML Model Implementation Using EMF

- page_id: `303988928`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988928/2026x+Refresh1+UML+Model+Implementation+Using+EMF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.122+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: ['uml-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Starting from version 17.0, a UML model is implemented using Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF), that is the UML model is an EMF model. All UML model classes implement the *org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EObject* interface. The UML model can be accessed and changed using the EMF API. For example:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Starting from version 17.0, a UML model is implemented using Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF), that is the UML model is an EMF model. All UML model classes implement the <em>org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EObject</em> interface. The UML model can be accessed and changed using the EMF API. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0b1bbe-90bf-4573-9e51-51f77796eef7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // get project model
    Package model = project.getPrimaryModel();
    // create session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "create class");


    // get name attribute
    EAttribute element_name = UMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getNamedElement_Name();
    // get name value (same as model.getName())
    Object name = model.eGet(element_name);
    System.out.println("name = " + name);
    // change name value (same as model.setName(name + "_1");)
    model.eSet(element_name, name + "_1");


    Class aClass = UMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createClass();


    // get packaged element collection
    Collection collection = (Collection)
    model.eGet(UMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getPackage_PackagedElement());
    // add new class (same result as model.getPackagedElement().add(aClass))
    collection.add(aClass);


    // close session
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af33752-130c-44db-a684-a1347acc778b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aca68646-5aeb-4b2c-99bf-d4236f7b83b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989107 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00252: (2026x Refresh1) UML model properties names

- page_id: `303989107`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989107/2026x+Refresh1+UML+model+properties+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:19.250+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Constants for UML model properties names are defined in several classes.

Use*com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PropertyNames* for standart UML metamodel property names.

Use*com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PrivatePropertyNames*for auto-generated opposite property names.

The events’ names related with model creation/ deletion are listed in the*com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.UML2MetamodelConstants*class.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Constants for UML model properties names are defined in several classes.</p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: black;">Use </span><span style="color: black;"><em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PropertyNames</em> for standart UML metamodel property names.</span></p><p><span style="color: black;">Use </span><em style="color: black;line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PrivatePropertyNames</em><span style="color: black;"> for auto-generated opposite property names. </span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The events’ names related with model creation/ deletion are listed in the </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.UML2MetamodelConstants</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> class.</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988914 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00253: (2026x Refresh1) UML-based

- page_id: `303988914`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988914/2026x+Refresh1+UML-based
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:06.710+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['omg-uml-metamodel', 'element', 'base-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In projects created with our modeling tools the UML model is an implementation of the OMG UML 2.5.1 metamodel. We do not provide a very detailed description of all UML metamodel elements and their properties in this documentation or javadoc. Our UML implementation covers whole UML specification as its and also extends it by introducing some additional Elements (like Diagram). You can find all this information in the UML 2.5.1 superstructure specification on the Object Management Group official Web site at[http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/.](http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/)

You should use UML model interfaces from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw*package.

Never use implementation classes like com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.impl.ElementImpl in your code!

The base interface of all model classes is *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, it implements the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement* inteface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

All structure derived from the *Element*you can see in the OMG-UML 2.5.1 Superstructure Specification.

All attributes and references described in the UML specification are implemented and accessible through setters and getters.

The UML model is thread safe for reading, but still the **UML model writing (modification) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Multi-threading' space='']**.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

We do not provide a very detailed description of all UML metamodel elements and their properties in this documentation or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">javadoc</span>. Our UML implementation covers whole UML specification as its and also extends it by introducing some additional Elements (like Diagram). You can find all this information in the UML 2.5.1 superstructure specification on the Object Management Group official Web site at<a href="http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/"> http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/.</a></p><p>You should use UML model interfaces from the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw </em>package.</p><ac:structured-macro com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.impl.ElementImpl in your code!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The base interface of all model classes is <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, it implements the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</em> inteface.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="BaseElements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UML-based" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>All structure derived from the <em>Element </em>you can see in the OMG-UML <strong>UML model writing (modification) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Multi-threading" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[is not thread safe]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6effda46-6001-4f01-b36f-045a9ad9dab4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="030be7ec-b3bf-4c1f-b315-febbc4cbc82c"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989309 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00254: (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the Oracle DDL template structure

- page_id: `303989309`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989309/2026x+Refresh1+Understanding+the+Oracle+DDL+template+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:30.072+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default Oracle DDL template is stored in *<program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template*folder. The template consists of many macros - velocity functions. Each macro is dedicated for a particular object generation.

This example shows the velocity macro for the Oracle VIEW object generation:

```text
0 )
        #if( $force ) FORCE
        #else NO FORCE
        #end
    #end 
    VIEW $oracleHelper.getQualifiedName( $data )$columns AS" $nospace)
    #if ( $sql )
        #writeLine( $sql $tab )
    #end
    #writeText( ${sqlrestriction} ${space}${nospace})${semicolon}
    #end

]]>
```

A model element with the stereotype “view” is passed to a macro function, and then all element information is retrieved by the *$oracleHelper* utility class. The Oracle *View* stereotype has 3 properties. These tagged values store information about the force, query restriction, and query statements.

```text

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The default Oracle DDL template is stored in <em>&lt;program installation folder&gt;\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder. The template consists of many macros - velocity functions. Each macro is dedicated for a particular object generation.</p><p>This example shows the velocity macro for the Oracle VIEW object generation:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1754f03-c020-44f8-880b-cfa82d9449d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro ( generateView $data )
    #set ( $QUERY_RESTICTION = "query restriction")
    #set ( $force = false)
    #set ( $force = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $FORCE_TAG ) )
    #set ( $sqlrestriction = false)
    #set ( $sqlrestriction = $ oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUERY_RESTICTION ) )
    #set ( $sql = false)
    #set ( $sql = $ oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUERY_TAG ))
    #set ( $columns = false)
    #set ( $columns = $ oracleHelper.getViewColumnList( $data ) )
    #if ($columns)
        #set ( $columns = " ($columns)" )
    #else
        #set ( $columns = $nospace )
    #end
    #writeDocumentation ( $data $nospace )
    #writeLine( "$ oracleHelper.getCreate($data)
    #if ( $oracleHelper.getPropertiesListFromDefaultProfile($data, $VIEW_STEREOTYPE,$FORCE_TAG).size()>0 )
        #if( $force ) FORCE
        #else NO FORCE
        #end
    #end 
    VIEW $oracleHelper.getQualifiedName( $data )$columns AS" $nospace)
    #if ( $sql )
        #writeLine( $sql $tab )
    #end
    #writeText( ${sqlrestriction} ${space}${nospace})${semicolon}
    #end

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p></div><p>A model element with the stereotype “view” is passed to a macro function, and then all element information is retrieved by the <em>$oracleHelper</em> utility class. The Oracle <em>View</em> stereotype has 3 properties. These tagged values store information about the force, query restriction, and query statements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d60ef16-76d4-4295-a5d9-13307027b8b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Method $oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile( $data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUE- RY_RESTICTION) gets query restriction statement from element $data.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989064 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00255: (2026x Refresh1) Used project management

- page_id: `303989064`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989064/2026x+Refresh1+Used+project+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:16.197+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Working with project > (2026x Refresh1) Projects management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* provides the used project management (project usage, export, import, reload, and package sharing) methods.

Example #1. Exporting a used local project

The collection of project packages can be exported as a used project.

```java

```

###### Example #2. Exporting a used server project

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork.application.TeamworkUtils*class is used to export a used server project.

```java

```

###### Example #3. Using a project

The local and server project usage is similar - an appropriate project descriptor must be used, but module directories project option should be updated before using local project:

```java
directories = projectOptions.getModulesDirectories(true);
    if (!directories.contains(moduleDir))
    {
        projectOptions.addModuleDirectory(moduleDir);
    }
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI());
    // Use a project
    projectsManager.useModule(project, projectDescriptor);]]>
```

###### Example #4. Importing a used project

The local and server project import does not differ. Just an appropriate project descriptor must be used:

```java

```

###### Example #5. Reloading a used project

The local and server project reload does not differ. Just an appropriate project descriptor must be used:

```java

```

###### Example #6. The package sharing

```java

```

###### Example #7. Editing a used project within the project

```java
moduleUsages = Collections.singleton(moduleUsage);
 
        final boolean readOnly = attachedProject.isReadOnly();
        if (readOnly)
        {
            // Make the used project editable (the read-write accessibility mode) 
            ModulesService.setReadOnlyOnTask(moduleUsages, false);
        }
        // Get the first shared package of the used project 
        final Collection sharedPackages = ProjectUtilities.getSharedPackages(attachedProject);
        final Package aPackage = sharedPackages.iterator().next();
        // Create a class in the used project
        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Create use case in used project");
        final Class aCase = project.getElementsFactory().createClassInstance();
        aCase.setOwner(aPackage);
        aCase.setName("myClass");
        SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();
        // save the used project
        Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager().saveModule(project, attachedProject, true, false);
        if (readOnly)
        {
             // Make a used project not editable (the read-only accessibility mode) 
             ModulesService.setReadOnlyOnTask(moduleUsages, true);
        }
    }]]>
```

**Advanced management**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities* utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* or***ProjectsManager* classes for managing attached projects.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project structure decomposition' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> provides the used project management (project usage, export, import, reload, and package sharing) methods. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-weight: bold;line-height: 1.4285715;">Example #1. Exporting a used local project</span></p><p>The collection of project packages can be exported as a used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="913962ec-aad1-48bb-8902-a81e7ae50e3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(moduleFilePath);
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI());
    // Export a collection of packages as a used project 
    projectsManager.exportModule(project, packages, "My used local project", projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><h4>Example #2. Exporting a used server project</h4><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork.application.TeamworkUtils </em>class is used to export a used server project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8337accb-146f-422a-b9e7-a276c3514fde"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    TeamworkUtils.exportTeamworkModule(project, packages, "My used remote project", remoteProjectQualifiedName);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>Example #3. Using a project</h4><p>The local and server project usage is similar - an appropriate project descriptor must be used, but module directories project option should be updated before using local project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f167a1d-1b61-492d-af07-7fecf7ab221a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(moduleFilePath);
    // update project options only when using local project
    String moduleDir = file.getParent();
    ProjectOptions projectOptions = project.getOptions();
    List<String> directories = projectOptions.getModulesDirectories(true);
    if (!directories.contains(moduleDir))
    {
        projectOptions.addModuleDirectory(moduleDir);
    }
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI());
    // Use a project
    projectsManager.useModule(project, projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>Example #4. Importing a used project</h4><p>The local and server project import does not differ. Just an appropriate project descriptor must be used:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfc417a8-1948-41b1-a941-072fa2cf2855"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(moduleFilePath);
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.importModule(project, projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>Example #5. Reloading a used project</h4><p>The local and server project reload does not differ. Just an appropriate project descriptor must be used:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34b7bdea-cb62-4982-b563-3d4aa8eabbc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    File file = new File(moduleFilePath); 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI());
    projectsManager.reloadModule(project, projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>Example #6. The package sharing</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63bfe953-0a0a-49b8-ae86-0b8b4fa64a44"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Create shared package");
    // Create a package to share
    Package aPackage = project.getElementsFactory().createPackageInstance();
    aPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
    aPackage.setName("myShare");
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();
 
    // Share a package
    projectsManager.sharePackage(project, Arrays.asList(aPackage), "my used project");


    // Save a project
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.getDescriptorForProject(project);
    projectsManager.saveProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4>Example #7. Editing a used project within the project</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1d6e8ec-af93-4da5-931d-36ba95abccf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    final String moduleFileName = new File(moduleFilePath).getName();
    // Find a used project directly used by the project (the primary project)
    final IAttachedProject attachedProject = ProjectUtilities.findAttachedProjectByName(project, moduleFileName, false);
    if (attachedProject != null)
    {
        final ModuleUsage moduleUsage = new ModuleUsage(project.getPrimaryProject(), attachedProject);
        final Set<ModuleUsage> moduleUsages = Collections.singleton(moduleUsage);
 
        final boolean readOnly = attachedProject.isReadOnly();
        if (readOnly)
        {
            // Make the used project editable (the read-write accessibility mode) 
            ModulesService.setReadOnlyOnTask(moduleUsages, false);
        }
        // Get the first shared package of the used project 
        final Collection<Package> sharedPackages = ProjectUtilities.getSharedPackages(attachedProject);
        final Package aPackage = sharedPackages.iterator().next();
        // Create a class in the used project
        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Create use case in used project");
        final Class aCase = project.getElementsFactory().createClassInstance();
        aCase.setOwner(aPackage);
        aCase.setName("myClass");
        SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();
        // save the used project
        Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager().saveModule(project, attachedProject, true, false);
        if (readOnly)
        {
             // Make a used project not editable (the read-only accessibility mode) 
             ModulesService.setReadOnlyOnTask(moduleUsages, true);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Advanced management</strong></p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em> utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.</p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> or<em> </em><em>ProjectsManager</em> classes for managing attached projects.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a08eab3-2fc2-4bad-9ef7-29af33cb5d38"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project structure decomposition" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
