<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437107 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00298: (2026x) Browser

- page_id: `254437107`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437107/2026x+Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:01.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['browser', 'browser-tree']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Model Browser (Containment tree, Diagrams tree, etc.) is a based GUI part for displaying various model trees and panels

```java

```

The browser has five trees by default (some of them are be default hidden):

- containment
- diagrams
- inheritance
- extensions
- search results

The browser has 3 panel by default

- documentation
- properties
- zoom

Last activated tree is marked as active:

```java

```

Every tree is based on Swing JTree and all manipulations can be done by using the API provided by Swing:

```java

```

Selected nodes are accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Node is derived from *javax.swing.tree.DefaultMutableTreeNode*.

**Selecting/opening element in tree**

In order to select some element in tree, you need

- Open(activate) a tree using com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser#getContainmentTree ;
- Call com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Tree#openNode(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement).

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437618 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00299: (2026x) Building a resource distribution file

- page_id: `254437618`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437618/2026x+Building+a+resource+distribution+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:48.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate **.zip** files.

This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.

There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:

1) Using the **Development Tools** plugin GUI

1. Install the Development Tools plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.
2. Restart the modeling tool.
3. Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.
4. In the modeling tool main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Resource Distribution File .
5. Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click OK . A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.

The**Development Tools**plugin can be installed from[nomagic.com](https://www.nomagic.com/)or found in the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*).

2) Using command line tool.

In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.

Three arguments are required:

- Resource directory path
- Output directory path
- Result distribution file name

Example:

```text

```

Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating required files and folders structure' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate <strong>.zip</strong> files.</p><p>This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.</p><p>There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:</p><p>1) Using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin GUI</p><ol><li>Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li><li>Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.</li><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Development Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Build Resource Distribution File</strong>.</li><li>Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>. A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5659f791-f619-43e5-95fc-bc26a98bc206"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><strong> Development Tools</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> plugin can be installed from </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.nomagic.com/" rel="nofollow">nomagic.com</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> or found in the resource distribution file (.</span><em>rdzip</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2) Using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Three arguments are required:</p><ul><li>Resource directory path</li><li>Output directory path</li><li>Result distribution file name</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03b2273a-6b5a-4114-bc74-74151ee8841a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -cp "lib/*;lib/bundles/*" com.nomagic.magicdraw.resourcemanager.distribution.GenerateResourceDistributionFileTask "E:\myResources" "E:\myBundles" "myCustomResourceDistributionFile"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74a60d1f-d3d7-4766-8732-0a95016063c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00301: (2026x) Changing path break points

- page_id: `254437256`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437256/2026x+Changing+path+break+points
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:15.959+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)*. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.

The following code snippet shows how to do this:

```java

```

.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)</em>. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.</p><p>The following code snippet shows how to do this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81eb7a61-b3f9-4fd8-b334-5374f9e7d02b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PathElement element = ...;
    ArrayList points = new ArrayList(); 
    points.add(new Point(100, 100));
    points.add(new Point(100, 150));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().changePathBreakPoints(element, points);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>The order of points in the list must be from the supplier to client connection point. The list may or may not include the client and supplier connection points. At first the given points list will be adopted for the current path style (Rectilinear, Bezier<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. </em>or Oblique) and only then applied for the path.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7d0be1b-f166-46b8-8b28-4554d9e0577c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437240 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00302: (2026x) Changing properties of presentation elements

- page_id: `254437240`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437240/2026x+Changing+properties+of+presentation+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-16T15:42:41.344+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example *Autosize* for shapes or *Path Style*for paths):

- To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentStyle() .
- To get all properties of this element, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement) .
- To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager .getProperty(PresentationElement, java.lang.String) .
- To change element properties, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties( PresentationElement, PropertyManager) . The given com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).

The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID*.

Look at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space=''] page for more details about properties structure.

There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze *PresentationElementsManager**.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement)* object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.

If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.

The following code example shows how to change element properties:

```java

```

The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example <em>Autosize</em> for shapes or <em>Path Style </em>for paths):</p><ul><li data-uuid="278bcd4e-55e6-4444-a7a7-57cac90789a2">To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentStyle()</em>.</li><li data-uuid="31a08ea8-3523-4942-bab0-2a98c5e71a50">To get all properties of this element, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement)</em>.</li><li data-uuid="e5609489-1d51-4a94-ae0b-6774d0354f0d">To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager</em><em>.getProperty(PresentationElement, java.lang.String)</em>.</li><li data-uuid="5bf4c18e-ebc0-4207-8bdf-5ff05e1ec099">To change element properties, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties(</em><em>PresentationElement, PropertyManager)</em>. The given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).</li></ul><p>The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID</em>.</p><p>Look at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link> page for more details about properties structure.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76534000-96db-443a-afbc-2a32a34c27d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze <em>PresentationElementsManager</em><em>.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement)</em> object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c6fcc7-ed86-41f2-8ecf-5fe252f7261c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following code example shows how to change element properties:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb4de8c9-109d-46bb-a8d8-dd03610cf555"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Property property = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().getProperty(element, PropertyID.AUTOSIZE);
    if (property instanceof BooleanProperty)
    {
        BooleanProperty autoSize = (BooleanProperty) property.clone();
        autoSize.setValue(true);

        PropertyManager properties = new PropertyManager();
        properties.addProperty(autoSize);

        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
        PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
        SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9153917b-9322-4ccd-bd7f-879d217fcb51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad358495-8ca5-47aa-abba-1c0b8258ebc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    PropertyManager properties = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().getPropertyManager(element);
    properties.getProperty(PropertyID.AUTOSIZE).setValue(new Boolean(true));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa467e03-adb1-48ac-9d6c-74ab1f87088b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437441 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00303: (2026x) Checking element editing permissions

- page_id: `254437441`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437441/2026x+Checking+element+editing+permissions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:31.047+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is a programmer responsibility to check model modification permissions before doing model changes. Some Elements in model can be read-only, but API **does not prevent** modifications of these elements.

The Element is read-only if:

- The Element belongs to the read-only used project (module)
- The Element is not locked for editing in a Teamwork Cloud environment
- Some other restrictions are added in some specific modeling case

##### Checking editing permission

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.isEditable()* to check if Element's properties can be changed.

Editing permissions should be checked if primitive Element properties are modified (like a name, for example) or references are modified (like a Property type).

Check the adding permission if you are going to modify some containment property (like *Element.ownedElement*).

##### Checking adding permission

Use *BaseElement.canAddChild()* to check if children can be added into the Element.

Use *BaseElement.canAdd(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement)* to check if a parent can own a given element and a parent has permissions to own. This method will fail, for example, if you will try to add an Operation into a Package.

##### Checking removing permission

Use *BaseElement.isEditable()* to check if an Element can be removed (disposed) from the model.

##### Checking moving permission

The moving permission is similar to the adding permission, but checks more conditions.

##### Plugging custom permissions handler

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager.addPermissionsHandler(**ElementPermissions)* to register a custom element permissions handler. Using this API you can handle custom rules for putting a permission on the top of some specific elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>It is a programmer responsibility to check model modification permissions before doing model changes. Some Elements in model can be read-only, but API <strong>does not prevent</strong> modifications of these elements. </p><p>The Element is read-only if:</p><ul><li>The Element belongs to the read-only used project (module)</li><li>The Element is not locked for editing in a Teamwork Cloud environment</li><li>Some other restrictions are added in some specific modeling case</li></ul><h3>Checking editing permission</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.isEditable()</em>  to check if Element's properties can be changed.</p><p>Editing permissions should be checked if primitive Element properties are modified (like a name, for example) or references are modified (like a Property type).</p><p>Check the adding permission if you are going to modify some containment property (like <em>Element.ownedElement</em>). </p><h3>Checking adding permission</h3><p>Use <em>BaseElement.canAddChild()</em>  to check if children can be added into the Element. </p><p>Use <em>BaseElement.canAdd(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement)</em> to check if a parent can own a given element and a parent has permissions to own. This method will fail, for example, if you will try to add an Operation into a Package.</p><h3>Checking removing permission</h3><p>Use <em>BaseElement.isEditable()</em> to check if an Element can be removed (disposed) from the model.</p><h3>Checking moving permission</h3><p>The moving permission is similar to the adding permission, but checks more conditions. </p><h3>Plugging custom permissions handler</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager.addPermissionsHandler(</em><em>ElementPermissions)</em> to register a custom element permissions handler. Using this API you can handle custom rules for putting a permission on the top of some specific elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437260 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00304: (2026x) Code engineering set

- page_id: `254437260`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437260/2026x+Code+engineering+set
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:16.226+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Code engineering using API
- labels: ['forward-engineering', 'reverse-engineering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* object represents a code-engineering object as a resource for forward or reverse engineering.

Each *CodeEngineeringSet* provides properties, which can be read:

- Name . Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.
- Model elements . Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.
- W orking directory . Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located.

###### **Forwar****d Engineering**

To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the *CodeEngineeringSet* object. Use the *CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<BaseElement[>)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-)*method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.

Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.

**Revers****e Engineering**

Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:

1. CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<java.io.File>); This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.
2. CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.

Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet</em> object represents a code-engineering object as a resource for forward or reverse engineering.</p><div class="WordSection1"><p>Each <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">CodeEngineeringSet</em> provides properties, which can be read:</p><ul><li><strong>Name</strong>. Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model elements</strong>. Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">W</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">orking directory</strong>. Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located. </li></ul><h4><strong>Forwar</strong><strong>d Engineering</strong></h4><p>To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> object. Use the <em>CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;BaseElement<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-">&gt;)</a> </em>method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81f4f76b-be13-4403-bd5d-4ad93de08e70"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Revers</strong><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">e Engineering</strong></p></div><p>Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:</p><ol><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;java.io.File&gt;); </em>This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.</li><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); </em>This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee5c32f1-5d22-4d6c-b6ec-db6c906584b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p> Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437258 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00305: (2026x) Code engineering using API

- page_id: `254437258`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437258/2026x+Code+engineering+using+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:16.113+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.

Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:

- Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.
- Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.
- The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.

In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\codeengineering

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.</p><p>Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:</p><ul><li>Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.</li><li>Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.</li><li>The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.</li></ul><p>In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\codeengineering</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="435c38b8-d959-4e50-ab09-d696275959df"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb4629e6-9e17-496a-aa8e-a97a73d4d905"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437295 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00306: (2026x) Collecting presentation elements, finding usages in diagrams

- page_id: `254437295`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437295/2026x+Collecting+presentation+elements+finding+usages+in+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:19.097+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.

Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.

**Use SymbolElementMap**

You can take this map using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()*. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.

Presentation elements can be collected only from loaded diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.

Load diagram using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()*

****

**Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)**

Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.

*DiagramPresentationElement* API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.

*DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)* to get all displayed elements in the diagram

Use** *DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(* *Element)* to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram

If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following

- Use DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram( Element) to check if element is in diagram
- Use DiagramPresentationElement.*ensureLoaded()* to load a diagram
- Use Project.getSymbolElementMap() API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram

****

**Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them**

Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.

Use *DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)* method

More information is available in javadoc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.</p><p>Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Use SymbolElementMap</strong></p><p>You can take this map using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em>. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f94ca3-a7c2-4ffc-b4a3-46dc51de4759"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Presentation elements can be collected only from <u>loaded</u> diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.</p><p>Load diagram using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)</strong></p><p>Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement</em> API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)</em> to get all displayed elements in the diagram</p><p>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34d13d2a-0f3a-478a-afb7-93e04f472406"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following</p><ul><li>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if element is in diagram</li><li>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.<em>ensureLoaded() </em> </em> <em> </em>to load a diagram</li><li>Use <em>Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em> API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong> <br /></strong></p><p><strong>Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them </strong></p><p>Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.</p><p>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)</em> method</p><p><br /></p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00307: (2026x) Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration

- page_id: `254437516`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437516/2026x+Command+Line+Interface+CLI+for+user+administration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:38.555+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: HTTP request body

1323594362

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: HTTP request body

1323594382

1323594358

**On this page:**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: HTTP request body

1323594372

Besides using Teamwork Cloud Admin for administrative tasks, you can also use the Command Line Interface (CLI) to create and manage users, roles, and permissions. All of the CLI supported in Teamwork Cloud are properly defined on this page. You can also find them via Swagger where you can find the command-line description and issue it easily. We recommend you use CLI via [Swagger UI](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) because executing the command via Swagger also shows you the correct curl command. This link [https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) takes you to the Teamwork Cloud CLI documentation in Swagger.

###### Assign a project role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resouceId}/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a project role to a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body.

###### List all permissions

The */osmc/admin/permissions* lists all permissions in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### createroleCreate a role

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles* creates a row in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a role

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* edits a row in the server. For the format of the request and the response, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration' space=''].

###### Delete a role

DELETE to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* deletes a row in the server.

###### Create a user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/users* creates a new user. The user must not be an LDAP user.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a user

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/users/{username}* edits a user.

```text

```

The key *userName* is ignored. By default, new **otherAttributes** are merged into the server. If the value of a key in **otherAttributes** is empty, the key will be removed from the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all LDAP configurations

The */osmc/admin/ldaps* returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Import a user from LDAP

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}* imports an LDAP user into Teamwork Cloud.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Search a user in an LDAP server

Sending GET to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?<query string>* searches users from a specified LDAP server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all users

The */osmc/admin/users* returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Get user information

The */osmc/admin/users/{username}* returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

###### LIst all roles

The */osmc/admin/roles* lists all rows in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List users assigned to a particular role

The *_/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* lists users assigned to a particular role.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Unassign a role from a particular user

Sending HTTP DELETE to */admin/roles/{roleId}/users/{username}* unassigns a particular role from a specified user.

**HTTP Response**

204 with empty body.

###### Unassign a project role from a particular user

Sending DELETE to *_/osmc/workspaces/\{workspaceId\}/resources/\{resourceId\}/roles/\{roleId\}/users/\{username\}_* unassigns a project role from a particular user.

**HTTP Status**

204 with empty body.

###### Assign a role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a role to a user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body

````html
	"password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1618f2a1-9cec-4d4d-9bb5-34b9119876c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31827ac7-726d-4d71-9ab9-ecbc613b087b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{

        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>List all LDAP configurations</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps</em> returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81793cdb-7e88-4bae-9ff0-3be38292c4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95405d87-0c87-4d9a-af74-8a86524cc5d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "environment": {
        "authentype": "simple",
        "searchbase": "ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "authen_dntype": "template",
        "anoymousbind": "false",
        "ldap_realm_name": "ldap0",
        "java.naming.factory.initial": "com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory",
        "java.naming.security.protocol": "none",
        "userDNTemplate": "uid={0},ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "com.sun.jndi.ldap.read.timeout": "500",
        "java.naming.provider.url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389",
        "weight": "1073741823",
        "enabled": "true"
    },
    "protocol": "ldap",
    "port": "389",
    "IP": "ldap.th.nomagic.com",
    "ID": "36f8b736-f51c-43a2-a9a6-63905419838a",
    "authen": "simple",
    "userName": "cn=admin,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
    "url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Import a user from LDAP</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}</em> imports an LDAP user into Teamwork Cloud.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e4039fe-5cc2-4fba-b8f3-4f59dfa3eef1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="633ae014-fa29-4246-8bd2-aad4e767890f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "realmid": "36f8b736-f56c-43a2-a9a6-54128919838a",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Search a user in an LDAP server</h4><p>Sending GET to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?&lt;query string&gt;</em> searches users from a specified LDAP server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="716cf3c7-d0a7-497e-9c85-e858fbaea137"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="833c82a9-15ff-4d4a-830a-a64b297e4c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "mobile": "0123456789",
    "fullName": "Bat",
    "department": "production",
    "userName": "bat",
    "email": "bat@nma.com",
    "userDN": "uid=bat,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "abc",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "abc",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=abc,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "aaaa",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "aaa",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=aaa,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>List all users</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users</em> returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0eedc27c-44a6-49ae-87c3-b4cbb4d56698"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="021da6af-cb78-46e6-90dc-2d0a3b4b6d14"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[["admin", "observer1", "user1", "user2", "worker1"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Get user information</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users/{username}</em> returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb1b2b61-98a6-4e4c-b20f-204926726d51"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aff6d549-2ed1-4bbc-ac71-3c3da655be30"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "roleAssignments": [{
        "roleID": "3e4696d5-1937-4741-aacb-062378e0cda2",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "3871e058-65e6-40d9-bd23-f80bdc074394"
    }, {
        "roleID": "417494bc-d0e8-449a-a8ac-5476dc2e6537",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "54b8ef7f-f60c-410f-8a09-93a15c51bbaa"
    }, {
        "roleID": "a020ca32-22a2-4c90-8513-f6b9c4ea8513",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "51ae2287-405e-42a6-807f-ca5e6f2a41d0"
    }, {
        "roleID": "e3cf7416-9cf3-42ff-84e6-e9d4283dfd18",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "a147bf72-1a51-49f5-baf9-ac3f2b6faf50"
    }],
    "userName": "user1",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "realmid": "",
        "mobile": "",
        "name": "",
        "department": "",
        "email": ""
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>LIst all roles</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/roles</em> lists all rows in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09895de5-df3d-4961-b0a2-072397b91b10"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da72351-7f11-4333-b81f-cd943b9fbaad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "permissions": [{

}, {

charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1f120ab-496b-4ac4-8175-c2bb63c72bd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[["admin","observer1"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Unassign a role from a particular user</h4><p>Sending HTTP DELETE to <em>/admin/roles/{roleId}/users/{username}</em> unassigns a particular role from a specified user.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>HTTP Response</strong></p><p>204 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4>Unassign a project role from a particular user</h4><p>Sending DELETE to <em>_/osmc/workspaces/\{workspaceId\}/resources/\{resourceId\}/roles/\{roleId\}/users/\{username\}_</em> unassigns a project role from a particular user.</p><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>204 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4>Assign a role to a particular user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users</em> assigns a role to a user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73926ca-e6c3-4f0a-a22b-9675af71c27d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: text/plain]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43c88f46-412f-4ba3-b8ad-e4849cd86cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[user1,user2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>201 with empty body</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437486 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00308: (2026x) Comparing projects for testing purposes

- page_id: `254437486`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437486/2026x+Comparing+projects+for+testing+purposes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:34.783+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Creating test cases
- labels: ['projects-comparator', 'model-comparator', 'model-comparator-filter', 'diagram-comparator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To compare two projects for testing purpose, the test framework provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ProjectsComparator* class with the *compare(Project[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)* method for comparing two projects (see the following figure). The default implementation of *ProjectComparator* can be created using the *createProjectComparator(java.lang.String)* method of the *MagicDrawTestCase* class. Projects can be compared by checking their model elements and element symbols in diagrams for equality.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The model elements comparison can be configured using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparator* interface. Developers may exclude some model elements from the comparison by providing the custom *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparatorFilter* interface implementation. Please note that the default implementation of *ProjectComparator* does not compare the diagram information table, root model comment, and elements from used projects.

The default implementation of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.DiagramComparator* compares diagrams for changes by analyzing a location and a size of the symbols presented in diagrams. The graphical differences of compared diagrams can be saved in the graphical PNG file using the *saveDiffToImageFiles(java.io.File)* method of the *DiagramComparator*interface.

Developers may create their own comparators for the custom diagrams and model elements comparison by implementing *ModelComparator* and *DiagramComparator* interfaces. The custom *Model* and *Diagram* comparators implementation can be set to *ProjectComparator* using appropriate setter methods.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To compare two projects for testing purpose, the test framework provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ProjectsComparator</em> class with the <em>compare(Project<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-">, </a>Project<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-">)</a></em> method for comparing two projects (see the following figure). The default implementation of <em>ProjectComparator</em> can be created using the <em>createProjectComparator(java.lang.String)</em> method of the <em>MagicDrawTestCase</em> class. Projects can be compared by checking their model elements and element symbols in diagrams for equality.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Project comparator provided by test framework" ac:alt="Project comparator provided by test framework"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_comparator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Comparing projects for testing purposes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The model elements comparison can be configured using the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparator</em> interface. Developers may exclude some model elements from the comparison by providing the custom <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparatorFilter</em> interface implementation. Please note that the default implementation of <em>ProjectComparator</em> does not compare the diagram information table, root model comment, and elements from used projects.</p><p>The default implementation of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.DiagramComparator</em> compares diagrams for changes by analyzing a location and a size of the symbols presented in diagrams. The graphical differences of compared diagrams can be saved in the graphical PNG file using the <em>saveDiffToImageFiles(java.io.File)</em> method of the <em>DiagramComparator </em>interface.</p><p>Developers may create their own comparators for the custom diagrams and model elements comparison by implementing <em>ModelComparator</em> and <em>DiagramComparator</em> interfaces. The custom <em>Model</em> and <em>Diagram</em> comparators implementation can be set to <em>ProjectComparator</em> using appropriate setter methods.</p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437565 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00309: (2026x) Compilation classpath

- page_id: `254437565`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437565/2026x+Compilation+classpath
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:01:31.134+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins
- labels: ['patch', 'brand', 'jar']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Core libraries**

The correct classpath must be specified when compiling your (plugin) code or running **.jar files from <modeling tool installation directory>\lib*, as well as all its subdirectories must be included into the classpath.

You can open the properties file in*<modeling tool installation directory>\bin*and check theCLASSPATHproperty to be sure what files must be included.

Make sure that following *.jar files are added in such order:

1. brand.jar (if the file exists) . This file is needed if the Cameo Enterprise Architecture program is used. MagicDraw does not requires this file.
2. brand_api.jar (if the file exists)*
3. Rest *.jar files. Order of them is not important

Make sure that only the *.jar files coming with the current program version you are included. If you have upgraded your modeling tool from an earlier version, the *lib* directory may contain files from the earlier version. Do not include these obsolete *.jar files.

**Plugins libraries**

Keep in mind that some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that *jar*files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Core libraries</strong></p><p>The correct classpath must be specified when compiling your (plugin) code or running <em>*.jar files from &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\lib</em>, as well as all its subdirectories must be included into the classpath.</p><p><span>You can open the properties file in </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em><span> and check the </span>CLASSPATH<span> property to be sure what files must be included.</span></p><p>Make sure that following *.jar files are added in such order:</p><ol><li>brand.jar (if the file exists)<span>. </span><span>This file is needed if the Cameo Enterprise Architecture program is used. </span><span>MagicDraw does not requires this file.</span></li><li>brand_api.jar (if the file exists)*</li><li>Rest *.jar files. Order of them is not important<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="379fd914-ffb0-475a-8fe2-bf0ddd4645e7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure that only the *.jar files coming with the current program version you are included. If you have upgraded your modeling tool from an earlier version, the <em>lib</em> directory may contain files from the earlier version. Do not include these obsolete *.jar files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Plugins libraries</strong></p><p>Keep in mind that some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that <em>jar </em>files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437423 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00310: (2026x) Compiler API

- page_id: `254437423`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437423/2026x+Compiler+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:28.679+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Alf Plugin Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *AlfCompiler* class provides the basic interface for compiling Alf code. The processing of a single piece of Alf text is called a *compilation,* which takes place in two steps:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compiler API: Parsing' space=''] (including constraint checking)
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compiler API: Mapping' space='']

The API allows you to carry out these steps individually (which is useful if, for example, you wish to parse but not map some code) or using a single call.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>AlfCompiler</em> class provides the basic interface for compiling Alf code. The processing of a single piece of Alf text is called a <em>compilation,</em> which takes place in two steps:</p><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compiler API: Parsing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parsing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (including constraint checking)</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compiler API: Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mapping]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol><p>The API allows you to carry out these steps individually (which is useful if, for example, you wish to parse but not map some code) or <span class="confluence-link">using a single call</span>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e705e7-c70e-4d93-b5f0-d11eaf392bbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">
       Related Pages
      </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f1fa4f0e-d74d-43ad-a221-703585bb068a" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437431 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00311: (2026x) Compiler API: Mapping

- page_id: `254437431`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437431/2026x+Compiler+API+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:29.833+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x) Compiler API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*Mapping* is the process of generating a UML activity model from the abstract syntax representation of some Alf code. If this mapping is successful, then the Alf compiler updates the context element using the generated elements. How this update is done depends on what the context element is:

- If the context element is an Activity, the nodes and edges of the Activity are replaced with those generated by the compilation.
- If the context element is an Opaque Behavior or Opaque Action, a CompiledRepresentation stereotype is applied with its behavior tag pointing to the compiled Behavior.
- If the context element is an Opaque Expression, its behavior property is set to the compiled Behavior.

In addition, there may be some generated elements that cannot be stored in conjunction with the context element. In particular, generated Instance Specifications are stored in the nearest Package containing the context element, and generated template instantations are stored in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='The Alf compiler' space='MAA'] package.

If you have successfully parsed some Alf code text, then you can map that code by calling the *map* method. (If the parse was not successful, then calling *map* has no effect.) Normally, unless there is a system error, mapping should always complete successfully. Since mapping results in an update to the UML model, the *map* method should be called within a MagicDraw session. This can be done conveniently using the *AlfActionUtil.executeSession* method, which also ensures that any automatic Alf compilation is turned off during the session, so new compilations are not accidentally triggered by updates from the mapping process.

```java

```

Rather than separately parsing and mapping some Alf text, you can attempt to do both with one call to the *compile* method. This method first parses some text for a given context element and, if that is successful, maps it and updates the UML model appropriately with the results of the mapping. If the parse is not successful, then compilation errors are available using the *getCompilerErrors* method, as after a call to the *parse* method. As for the *map* method, the *compile* method should be called within a MagicDraw session, since it may potentially update the UML model if a mapping is carried out.

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compiler API: Parsing' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>Mapping</em> is the process of generating a UML activity model from the abstract syntax representation of some Alf code. If this mapping is successful, then the Alf compiler updates the context element using the generated elements. How this update is done depends on what the context element is:</p><ul><li>If the context element is an Activity, the nodes and edges of the Activity are replaced with those generated by the compilation.</li><li>If the context element is an Opaque Behavior or Opaque Action, a <em>CompiledRepresentation</em> stereotype is applied with its <em>behavior</em> tag pointing to the compiled Behavior.</li><li>If the context element is an Opaque Expression, its <em>behavior</em> property is set to the compiled Behavior.</li></ul><p>In addition, there may be some generated elements that cannot be stored in conjunction with the context element. In particular, generated Instance Specifications are stored in the nearest Package containing the context element, and generated template instantations are stored in the <ac:link ac:anchor="template-bindings-package"><ri:page ri:space-key="MAA" ri:content-title="The Alf compiler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[$$Template Bindings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> package.</p><p>If you have successfully parsed some Alf code text, then you can map that code by calling the <em>map</em> method. (If the parse was not successful, then calling <em>map</em> has no effect.) Normally, unless there is a system error, mapping should always complete successfully. Since mapping results in an update to the UML model, the <em>map</em> method should be called within a MagicDraw session. This can be done conveniently using the <em>AlfActionUtil.executeSession</em> method, which also ensures that any automatic Alf compilation is turned off during the session, so new compilations are not accidentally triggered by updates from the mapping process.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="122c120f-9064-4027-a2bd-0099d7c61cba"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">
       java
      </ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[AlfCompiler compiler = new AlfCompiler();
compiler.setContextElement(element);
compiler.parse(text);
if (compiler.isSuccessful()) {
	AlfActionUtil.executeSession("Map Alf", new Runnable() {
		public void run() {
			compiler.map();
			AlfActionUtil.registerDependencies(compiler);
 		}
 	});
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Rather than separately <span class="confluence-link">parsing</span> and <span class="confluence-link">mapping</span> some Alf text, you can attempt to do both with one call to the <em>compile</em> method. This method first parses some text for a given context element and, if that is successful, maps it and updates the UML model appropriately with the results of the mapping. If the parse is not successful, then compilation errors are available using the <em>getCompilerErrors</em> method, as after a call to the <em>parse</em> method. As for the <em>map</em> method, the <em>compile</em> method should be called within a MagicDraw session, since it may potentially update the UML model if a mapping is carried out.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33649f26-e83d-42ba-8e21-89c56e23401a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">
       java
      </ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Compiler compiler = new AlfCompiler();
AlfActionUtil.executeSession("Compile Alf", new Runnable() {
	public void run() {
		compiler.compile(element, text); 
		AlfActionUtil.registerDependencies(compiler);
	}
});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20911721-b872-4176-bfa5-9aae19d59ee1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">
       Related Pages
      </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compiler API: Parsing" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437425 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00312: (2026x) Compiler API: Parsing

- page_id: `254437425`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437425/2026x+Compiler+API+Parsing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:28.849+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x) Compiler API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*Parsing* is the process of creating an abstract syntax representation of some Alf code. If parsing is successful, the Alf compiler also performs constraint checking on the resulting abstract syntax tree. *Constraint checking* is the process of doing name resolution and validating the Alf code against the static-semantic constraints defined in the Alf specification.

A compilation takes place in the context of a specific Named Element in the UML model, called the *context element*. During parsing and constraint checking, name resolution is performed in the scope containing the context element (known, in Alf terms, as the *model scope*). After the successful mapping of Alf code to UML, the context element is updated with the results of the compilation. The context element should be an Activity, Opaque Behavior, Opaque Action or Opaque Expression

In UML, a Behavior may have a context Classifier (for example, the Class that owns a State Machine as its classifier behavior is the context Classifier for the State Machine). This use of the term "context" is unrelated to the term "context element" for an Alf compilation.

To compile some Alf code text, first set the context element using the *setContextElement* method and then call the *parse* method, passing the text. What Alf text can be validly parsed depends on what the context element is.

- If the context element is a Value Specification, then the text is assumed to be for an Alf Expression.
- If the context element is an Opaque Action, then the text may be an Alf Expression or Statement Sequence.
- Otherwise the text is assumed to be for an Alf Statement Sequence.

The Alf code associated with an Activity, Opaque Behavior, Opaque Action or Opaque Expression is known as the *Alf body* of that element. The Alf code is saved in the model differently for different kinds of Elements. However, you can use the *AlfElementUtil.getAlfBody* operation to get the Alf body of an Element (if it has one).

After the parsing process completes, you can check if it was successful by calling the *isSuccessful* method. If the parse is successful, then the resulting abstract syntax tree is rooted in an Alf Unit Definition obtained by calling the *getUnit* method, which in turn always contains an Activity Definition (which may be obtained by calling the *UnitDefinition.getDefinition* method). If there were errors, then you can retrieve them using the *get**CompilerErrors* method. A single *AlfCompiler* instance my be used to compile multiple Alf code texts, for the same or different context elements.

Alf abstract syntax classes are found in sub-packages of the package *org.modeldriven.alf.syntax,* which is from the Alf Reference Implementation, with source available [here](https://github.com/ModelDriven/Alf-Reference-Implementation/tree/master/org.modeldriven.alf/src/org/modeldriven/alf/syntax). These abstract syntax classes allow the abstract syntax tree to be navigated based on the structure of the abstract syntax metamodel defined in the Alf specification. For instance, the value of the *definition* property of a *UnitDefinition* is obtained by calling *getDefinition,* the *name* of that definition is obtained using *getName,* etc. (Note that use of the Reference Implementation outside of MagicDraw tooling is subject to its separate open-source licensing terms.)

For the purposes of triggering automatic compilation, the Alf compiler also [manages a record of the dependencies](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MAA/Dependency+Management) of Alf code in a model on other model elements. After successfully parsing some Alf code, if you want to update the dependency record based on the parse, you need to call the *AlfActionUtil.registerDependencies* method. If you do multiple parses with the same *AlfCompiler* instance, then you need to call *registerDependencies* after each parse operation.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If the parse is successful, then the resulting abstract syntax tree is rooted in an Alf Unit Definition obtained by calling the <em>getUnit</em> method, which in turn always contains an Activity Definition (which may be obtained by calling the <em>UnitDefinition.getDefinition</em> method). If there were errors, then you can retrieve them using the <em>get</em><em>CompilerErrors</em> method. A single <em>AlfCompiler</em> instance my be used to compile multiple Alf code texts, for the same or different context elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ad16a2d-8894-4280-816c-b63c48c9c437"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Alf abstract syntax classes are found in sub-packages of the package <em>org.modeldriven.alf.syntax,</em> which is from the Alf Reference Implementation, with source available <a href="https://github.com/ModelDriven/Alf-Reference-Implementation/tree/master/org.modeldriven.alf/src/org/modeldriven/alf/syntax">here</a>. For instance, the value of the <em>definition</em> property of a <em>UnitDefinition</em> is obtained by calling <em>getDefinition,</em> the <em>name</em> of that definition is obtained using <em>getName,</em> etc. (Note that use of the Reference Implementation outside of MagicDraw tooling is subject to its separate open-source licensing terms.)</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">For the purposes of triggering automatic compilation, the Alf compiler also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MAA/Dependency+Management">manages a record of the dependencies</a> of Alf code in a model on other model elements. After successfully parsing some Alf code, if you want to update the dependency record based on the parse, you need to call the <em>AlfActionUtil.registerDependencies</em> method. If you do multiple parses with the same <em>AlfCompiler</em> instance, then you need to call <em>registerDependencies</em> after each parse operation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c17cab0-e479-4393-b607-716e92aee94a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">
````html
       java
      </ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[AlfCompiler compiler = new AlfCompiler(); 
compiler.setContextElement(element); 
compiler.parse(AlfElementUtil.getAlfBody(element)); 
if (compiler.isSuccessful()) { 
	AlfActionUtil.registerDependencies(compiler); 
	UnitDefinition unit = compiler.getUnit(); 
	... 
} else { 
	for (CompilerError error: compiler.getCompilerErrors()) { 
		... 
	} 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
