<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437429 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00313: (2026x) Compiler API: Utilities

- page_id: `254437429`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437429/2026x+Compiler+API+Utilities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:29.265+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x) Compiler API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of explicitly creating an *AlfCompiler* object and using its methods, you can alternatively parse and map Alf code using static methods provided by the *AlfActionUtil* class.

The *AlfActionUtil.parse* method takes a Named Element and, using this as the context element, parses and constraint checks the Alf body of the Element, if it has one. If the parsing and constraint checking succeed, then the method also automatically registers dependencies. The method returns a Boolean indicating whether the parse succeeded or not. If the parse fails, Compiler Errors are reported in an error message that is attached to the context element using an error Annotation (which then becomes available as an Active Validation Result, as described in [the Alf editor](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MAA/The+Alf+editor)).

```java

```

The *AlfActionUtil.compile* method takes a Named Element and, using this as the context element, both parses and maps the Alf body of the Element, if it has one. If the compilation is successful, then the method updates the context element and automatically registers dependencies. The method returns a Boolean indicating whether the parse succeeded or not. As with the *parse* method, if the compilation fails, Compiler Errors are recorded in an error message that is attached to the context element using an error Annotation. Unlike the *AlfCompiler.map* and *AlfCompiler.compile*methods, you do not need to explicitly start a MagicDraw session to call the *AlfActionUtil.compile* method, because it ensures internally that a session is started for mapping, if necessary.

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compiler API: Parsing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compiler API: Mapping' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

	...
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <em>AlfActionUtil.compile</em> method takes a Named Element and, using this as the context element, both parses and maps the Alf body of the Element, if it has one. }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec166587-49db-4c7d-9533-92f8b7225bb7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437491 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00314: (2026x) Configure test environment

- page_id: `254437491`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437491/2026x+Configure+test+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:35.526+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Creating test cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

502787105

502787126

502787116

Test cases can be run as a regular JUnit test case using the JUnit *TestRunner* class or configuration tools such as Ant or Maven. However, some test case specific system properties should be set.

The program installation directory should be also specified using the *install.root* system property which can be passed to JVM as a runtime argument *-Dinstall.root=path_to_modeling_tool_installation_directory*.

It is possible to set up the program installation to use the floating license server or license file. For more information, see the "Providing licensing information" chapter at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Licensing information' space=''].

Test cases may use external resources such as configuration files and projects during tests. It is recommended to keep external test resources apart from test case implementation class files. The test framework provides methods for retrieving test resources from the one specific resource directory. The resource directory for test cases can be configured by specifying the*tests.resources* system property of JVM. The tests resource property can be passed to JVM as a runtime argument

```text

```

The Log4j logger used in test cases can be configured by specifying the *test.properties* file which should be placed in *<modeling tool installation directory>\data*.

The program is started in a "silent" mode when running tests - the user interference requiring dialogs is not shown, instead of the warning "*WARN GENERAL - TRYING TO DISPLAY "<dialog title>" DIALOG IN SILENT MODE*" is logged. The stack trace is logged too if a special system property *-DprintSilentDialogStackTrace=true* is set.

502787104

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Developing plugins using IDE' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Licensing information' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437342 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00315: (2026x) Configuring cell entries - dependencies

- page_id: `254437342`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437342/2026x+Configuring+cell+entries+-+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:22.841+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['dependency-extractor', 'configure-dependency-handlers', 'dependency-matrix-configurator', 'get-dependencies', 'dependency-entry', 'dependency-editor', 'can-create', 'create-add-actions', 'can-edit', 'create-edit-actions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In order to get the dependencies between row and column elements in the dependency matrix, components of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor* interface are used. These components allow to

- Define custom dependencies that can be created in a cell.
- Add shortcut menu commands for navigation (other than opening the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the element and selecting the element in the Containment tree).
- Construct smart listener configurations that define cases when some element dependencies should be updated instantly (without the full rebuild of the matrix).

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.configureDependencyHandlers(java.util.Collection<**DependencyExtractor>, java.util.Collection<DependencyEditor>)* method to register a custom dependency extractor. The method can remove the default dependency extractor from the collection and leave only a custom extractor.

The most important method is com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor.getDependencies(ElementNode, ElementNode). It is called to add dependencies in the matrix cell. By using custom algorithms you can create additional instances of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyEntry* class for each cell.

###### Configuring the shortcut menu of a cell

All commands of the shortcut menu of a cell, are available only from the components of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.editing.DependencyEditor* interface. These components allow for

- Defining if new dependency can be created in the cell.
- Defining if current cell value can be edited.
- Adding custom actions for creating or editing dependencies.

Use the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator.configureDependencyHandlers(java.util.Collection<**DependencyExtractor>, java.util.Collection<DependencyEditor>)* method to register a custom dependency editor. The method implementation can remove the default dependency editor from the collection and leave only a custom editor.

Dependency matrix cells with the default renderer can be displayed either as editable or as read-only. If it is possible to create or delete at least one dependency in the cell, the cell is displayed as editable, that is, the cell's background is white.

When adding a new command, the *DependencyEditor.canCreate(**PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElemenNode, AbstractMatrixCell)* method is first called and if it returns *true*, the *DependencyEditor.createAddActions(**PersistenceManager, *ElementNode*, *ElementNode*, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory)* method is called to add the command.

When editing a command, the *DependencyEditor.canEdit(**PersistenceManager, **ElementNode**, **ElementNode**, AbstractMatrixCell)* method is first called and if it returns *true*, the *DependencyEditor.createEditActions(PersistenceManager[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)**ElementNode**[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)**ElementNode**[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)AbstractMatrixCell[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)ActionsCategory[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)ActionsCategory[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)* method is called to edit the command.

## PAGE 00316: (2026x) Configuring element Specification window

- page_id: `254437117`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437117/2026x+Configuring+element+Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:03.841+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Using and extending other UI components
- labels: ['ispecification-node-configurator', 'node', 'tree']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API provides a way to configure the elements' [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. With your own configurator you can create new nodes or remove already existing nodes. Nodes are items of the tree visible on the left side in every [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.SpecificationDialogManager*****class should be used for registering a specification dialog configurator***com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.configurator.ISpecificationNodeConfigurator*.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/elementspecification*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Open API provides a way to configure the elements' <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. With your own configurator you can create new nodes or remove already existing nodes. Nodes are items of the tree visible on the left side in every <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.SpecificationDialogManager<strong> </strong></em>class should be used for registering a specification dialog configurator<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.configurator.ISpecificationNodeConfigurator</em>.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/elementspecification</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437113 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00317: (2026x) Configuring node text and icon

- page_id: `254437113`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437113/2026x+Configuring+node+text+and+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:03.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You might need to change a text or icon for some Elements(nodes) in trees. For example, the adorn standard Element icon for showing that Element is annotated like the Validation framework does.

**Option #1 for changing just a text**

The text can be changed using a custom representation provider as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating textual element representation' space='']. Using this approach, the text will be changed everywhere in UI, not just in trees.

**Option #2 for changing a node text or icon**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addIconAdornment(**IconAdornment)* to register a node icon customizer.

Use *TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addTextAdornment(TextAdornment[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/TreeNodeAdornmentManager.html#addTextAdornment-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TextAdornment-)* to register a node text customizer.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating textual element representation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You might need to change a text or icon for some Elements(nodes) in trees. For example, the adorn standard Element icon for showing that Element is annotated like the Validation framework does.</p><p><strong>Option #1 for changing just a text</strong></p><p>The text can be changed using a custom representation provider as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating textual element representation" /></ac:link>. Using this approach, the text will be changed everywhere in UI, not just in trees.</p><p><strong>Option #2 for changing a node text or icon</strong></p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addIconAdornment(</em><em>IconAdornment)</em> to register a node icon customizer.</p><p>Use <em>TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addTextAdornment(TextAdornment<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/TreeNodeAdornmentManager.html#addTextAdornment-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TextAdornment-">)</a></em> to register a node text customizer.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f80fb8f-62ab-4a95-b916-c5df66cb9b23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating textual element representation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437330 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00318: (2026x) Configuring shortcut menus of a row or column header element and a filter panel

- page_id: `254437330`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437330/2026x+Configuring+shortcut+menus+of+a+row+or+column+header+element+and+a+filter+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:22.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['dependency-matrix-action-registry', 'amconfigurator', 'for-row']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a custom command to the shortcut menu of the row or column header element, or to the one of the filter panel, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixActionRegistry* class.

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator* interface has a single method that adds custom commands. The location of a new command depends on the parameters, such as the following:

- If the collection of element nodes is empty, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the filter panel.
- If the collection of element nodes is not empty and forRow is true , the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the row header.
- If the collection of element nodes is not empty and forRow is false the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the column header element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a custom command to the shortcut menu of the row or column header element, or to the one of the filter panel, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixActionRegistry</em> class.</p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator</em> interface has a single method that adds custom commands. The location of a new command depends on the parameters, such as the following:</p><ul><li>If the collection of element nodes is empty, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the filter panel.</li><li>If the collection of element nodes is not empty and <em>forRow</em> is <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">true</em>, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the row header.</li><li>If the collection of element nodes is not empty and <em>forRow</em> is <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">false </em>the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the column header element.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437320 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00319: (2026x) Configuring the command bar and shortcut keys

- page_id: `254437320`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437320/2026x+Configuring+the+command+bar+and+shortcut+keys
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:21.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['amconfigurator', 'actions-configurator-manager', 'dependency-matrix-diagram-descriptor', 'dependency-matrix-configurator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add custom commands to the command bar of a dependency matrix as well as assign custom shortcut keys for the commands.

To add a new command to the command bar, add an instance of the *com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator* interface to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager* class, as is shown in the following example:

```java

```

When customizing the default dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE* constant*.*And when customizing a custom dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the instance of the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class as well as in the **Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog.

The same code example can be used for the shortcut key configuration:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>You can add custom commands to the command bar of a dependency matrix as well as assign custom shortcut keys for the commands.</p><p>To add a new command to the command bar, add an instance of the <em>com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator</em> interface to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> class, as is shown in the following example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0b586e6-5698-4f89-bd97-44da0e21109f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addDiagramCommandBarConfigurator(
			DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE, new AMConfigurator()
    {
        @Override
        public void configure(ActionsManager mngr)
        {
            mngr.addCategory(new ActionsCategory("ID", "My Category"));
        }
 
        @Override
        public int getPriority()
        {
             return AMConfigurator.HIGH_PRIORITY;
        }
    });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>When customizing the default dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE</em> constant<em>. </em>And when customizing a custom dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the instance of the <em>DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class as well as in the <strong>Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard </strong>dialog.</p><p>The same code example can be used for the shortcut key configuration:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65e7f44d-19b8-4e4d-90f4-c1a5551bdd10"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addDiagramShortcutsConfigurator(...)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437529 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00320: (2026x) Copying elements and symbols

- page_id: `254437529`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437529/2026x+Copying+elements+and+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:40.218+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.

There are two modes of making copies:

- Deep copying (with new data)
- Shallow copying (with reused data)

###### **Example #1: Copying an element**

****

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**\openapi\examples\copypaste*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Checking element editing permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.</p><p>There are two modes of making copies:</p><ul><li>Deep copying (with new data)</li><li>Shallow copying (with reused data)</li></ul><p> </p><h4><strong>Example #1: Copying an element</strong></h4><p> </p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deac64b1-d060-474a-a06a-bf2681845ca5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element element = ...; // An element to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which the element has to be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");
 
    // A 3rd parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    CopyPasting.copyPasteElement(element, parent, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></div><h4><strong>Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols</strong></h4><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88ba897e-6dca-4f24-b872-a494d56d1ed5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List elements = ...; // Elements to copy/paste. 
    List views = ...; // Symbols to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which elements should be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    BaseElement symbolParent = ...; // A parent to which symbols should be pasted. 
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");


    // A 4th parameter indicates whether deep or shallow copy is applied.
    // A 5th parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    List baseElements = CopyPasting.copyPasteElements(views, parent, symbolParent, true, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>\openapi\examples\copypaste</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437246 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00321: (2026x) Create OCL2.0 validation rule

- page_id: `254437246`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437246/2026x+Create+OCL2.0+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:15.272+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OCL2.0 validation rule describes the validation logic using OCL2.0.

To create the OCL2.0 validation rule

1. Create a constraint.
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language OCL2.0
6. Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification
7. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating validation rules' space='']

The OCL2.0 validation rule can be used in an active validation suite. In this case the validation rule will be executed on any change of constrained elements that are from the validation scope. Executing of the validation rule might be triggered even if no properties important to the validation rule actually changed and this can slow down the active validating speed. In order to avoid degradation of the performance you can create a binary validation rule that uses the OCL2.0 expression to validate model elements but also provides additional information for the modeling tool that allows to optimize triggering of executing after model elements change. For example, we want to check whether all classes have names. This can be done by creating the OCL2.0 validation rule and specifying the OLC2.0 expression:

```text
name <> ‘’
```

The problem is that such validation rule actually is interested in a class property name value change but it will be executed on every property value of a class change. How we can fix this problem and inform the modeling tool to execute the validation rule only on the class property name change:

1. Create a constraint.
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language OCL2.0
6. Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification
7. Create a Java class that extends the com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl class
8. Override the method DefaultValidationRuleImpl.getListenerConfigurations()

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations()
        {
            Map, Collection> configs = 
						new HashMap, Collection>();
            Collection configsForClass = new ArrayList();
            configsForClass.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
            configs.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configsForClass);
            return configs;
        }]]>
```

9. Specify the validation rule’s implementation property value - a qualified name of the class

10. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite

See the *MyOCLBasedValidationRuleImpl.java* example in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation* directory.

The implementation class must be in the classpath of the modeling tool or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binary validation rule in the plugin' space='']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The OCL2.0 validation rule describes the validation logic using OCL2.0.</p><p>To create the OCL2.0 validation rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a constraint.</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language OCL2.0</li><li>Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification</li><li>Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The OCL2.0 validation rule can be used in an active validation suite. In this case the validation rule will be executed on any change of constrained elements that are from the validation scope. Executing of the validation rule might be triggered even if no properties important to the validation rule actually changed and this can slow down the active validating speed. In order to avoid degradation of the performance you can create a binary validation rule that uses the OCL2.0 expression to validate model elements but also provides additional information for the modeling tool that allows to optimize triggering of executing after model elements change. For example,  we want to check whether all classes have names. This can be done by creating the OCL2.0 validation rule and specifying the OLC2.0 expression:</p></div><pre>    name &lt;&gt; ‘’</pre><p>The problem is that such validation rule actually is interested in a class property name value change but it will be executed on every property value of a class change. How we can fix this problem and inform the modeling tool to execute the validation rule only on the class property name change:</p><ol><li>Create a constraint.</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language OCL2.0</li><li>Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification</li><li>Create a Java class that extends the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl</em> class</li><li>Override the method<em> DefaultValidationRuleImpl.getListenerConfigurations()</em></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="973fd9f7-00bc-4430-9d30-eb80eb0615b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        public Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations()
        {
            Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> configs = 
						new HashMap<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>>();
            Collection<SmartListenerConfig> configsForClass = new ArrayList<SmartListenerConfig>();
            configsForClass.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
            configs.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configsForClass);
            return configs;
        }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>    9. Specify the validation rule’s implementation property value - a qualified name of the class</p><p>    10. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite</p><p><br /></p><p>See the <em>MyOCLBasedValidationRuleImpl.java</em> example in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation</em> directory.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32f9080c-2334-45c1-b680-ee01eae87bd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The implementation class must be in the classpath of the modeling tool or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binary validation rule in the plugin" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437681 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00322: (2026x) Creating a custom dependency matrix type

- page_id: `254437681`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437681/2026x+Creating+a+custom+dependency+matrix+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:52.129+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a custom dependency matrix type:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a custom dependency matrix type' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a custom dependency matrix type' space=''] .

###### **creatingCreatin****g a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugins' space=''] for custom dependency matrix type**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class to register components of the new dependency matrix type as shown in the following example:

```java

```

Remember the name of the new dependency matrix type that you pass as an argument while creating a new instance of the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class (in the preceding example, it is “Sample Extended Matrix”). You will have to specify the exact name when registering a new diagram type.

The sample of more detailed configuration code is provided in *<program installation folder>\openapi\examples\dependencymatrix*.

###### **registeringRegisterin****g a new dependency matrix type in UI**

Once the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugins' space='']is created, you have to register the new dependency matrix type by using the **Customize****Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog. Only then it will be added to program toolbars and menus.

To register a new dependency matrix type

1. Start a modeling tool, for example, MagicDraw.
2. On the Diagrams menu, click Customize . The Customize Diagrams dialog opens.
3. Click Create and then select Dependency Matrix Type . The Customize Dependency Matrix W izard dialog opens.
4. In the Type box, specify the name of the new dependency matrix type. The value must be the same as the one passed to the DependencyMatrixConfigurator class as the argument. [ATTACHMENT filename='CustomizeMatrixWizard.png']

*Specifying name of new Dependency Matrix type*

5. In the **Abbreviation**box, specify the abbreviation of the new dependency matrix type.

6. Click **Finish**.

7. Restart your modeling tool.

You can now create a custom dependency matrix.

For more information on how to customize a new dependency matrix type by using the **Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog, **see “Creating New Dependency Matrix Type” in theMagicDraw UMLProfiling&DSL UserGuide.pdf.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

    {

        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f58ccb-07d4-45bb-846c-efc41fe67055"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Remember the name of the new dependency matrix type that you pass as an argument while creating a new instance of the <em>DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class (in the preceding example, it is “Sample Extended Matrix”). Only then it will be added to program toolbars and menus.</p><p>To register a new dependency matrix type</p><hr /><ol><li>Start a modeling tool, for example, MagicDraw.</li><li>On the <strong>Diagrams </strong>menu, click <strong>Customize</strong>. The <strong>Customize Diagrams </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Create </strong>and then select <strong>Dependency Matrix Type</strong>. The <strong>Customize Dependency Matrix </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">W</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">izard </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Type </strong>box, specify the name of the new dependency matrix type. The value must be the same as the one passed to the <em>DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class as the argument.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="CustomizeMatrixWizard.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating a custom dependency matrix type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Specifying name of new Dependency Matrix type</em></p><p>    5. In the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Abbreviation </strong>box, specify the abbreviation of the new dependency matrix type.</p><p>    6. ## PAGE 00323: (2026x) Creating a diagram

- page_id: `254437688`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437688/2026x+Creating+a+diagram

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every diagram has a diagram type and this type should be provided during diagram creation.

An example shows how to create and add a diagram to a parent element:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><div>Every diagram has a diagram type and this type should be provided during diagram creation.</div><div>An example shows how to create and add a diagram to a parent element:</div><div><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7545b1a6-1ac5-499e-9b55-35faef700a9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Package parent = project.getPrimaryModel();
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Create a diagram");
    try
    {
        //a class diagram is created and added to a parent model element
        Diagram diagram = ModelElementsManager.getInstance().createDiagram(DiagramTypeConstants.UML_CLASS_DIAGRAM, parent);
        //open a diagram 
        project.getDiagram(diagram).open();
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7849a87-66ab-4f1a-96c8-df51caad3ac7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00324: (2026x) Creating a new execution engine

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating an execution engine, you are required to implement three abstract methods in the Subclass of ExecutionEngine (see JavaDoc for more details). These abstract methods are as follows

```text

```

API developers can insert any desired code into the above abstract methods and get the events when the execution engine is initialized, executed, and terminated. 
 You can execute the following code to create a MyExecutionEngine class

```text

```

The created class MyExecutionEngine must be returned from createEngine() of its descriptor as defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating an execution engine descriptor' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating an execution engine, you are required to implement three abstract methods in the Subclass of ExecutionEngine (see JavaDoc for more details). These abstract methods are as follows<br /> <span style="color: rgb(153,51,102);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6dab922e-1f3f-47d9-a939-2d7f2e34ae4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public abstract class ExecutionEngine {
		...
		public abstract void init(Element element);
		public abstract void execute(Element element);
		public abstract void onClose();
		...
	} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> API developers can insert any desired code into the above abstract methods and get the events when the execution engine is initialized, executed, and terminated. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can execute the following code to create a MyExecutionEngine class</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5c36737-bb57-4b71-b927-fe7663c85a59"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyExecutionEngine extends ExecutionEngine {

		public MyExecutionEngine(ExecutionEngineDescriptor engineDescriptor) {
			super(engineDescriptor);
		} 
	
		@Override
		public void execute(Element element) {
		...
		} 
		
		@Override
		public void init(Element element) {
			Project project = Project.getProject(element);
			// add engine listener if needed
			EngineListener listener = new MyEngineListener(project);
			addEngineListener(listener);
		} 

		@Override
		public void onClose() {
		...
		}
	} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />The created class MyExecutionEngine must be returned from createEngine() of its descriptor as defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating an execution engine descriptor" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437149 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00325: (2026x) Creating an execution engine descriptor

- page_id: `254437149`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437149/2026x+Creating+an+execution+engine+descriptor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:08.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows how to create an Execution Engine Descriptor

```text
getValidationSuite();
	}]]>
```

The new execution engine descriptor must implement the ExecutionEngineDescriptor or extend the AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor abstract Class. You can find details about these Classes in JavaDoc.

```text

```

The created execution engine descriptor must then be registered to the simulation manager (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager' space='']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b5f501d-cf79-4fde-bd45-cfea051e77b6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2986d1da-593f-473f-88bf-adc22be60bc1">The following example shows how to create an Execution Engine Descriptor</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1717ad49-21fb-46b4-9eef-5f836bc57e86" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public interface ExecutionEngineDescriptor {
		String getEngineName();
		ImageIcon getEngineIcon();
		boolean canExecute(Element element);
		ExecutionEngine createEngine();
		boolean canAnimate(PresentationElement element);
		boolean isAutoDiagramOpened();
		boolean canDebug();
		boolean canUserTriggerEvents();
		boolean isDiagramPerSession();
		boolean isHasIdle();
		boolean isFindEngine(Element element);
		List<? Extends ValidationSuite> getValidationSuite();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The new execution engine descriptor must implement the ExecutionEngineDescriptor or extend the AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor abstract Class. You can find details about these Classes in JavaDoc.<br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="300849dc-2a03-4030-8de9-bc925d475c22" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyExecutionEngineDescriptor extends AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor {
	@Override
	
	public boolean canExecute(Element element) {
		return element instanceof Interaction;
	} 
	
	@Override
	public ExecutionEngine createEngine() {
		return new MyExecutionEngine(this);
	} 

	@Override
	public String getEngineName() {
		return "My Execution Engine";
}
} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> The created execution engine descriptor must then be registered to the simulation manager (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager" /></ac:link>).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437167 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00326: (2026x) Creating an execution engine listener

- page_id: `254437167`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437167/2026x+Creating+an+execution+engine+listener
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:09.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows how to create an engine listener

```text
values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Activate Element-- : element name = " ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Deactivate Element-- : element name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void eventTriggered(String eventID) {
		NamedElement element = (NamedElement)project.getElementByID(eventID);
		System.out.printIn("--Event Trigger-- : event name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void executionTerminated() {
		System.out.printIn("--Engine Terminated--");
	}
} ]]>
```

Once you have created an EngineListener, you can register it to the specified ExecutionEngine if you want to receive events occurring in each execution engine. All engine listeners of a specific engine will be activated under the conditions as follows

- An element is activated.
- An element is deactivated.
- An event is triggered.
- An engine is terminated.

```text
values); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values); 
		void eventTriggered(String eventID); 
		void executionTerminated();	
	}]]>
```

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a new execution engine' space=''] for more information about adding execution engine listeners to the *MyExecutionEngine* class.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can add more than one execution engine listener to an execution engine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following example shows how to create an engine listener<br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a808a382-6c31-4dd6-aeb3-054feb1defd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyEngineListener implements EngineListener {
	
	private Project project; 
		public MyEngineListener(Project project) {
		this.project = project;
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementActivated(Element element, Collection<?> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Activate Element-- : element name = " ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection<?> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Deactivate Element-- : element name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void eventTriggered(String eventID) {
		NamedElement element = (NamedElement)project.getElementByID(eventID);
		System.out.printIn("--Event Trigger-- : event name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void executionTerminated() {
		System.out.printIn("--Engine Terminated--");
	}
} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> Once you have created an EngineListener, you can register it to the specified ExecutionEngine if you want to receive events occurring in each execution engine. All engine listeners of a specific engine will be activated under the conditions as follows</p><ul><li>An element is activated.</li><li>An element is deactivated.</li><li>An event is triggered.</li><li>An engine is terminated.</li></ul><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68658132-16ea-476d-8dc7-54965276de92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public interface EngineListener { 
		void elementActivated(Element element, Collection<?> values); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection<?> values); 
		void eventTriggered(String eventID); 
		void executionTerminated();	
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating a new execution engine" /></ac:link> for more information about adding execution engine listeners to the <em>MyExecutionEngine</em> class.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3f3f95e-b48a-4621-8a08-e79f342933b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can add more than one execution engine listener to an execution engine.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00327: (2026x) Creating and registering a new SimulationExecutionListener

- page_id: `254437147`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437147/2026x+Creating+and+registering+a+new+SimulationExecutionListener
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:08.870+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Acom.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecutionListener is a listener for various events that can occur during execution of a model.

```text
values, ActivationContext context); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values, ActivationContext context); 
		void eventTriggered(SignalInstance signal); 
		void operationCalled(Operation operation, List parameterValues, Object_ caller, Object_ target, boolean isSynchronous); 
		void behaviorCalled(Behavior behavior, List parameterValues, Object_ caller, Object_ target, boolean isSynchronous); 
		void objectCreated(Object_ sender, Object_ object); 
        void beforeObjectDestroyed(Object_ object)
        void valueChange(StructuredValue context, FeatureValue feature, Object oldValue, Object newValue)
        void objectStateActivated(StructuredValue context, State newState) 
		void busyStatusChange(StructuredValue context, Object oldValue, Object newValue);
    }]]>
```

To register a created execution listener to a global list, that ensures this listener to be added to each created SimulationExecution, use the following code:

```text

```

If the SimulationExecution is already created, listener can be registered directly to that specific execution by calling the following:

| Open API | Function |
| --- | --- |
| SimulationManager.registerSimulationExecutionListener(SimulationExecutionListener listener, SimulationExecution execution) | Registers SimulationExecutionListener dynamically during the execution. |
| SimulationManager.unregisterSimulationExecutionListener(SimulationExecutionListener listener, SimulationExecution execution) | Unregisters SimulationExecutionListener. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437476 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00328: (2026x) Creating custom drag and drop handlers

- page_id: `254437476`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437476/2026x+Creating+custom+drag+and+drop+handlers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:33.922+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw itself has many default drag and drop handlers for doing various things. Such handlers consist of :

- Dropping elements from various UI components(trees, specification windows, etc.) diagram or its presentation elements.
- Dropping external application items onto trees or diagrams.

Default drag and drop handlers for example create new presentation elements, change the properties of elements, external application image drop on diagram creates an image and etc. Same is true if presentation elements are dropped on top of other presentation elements.

API allows to register a custom drag and drop handler for doing some specific things using drag and drop mechanism.

OpenAPI drag and drop handler registrations:

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.register( com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping elements from browser(trees) into diagram.
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler-) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping one presentation element on other presentation element in the same diagram
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the browser tree nodes(elements).
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the diagram.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory> \openapi\examples\customdraganddrop

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler-">)</a></em> to register a factory
