<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437694 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00343: (2026x) Custom diagram painters

- page_id: `254437694`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437694/2026x+Custom+diagram+painters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:52.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams
- labels: ['diagram-surface']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.

The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface*. Only the opened diagram has *DiagramSurface*. A closed diagram returns *null*.

###### Example of the code:

```java

```

```text

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc78d5d3-78d1-479e-a45e-3b1a6bab7f5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface</em>. Only the opened diagram has <em>DiagramSurface</em>. A closed diagram returns <em>null</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example of the code:</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="223a2e8d-b1dd-4546-80f1-c344a7640e85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Application.getInstance().addProjectEventListener(new ProjectEventListenerAdapter()
{
   public void projectOpened(Project project)
   {
      project.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
      {
         public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
         {
            if(evt.getPropertyName().equals(Project.DIAGRAM_OPENED))
            {
               DiagramPresentationElement diagram = Application.getInstance().getProject().getActiveDiagram();
               diagram.getDiagramSurface().addPainter(new DiagramSurfacePainter()
               {
                  public void paint(Graphics g, DiagramPresentationElement diagram)
                  {
                     g.setColor(Color.BLUE);
                     List symbols = diagram.getPresentationElements();
                     for (int i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++)
                     {
                        PresentationElement o = (PresentationElement)symbols.get(i);
                        if( o instanceof ShapeElement)
                        {
                           Rectangle bounds = o.getBounds();
                           bounds.grow(5,5);
                           ((Graphics2D)g).draw(bounds);
                        }

                     }

                  };

               });

            }

         }

      });

   }

});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>    </pre></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437265 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00344: (2026x) Custom elements numbering

- page_id: `254437265`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437265/2026x+Custom+elements+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:16.973+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The generic numbering open API provides two interfaces of different complexity. They allow a user to modify how the numbering process executes.

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) IJavaNumberPart interface' space=''] interface produces a single part of a number generated by the framework. It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) INumberingAction interface' space=''] allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.

Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Numbering Scheme property values' space='MT']).

These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.</li><li>The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) INumberingAction interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[INumberingAction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.</li></ul><p>Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Numbering Scheme property values" ri:space-key="MT" /></ac:link>).<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><p>These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="918a5c6d-e796-417e-ae7a-a2c46c77d972"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="845f50c5-47fd-4d7e-b95e-17c259663b9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437400 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00345: (2026x) Custom Legend Item Implementation

- page_id: `254437400`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437400/2026x+Custom+Legend+Item+Implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:25.981+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to create Java implementation describing what model element or presentation element should a Legend Item adorn in a diagram.

1. The Java class should be implemented following the very same rules as when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Implementing a binary validation rule' space=''] . Each com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).
2. Specify the full class name as the value of "Implementation" property for the Legend Item in the modeling application.
3. Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.
4. Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.

#### TIP: Example

Example

See *AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java* example implementation in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\legendItem*directory.

Also see: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Each <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation</em> object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).</li><li>Specify the full class name as the value of &quot;Implementation&quot; property for the Legend Item in the modeling application. </li><li>Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.</li><li>Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6964f9b3-6d3c-4d75-bec5-da99cd79962f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>See <em>AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java</em> example implementation in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\legendItem </em>directory.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Also see: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437278 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00346: (2026x) Custom presentation elements rendering

- page_id: `254437278`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437278/2026x+Custom+presentation+elements+rendering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:18.048+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The custom symbol rendering API allows change a default default symbol view. This API includes:

1. Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.
2. Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.

In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

This API includes:</p><ol><li>Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.</li><li>Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.</li></ol><p>In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5400cec7-8f81-40d0-809c-38f6bac028a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="05088e21-c8f3-4865-8c3f-35850941b106"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437560 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00347: (2026x) Custom profile implementation

- page_id: `254437560`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437560/2026x+Custom+profile+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:44.029+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with stereotypes and tagged values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of using low level *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper* API to access information about profile elements, it is possible to generate a profile implementation class out of the UML Profile model. This implementation class wraps all mapping, naming details with a nice Java API. MagicDraw comes with already generated profiles implementation for standard profiles. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Standard profiles implementation' space=''] page for details.

To create your own custom profile implementation you need to follow these steps

1. Install Development Tools plugin from Help->Resource Manager
2. Open UML Profile model with MagicDraw.
3. Generate profile implementation code using Tools->Development Tools->Generate Profile Class Implementation (wrappers) ...

Profile implementation class has these features

- There is a constant defined for every DataType, Enumeration name
- There is a getter for every DataType element defined in the profile
- Java enumeration is generated for every Enumeration element defined in the profile. This class has following features
  - There are methods to get enumeration literals by generated enumeration and vice versa
- Inner java class is generated for every Stereotype defined in the profile. This class has following features
  - There is a constant defined for Stereotype name
  - There are constants defined for all stereotype tags names
  - There is a setter/getter method available for every stereotype tag. Signature of these methods corresponds to tag multiplicity type and etc. No more need to work with strings and casting values to primitives as in StereotypesHelper case.
  - There is a check method to check if Element has specific stereotype applied
  - There is a getter for Stereotype element itself

It is highly recommended to assign URI to Profile element in the UML model before generation. URI is an unique Profile identifier and helps to avoid conflicts if model has several profiles with same name applied.

**Custom sample profile**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Usages example**

```java

```

```text
Generated implementation class for this custom profile
```

```java
value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearFriends(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      public void addFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         Profiles.addValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void removeFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         java.util.List values = getFriends(element);
         values.remove(value);
         setFriends(element, values);
      }

      @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
      public java.util.List getFriends(Element element)
      {
         return (java.util.List) Profiles.getValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      @Override
      protected void clear()
      {
         super.clear();
         country = null;
         friends = null;
      }

      @Override
      public boolean is(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         return element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor &&
               _p.isTypeOf(element, getStereotype());
      }

      public static boolean isInstance(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor)
         {
            CustomProfile instance = getInstance(element);
            return instance.isTypeOf(element, instance.person().getStereotype());
         }
         return false;
      }
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection generatedGetAllElementWrappers()
   {
      Collection wrappers = new ArrayList<>();
      wrappers.add(personStereotype);
      return wrappers;
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection generatedGetAllStereotypes()
   {
      if (getProfile() != null)
      {
         final Collection stereotypes = new HashSet<>();
         stereotypes.add(personStereotype.getStereotype());
         return stereotypes;
      }
      return Collections.emptyList();
   }
}]]>
```

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Standard profiles implementation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Instead of using low level <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper</em> API to access information about profile elements, it is possible to generate a profile implementation class out of the UML Profile model. This implementation class wraps all mapping, naming details with a nice Java API. MagicDraw comes with already generated profiles implementation for standard profiles. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Standard profiles implementation" /></ac:link> page for details.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create your own custom profile implementation you need to follow these steps</p><ol><li>Install Development Tools plugin from Help-&gt;Resource Manager</li><li>Open UML Profile model with MagicDraw. </li><li>Generate profile implementation code using Tools-&gt;Development Tools-&gt;Generate Profile Class Implementation (wrappers) ...</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Profile implementation class has these features</p><ul><li>There is a constant defined for every DataType, Enumeration name</li><li>There is a getter for every DataType element defined in the profile</li><li>Java enumeration is generated for every Enumeration element defined in the profile. This class has following features<ul><li>There are methods to get enumeration literals by generated enumeration and vice versa</li></ul></li><li>Inner java class is generated for every Stereotype defined in the profile. This class has following features<br /><ul><li>There is a constant defined for Stereotype name</li><li>There are constants defined for all stereotype tags names</li><li>There is a setter/getter method available for every stereotype tag. Signature of these methods corresponds to tag multiplicity type and etc. No more need to work with strings and casting values to primitives as in <em>StereotypesHelper</em> case.</li><li>There is a check method to check if Element has specific stereotype applied</li><li>There is a getter for Stereotype element itself</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dce80e5-9c6e-41b1-b514-60eaa771f2dc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is highly recommended to assign URI to Profile element in the UML model before generation. URI is an unique Profile identifier and helps to avoid conflicts if model has several profiles with same name applied.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Custom sample profile</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2015-7-1 11:26:9.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom profile implementation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Usages example</strong></p><div><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76f1e3c4-f8cd-4155-8c47-5be8016f1e78"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;
CustomProfile customProfile = CustomProfile.getInstance(element);
//check if element has Person stereotype applied
customProfile.person().is(element);
//get Person.country tag value
String country = customProfile.person().getCountry(element);
//set Person.country tag value
customProfile.person().setCountry(element, "US");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><pre><strong>Generated implementation class for this custom profile</strong></pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81a5c4b5-6d3a-4665-84b9-2944d60941d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml2.Profiles;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfileCache;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfileImplementation;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfilesBridge;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Property;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashSet;

@SuppressWarnings("WeakerAccess, unused")
public class CustomProfile extends ProfileImplementation
{
   public static final String PROFILE_URI = "http://custom.com/custom_profile";

   public static final String PROFILE_NAME = "CustomProfile";

   private final PersonStereotype personStereotype;

   public static CustomProfile getInstance(BaseElement baseElement)
   {
      CustomProfile profile = ProfilesBridge.getProfile(CustomProfile.class, baseElement);
      if (profile == null)
      {
         return ProfilesBridge.createProfile(CustomProfile.class, baseElement, CustomProfile::new, PROFILE_NAME, PROFILE_URI);
      }
      return profile;
   }

   public CustomProfile(ProfileCache cache)
   {
      super(cache);
      personStereotype = new PersonStereotype(this);

   }

   public PersonStereotype person()
   {
      return personStereotype;
   }

   public static class PersonStereotype extends StereotypeWrapper
   {

      //stereotype Person and its tags
      public static final String STEREOTYPE_NAME = "Person";
      public static final String COUNTRY = "country";
      public static final String FRIENDS = "friends";

      private final CustomProfile _p;
      @CheckForNull
      private Property country;
      @CheckForNull
      private Property friends;

      protected PersonStereotype(CustomProfile profile)
      {
         super(profile);
         _p = profile;
      }

      @Override
      @SuppressWarnings("ConstantConditions")
      public Stereotype getStereotype()
      {
         return getElementByName(STEREOTYPE_NAME);
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public Property getCountryProperty()
      {
         if (country == null)
         {
            country = getTagByName(getStereotype(), COUNTRY);
         }
         return country;
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public Property getFriendsProperty()
      {
         if (friends == null)
         {
            friends = getTagByName(getStereotype(), FRIENDS);
         }
         return friends;
      }

      public void setCountry(Element element, @CheckForNull String value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getCountryProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearCountry(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getCountryProperty());
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public String getCountry(Element element)
      {
         return toString(Profiles.getFirstValue(element, getCountryProperty()));
      }

      public void setFriends(Element element, @CheckForNull java.util.Collection<String> value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearFriends(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      public void addFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         Profiles.addValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void removeFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         java.util.List<String> values = getFriends(element);
         values.remove(value);
         setFriends(element, values);
      }

      @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
      public java.util.List<String> getFriends(Element element)
      {
         return (java.util.List<String>) Profiles.getValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      @Override
      protected void clear()
      {
         super.clear();
         country = null;
         friends = null;
      }

      @Override
      public boolean is(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         return element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor &&
               _p.isTypeOf(element, getStereotype());
      }

      public static boolean isInstance(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor)
         {
            CustomProfile instance = getInstance(element);
            return instance.isTypeOf(element, instance.person().getStereotype());
         }
         return false;
      }
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection<ProfileElementWrapper> generatedGetAllElementWrappers()
   {
      Collection<ProfileElementWrapper> wrappers = new ArrayList<>();
      wrappers.add(personStereotype);
      return wrappers;
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection<Stereotype> generatedGetAllStereotypes()
   {
      if (getProfile() != null)
      {
         final Collection<Stereotype> stereotypes = new HashSet<>();
         stereotypes.add(personStereotype.getStereotype());
         return stereotypes;
      }
      return Collections.emptyList();
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c183a4e-7197-4e37-ba4d-c9762ce8158c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Standard profiles implementation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437280 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00348: (2026x) Custom renderer provider

- page_id: `254437280`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437280/2026x+Custom+renderer+provider
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:18.236+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: ['presentation-element-render-provider', 'presentation-element-renderer-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererProvider* is an object, which provides a custom symbol renderer for a given presentation element.

*PresentationElementRendererProvider.getRenderer(**PresentationElement)* method provides a specific renderer for the given *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement* (Symbol) or null.

To make your provider start working, you must register it into the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager*using:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererProvider</em> is an object, which provides a custom symbol renderer for a given presentation element. </p><p><em>PresentationElementRendererProvider.getRenderer(</em><em>PresentationElement)</em> method provides a specific renderer for the given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em> (Symbol) or null.</p><p>To make your provider start working, you must register it into the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager </em>using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e41b4cdd-300e-44b8-83fc-143bc79ebb89"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[PresentationElementRendererManager.getInstance().addProvider(new RendererProvider());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437307 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00349: (2026x) Custom renderers sample

- page_id: `254437307`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437307/2026x+Custom+renderers+sample
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:20.242+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This example covers:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom renderers sample' space=''] The package without inner elements is filled with a green color.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom renderers sample' space=''] The slot values are rounded.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom renderers sample' space=''] The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends.

```text

```

This example shows, how to create custom renderers for the preceding pointed symbols.

```text

```

**The custom package rendererThe custom package renderer.**The empty package (that is without inner elements) is filled with green color:

```java

```

**The custom slot rendererThe custom slot renderer (used in the instance specification symbol).**The slot values are rounded:

```java
values = slot.getValue();
                        for (ValueSpecification value : values)
                        {
                            if (value instanceof LiteralString)
                            {
                                LiteralString literalString = (LiteralString) value;
                                if (string.length() > 0)
                                {
                                   string += "; ";

                                }
                                String literalValue = literalString.getValue();
                                try
                                {
                                    // round a value
                                    double doubleValue = Double.parseDouble(literalValue); 
                                    double rounded = Math.round(doubleValue * 100) / 100; 
                                    literalValue = Double.toString(rounded);
                                }
                                catch (NumberFormatException e)
                                {
                                }
                                string += literalValue;
                            }
                        }
                        return slot.getDefiningFeature().getName() + "=" + string;
                    }
                }
            }
            return super.getText(presentationElement, textEnum);
        }
    }]]>
```

**The custom dependency link rendererThe custom dependency link renderer.**The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>This example covers:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom package renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the package.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom renderers sample" /></ac:link> </strong>The package without inner elements is filled with a green color.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom slot renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the slot.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom renderers sample" /></ac:link> </strong>The slot values are rounded.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom dependency link renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the dependency link.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom renderers sample" /></ac:link> </strong>The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends.</li></ul><div class="WordSection8"><pre> </pre><p>This example shows, how to create custom renderers for the preceding pointed symbols.</p><pre><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></pre><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="397ce5c4-bcbc-4d41-95af-cef715ca7ce5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom package renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom package renderer. </strong>The empty package (that is without inner elements) is filled with green color:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be4a4052-0b22-4a8d-82d9-666a84f7689c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class PackageRenderer extends ShapeRenderer
    {
        public Color getColor(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementColorEnum colorEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementColorEnum.FILL.equals(colorEnum))
            {
                // the color to fill
                Element element = presentationElement.getElement();
                if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package && 
                    !((com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package) element).hasOwnedElement())
                {
                    // the package has no elements, use the green color to fill 
                    return Color.GREEN;
                }
            }
            return super.getColor(presentationElement, colorEnum);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad861a70-cfba-48c7-8f42-1e2ef552c0df"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom slot renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom slot renderer (used in the instance specification symbol). </strong>The slot values are rounded:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b629cb6d-b54a-4f8e-b68a-2e1114e57727"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class SlotRenderer extends ShapeRenderer
    {
        public String getText(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementTextEnum textEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementTextEnum.NAME.equals(textEnum))
            {
                // the slot text is shown as a name
                Element element = presentationElement.getElement();
                if (element instanceof Slot)
                {
                    Slot slot = (Slot) element;
                    if (slot.hasValue())
                    {
                        String string = "";
                        List<ValueSpecification> values = slot.getValue();
                        for (ValueSpecification value : values)
                        {
                            if (value instanceof LiteralString)
                            {
                                LiteralString literalString = (LiteralString) value;
                                if (string.length() > 0)
                                {
                                   string += "; ";

                                }
                                String literalValue = literalString.getValue();
                                try
                                {
                                    // round a value
                                    double doubleValue = Double.parseDouble(literalValue); 
                                    double rounded = Math.round(doubleValue * 100) / 100; 
                                    literalValue = Double.toString(rounded);
                                }
                                catch (NumberFormatException e)
                                {
                                }
                                string += literalValue;
                            }
                        }
                        return slot.getDefiningFeature().getName() + "=" + string;
                    }
                }
            }
            return super.getText(presentationElement, textEnum);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09575b30-840e-4689-aeec-e5d212c2e471"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom dependency link renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom dependency link renderer. </strong>The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="817c14d2-f29c-4c12-a37b-0098e2d0b912"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class DependencyRenderer extends PathRenderer
    {
        private PathEndRenderer mClientEndRenderer; 
        DependencyRenderer()
        {
            // a custom client end renderer, use a filled circle at the end
            mClientEndRenderer = new PathEndRenderer(PathEndAdornment.CIRCLE, PathEndAdornmentModifier.FILLED);
        }
 
        public Color getColor(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementColorEnum colorEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementColorEnum.LINE.equals(colorEnum))
            {
                // use a blue color for a line 
                return Color.BLUE;
            }
            return super.getColor(presentationElement, colorEnum);
        }


        protected PathEndRenderer getClientEndRenderer(PathElement pathElement)
        {
            // use a custom end renderer 
            return mClientEndRenderer;
        }
 
        public int getLineWidth(PresentationElement presentationElement)
        {
            // a line width is 2 
            return 2;
        }
 
        protected void drawPathAdornment(Graphics g, PathElement pathElement)
        {
            super.drawPathAdornment(g, pathElement);
 
            // draw a circle at the middle of the dependency line
            Color background = Color.WHITE;
            Property property = pathElement.getDiagramPresentationElement().getProperty(PropertyID.DIAGRAM_BACKGROUND_COLOR);
            if (property != null)
            {
                Object value = property.getValue();
                if (value instanceof Color)
                {
                    background = (Color) value;
                }
            }
            Point middlePoint = pathElement.getMiddlePoint();
            int diameter = 10;
            int radius = diameter / 2;
            int x = middlePoint.x - radius;
            int y = middlePoint.y - radius;
            Color color = g.getColor(); 
            g.setColor(background);
            g.fillOval(x, y, diameter, diameter);
            g.setColor(color);
            g.drawOval(x, y, diameter, diameter);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437317 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00351: (2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix

- page_id: `254437317`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437317/2026x+Customizing+a+dependency+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:21.234+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The topic explains how to configure the following components of the dependency matrix:

- Command bar
- Shortcut keys
- Shortcut menus of the row or column header element, cell, and filter panel
- Cell entries (dependencies)
- Appearance of cells and headers of rows and columns

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*Components of dependency matrix pane*

The names of the components differ in other documentation as follows:

- “Toolbar” is used for “Command bar”
- “ Criteria area” is used for “Filter panel”
- “Row elements” is used for “Row header elements”

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The topic explains how to configure the following components of the dependency matrix:</p><ul><li>Command bar</li><li>Shortcut keys</li><li>Shortcut menus of the row or column header element, cell, and filter panel</li><li>Cell entries (dependencies)</li><li>Appearance of cells and headers of rows and columns</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dep_matrix_components.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><em>Components of dependency matrix pane</em></p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="549ce9c7-9c94-48f3-9a30-65c1f6df960c"><ac:rich-text-body><p align="center" style="text-align: left;">The names of the components differ in other documentation as follows:</p><ul><li>“Toolbar” is used for “Command bar”</li><li>“<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Criteria </strong>area” is used for “Filter panel”</li><li>“Row elements” is used for “Row header elements”</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ab2505-7714-456b-bfb3-1934ea08df7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a8a2e96a-7687-479a-b58c-75dad9215cdf"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437103 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: (2026x) Customizing double-click behavior

- page_id: `254437103`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437103/2026x+Customizing+double-click+behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:00.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default application behavior on a double-click on the element is to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] or open a diagram. This behavior can be changed by registering custom element activators.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivationManager.addActivator(ElementActivator[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ElementActivationManager.html#addActivator-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivator-)* for that purpose.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The default application behavior on a double-click on the element is to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> or open a diagram. This behavior can be changed by registering custom element activators.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivationManager.addActivator(ElementActivator<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ElementActivationManager.html#addActivator-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivator-">)</a></em> for that purpose.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437305 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: (2026x) Customizing templates

- page_id: `254437305`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437305/2026x+Customizing+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:20.036+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information.

We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in <*program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template*folder. The template file can be changed in the **CG Properties Editor**dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the **Properties**command).

When generating DDL, objects are passed from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Code engineering set' space=''] to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:

| Object name | Object type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| $CESList | List<NamedEleme nt> | The list of model elements that are added to the . |
| $dropRequired | boolean | Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement. |
| $oracleHelper | Java.lang.Class | This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information. |
| $newLineBracket | boolean | The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line. |
| $genDocumentation | boolean | The language option for the documentation generation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information. </p><p>We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in &lt;<em>program installation folder&gt;\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder. The template file can be changed in the <strong>CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Properties </strong>command).</p><p>When generating DDL, objects are passed from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:</p><table><tbody><tr><th><strong>Object name</strong></th><th><strong>Object type</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td><pre>$CESList</pre></td><td><pre>List&lt;NamedEleme nt&gt;</pre></td><td><span>The list of model elements that are added to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$dropRequired</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$oracleHelper</pre></td><td><pre>Java.lang.Class</pre></td><td><span>This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$newLineBracket</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$genDocumentation</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The language option for the documentation generation.</span></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00354: (2026x) Deleting presentation elements

- page_id: `254437252`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the given *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement* from the diagram, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.deletePresentationElement(**PresentationElement)*:

```java

```

All related presentation elements with all children and connected paths will be removed too.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove the given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em> from the diagram, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.deletePresentationElement(</em><em>PresentationElement)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fac020d6-9cfa-4b60-8e68-3b1eea64df87"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement element = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test"); 
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().removePresentationElement(element); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>All related presentation elements with all children and connected paths will be removed too.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21356239-0c67-43fd-9dd9-2ffb96badd06"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437453 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: (2026x) Developing plugins using IDE

- page_id: `254437453`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437453/2026x+Developing+plugins+using+IDE
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:32.393+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins
- labels: ['eclpise', 'intellij']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins extending your modeling tool can be effectively developed using popular IDEs (Integrated Development Environment) such as Eclipse, IntelliJ, and others. Developers may use theirs favorite IDE for an agile modeling tool plugin coding, building, testing, and debugging. This chapter describes the plugin development in Eclipse ([www.eclipse.org](http://www.eclipse.org/)) and IntelliJ IDEA ([www.jetbrains.com](http://www.jetbrains.com/)) IDEs.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Plugins extending your modeling tool can be effectively developed using popular IDEs (Integrated Development Environment) such as Eclipse, IntelliJ, and others. Developers may use theirs favorite IDE for an agile modeling tool plugin coding, building, testing, and debugging. This chapter describes the plugin development in Eclipse (<a href="http://www.eclipse.org/">www.eclipse.org</a>) and IntelliJ IDEA (<a href="http://www.jetbrains.com/">www.jetbrains.com</a>) IDEs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91d26bab-dc7b-4c36-9466-26315846dc64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="92ac631c-7363-4f6c-baa3-80257d3a1295"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437461 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00358: (2026x) Development in Eclipse

- page_id: `254437461`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437461/2026x+Development+in+Eclipse
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:33.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Developing plugins using IDE
- labels: ['setup-eclipse-environment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The pre-configured Eclipse projects for two sample plugins and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] (command-line) tool are provided with a program installation. These projects can be found in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip*.

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.

To setup the Eclipse environment for the modeling tool development

1. Import java projects from <*modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip*into your Eclipse Workspace: 
1.1. In the main menu, click **File** > **Import**. The **Import** dialog opens. 
1.2. In the **Select an import wizard** list, select**General**> **Existing Projects into Workspace**and click**Next**. 
1.3. Copy the java projects zip location to the **Select archive file** box or click **Browse** and browse to the zip file. The project list appears in the **Projects** box. 
1.4. Select projects you need and click **Finish.** [ATTACHMENT filename='import_projects.png']
2. Open the **Package Explorer** view, expand the MagicDraw project and edit the MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY link: 
2.1. Right-click the link and select**Properties**. 
2.2. On the left side of the opened dialog, select **Resource**and click the **Edit**button on the right side of the dialog. The **Edit Link Location**dialog opens. 
2.3. Select the location and click **OK**. Be sure the link points to your MagicDraw (or other according to a modeling tool you are using) installation directory (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Make sure that precisely Java 17 version is used by the **MagicDraw** project in your Eclipse IDE: 
3.1. Right-click the **MagicDraw** project and select**Properties**. 
3.2. On the left side of the open dialog, select **Java Build Path** and then select **Libraries** tab on the right. 
3.3. Double-click **JRE System Library [JavaSE-17]**. 
3.4. Click **Installed JREs** and check if Java 17 is available here. If there is no Java 17, add it. *Java 17 version must be added, because later versions may not be supported by our tool.* 3.5. Click **Apply and Close**. 3.6. Select **Execution environment** check box and in the combo box, select **JavaSE-17**. 3.7. C lick **Environments** button. 
3.8. In the **Execution Environments** list, select **JavaSE-17** and in the **Compatible JREs** list, select Java 17. 
3.9.Click **Apply and Close** and then **Finish** buttons. 
3.10.Select **Order and Export** tab, click **Select All** button to select all check boxes. 
3.11. Click**Apply and Close**.

Eclipse Workspace is ready for the source code development and running/debugging.

To use one of the prepared launch configurations

- On the main menu, click Run > Run Configurations... (or Debug Configurations... ). The Run Configurations dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the MagicDraw (or other according to a modeling tool you are using ) installation directory ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Development in Eclipse' space='']) and two plugins from the Eclipse Workspace. Thus, if the *md.plugins.dir* java system property is not defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugins directories' space='']), developing plugins are not loaded.

- MagicDraw with all plugins launches MagicDraw (or other modeling tool) with plugins available in the IDE workspace namely My Plugin 1 and My Plugin 2 .
- My Commandline launches batch mode tool My Commandline .
- MyPlugin1JUnit<n>Test launches sample JUnit<n> test.

The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin. 
For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path. 
For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies' space=''] page.

When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar filesthat are required by your pluginfrom appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib* and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins* and its sub directories.

Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about the runtime plugin *.jar* file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this *.jar* file to a plugin directory while the plugin is developed under Eclipse.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These projects can be found in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip</em>.</p><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To setup the Eclipse environment for the modeling tool development</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Import java projects from &lt;<em style="line-height: 1.42857;">modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip </em>into your Eclipse Workspace:<br />1.1. Click <strong>Apply and Close</strong>.</span><br /><span>3.6. Select <strong>Execution environment</strong> check box and in the combo box, select <strong>JavaSE-17</strong>.<br /></span><span>3.7. C</span><span><span><span>lick <strong>Environments</strong> button.</span><br /><span><span><span>3.8. In the <strong>Execution Environments</strong> list, s</span>elect <strong>JavaSE-17</strong> and in the <strong>Compatible JREs</strong> list, select Java 17.</span></span><br /><span><span><span>3.9. </span>Click <strong>Apply and Close</strong> and then <strong>Finish</strong> buttons.</span></span><br /><span><span>3.10. </span>Select <strong>Order and Export</strong> tab, click <strong>Select All</strong> button to select all check boxes.<br />3.11. Click</span><span><span><span> <strong>Apply and Close</strong>.</span><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Eclipse Workspace is ready for the source code development and running/debugging.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use one of the prepared launch configurations</p><hr /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run Configurations...</strong> (or <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Debug Configurations...</strong>). The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run Configurations</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_configurations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Development in Eclipse" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85c7fe6-7a02-4c0e-be8f-abb4e2c1051a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the MagicDraw <span>(</span><span>or other according to a modeling tool you are using</span> <span>)</span> installation directory (<ac:link ac:anchor="step2"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[see step #2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Development in Eclipse" /></ac:link>) and two plugins from the Eclipse Workspace. Thus, if the <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property is not defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugins directories" /></ac:link>), developing plugins are not loaded. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><em>MagicDraw with all plugins</em> launches MagicDraw (or other modeling tool) with plugins available in the IDE workspace namely <em>My Plugin 1</em> and <em>My Plugin 2</em>.</li><li><em>My Commandline</em> launches batch mode tool <em>My Commandline</em>.</li><li><em>MyPlugin1JUnit&lt;n&gt;Test</em> launches sample <em>JUnit&lt;n&gt;</em> test.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e64b65a-660c-4727-831d-c768b2c8581b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin.</span><br /><span>For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.<br />For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b219f48f-c5b4-47e9-9921-be356cbf7674"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar files</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> that are required by your plugin</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> from appropriate plugins. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437455 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00359: (2026x) Development in IntelliJ IDEA

- page_id: `254437455`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437455/2026x+Development+in+IntelliJ+IDEA
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:32.753+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Developing plugins using IDE
- labels: ['setup-intellij-idea-environment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The preconfigured IntelliJ IDEA project with modules for two sample plugins and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] (command-line) tool is provided with a program installation directory. The project can be found in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.*

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.

To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (or other program according to a modeling tool you are using) development

1. In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.

2. Extract *<MagicDraw installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip*to a chosen directory.

3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project *MagicDraw development.ipr*from that directory.

The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the *md.plugins.dir* java system property is not defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugins directories' space='']), developing plugins are not loaded.

After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.

The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin. 
For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies' space=''] page.

When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar filesthat are required by your pluginfrom appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib* and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins* and its sub directories.

Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin *.jar* file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this *.jar*file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>The preconfigured IntelliJ IDEA project with modules for two sample plugins and a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Running programs in batch mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[batch mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (command-line) tool  is provided with a program installation directory. The project can be found in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool  installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.</em></p><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">or other program according to a modeling tool you are using</span>) development</p><hr /><ol><li>In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.</li></ol><p>      2. Extract <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip </em>to a chosen <span>directory </span>.</p><p>      3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project <em>MagicDraw development.ipr </em>from that <span>directory </span>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="979e476a-c332-4829-b545-e533465a22a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property is not defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugins directories" /></ac:link>), developing plugins are not loaded.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c27f445-666e-4c1f-918f-1b0d5c52c5fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin.</span><br /><span>For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.<br /></span><span>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="535eba12-13fe-4164-9536-bcf3bc0281c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar files</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> that are required by your plugin</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> from appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib</em> and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins</em> and its sub directories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b206bb9c-fbb2-4cd4-abdf-61dfd0080691"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin <em>.jar</em> file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this <em>.jar </em>file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437650 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00361: (2026x) Diagram presentation element

- page_id: `254437650`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437650/2026x+Diagram+presentation+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:50.022+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement* is a one to one mapping to UML *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram*. It means for every *Diagram* in a model exists one and only one *DiagramPresentationElement*. Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(Diagram[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-)* to get it for a Diagram.

The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:

- To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements() method. This method returns only direct children of the diagram.
- To open a diagram in the program UI, call DiagramPresentationElement.open() .
- To create/modify/delete inner presentation elements of the diagram, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em> is a one to one mapping to UML <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</em>. It means for every <em>Diagram</em> in a model exists one and only one <em>DiagramPresentationElement</em>. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(Diagram<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-">)</a></em> to get it for a Diagram.</p><p>The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:</p><ul><li>To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()</em> method. This method returns only direct children of the diagram.</li><li>To open a diagram in the program UI, call <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">DiagramPresentationElement.open()</em>.</li><li>To create/modify/delete inner presentation elements of the diagram, use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager</em>.<br /><br /></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437654 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00362: (2026x) Diagram types

- page_id: `254437654`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437654/2026x+Diagram+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:50.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two groups of diagrams in our modeling tools – creatable and not creatable. Only the diagrams of a creatable type can be created (instantiated). A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.

There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:

- Class
- Use Case
- Object
- Communication
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Activity
- Composite Structure
- Component
- Deployment
- Package
- Profile

Not creatable diagrams:

- Interaction
- Behavior
- Any
- Static

Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. Activity, Interaction, State Machine, and Protocol State Machine diagrams are subdiagrams of the Behavior diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The only way to add a new diagram type to the modeling tool is to extend one of the already existing diagram types and register it. This mechanism is described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding new diagram types' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

Only the diagrams of a creatable type can be created (instantiated). A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.</p><p>There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:</p><ul><li>Class</li><li>Use Case</li><li>Object</li><li>Communication</li><li>Sequence</li><li>State Machine</li><li>Protocol State Machine</li><li>Activity</li><li>Composite Structure</li><li>Component</li><li>Deployment</li><li>Package</li><li>Profile</li></ul><p><br />Not creatable diagrams:</p><ul><li>Interaction</li><li>Behavior</li><li>Any</li><li>Static</li></ul><p>Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. Activity, Interaction, State Machine, and Protocol State Machine diagrams are subdiagrams of the Behavior diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_type_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The only way to add a new diagram type to the modeling tool is to extend one of the already existing diagram types and register it. This mechanism is described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding new diagram types" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d2f565e-f93a-44ee-a965-cd4978a35814"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb619759-b916-4ba7-89cb-addce6204b76"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
