<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437267 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00394: (2026x) INumberingAction interface

- page_id: `254437267`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437267/2026x+INumberingAction+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:17.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Custom elements numbering
- labels: ['auto-number', 'numbering-scheme', 'number-part']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

What if we want to customize the numbering of elements even further? Let's say we need to number uml:Activities depending on their *getStructuredNode()*value. By implementing the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.INumberingAction* interface, it is possible to create the numbering that follow arbitrary conditions, this comes of course at the cost of increased complexity. The implementation must calculate the values for the numbers all by itself.

There are two parts to be explained that pertain to this interface. The actual interface that provides access to customized numbering and an immutable support class which encapsulates the values as defined in the *AutoNumber* and the associated *NumberingScheme* objects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The *INumberingAction*Interface**. The interface has one method, which is invoked whenever a new *Element* is created or when **Create**or **Renumber**buttons are clicked in the **Element Numbering** dialog. This applies of course only if the Element type equals the type for which the *AutoNumber* has been customized. So at the time when a new element is created, this method receives a *java.util.List* with a single entry. On the other hand, when the user navigates the **Element Numbering**dialog, the method receives all elements that are currently listed in the right partition of the dialog. This implies that all elements have the same owner. Since this will give access to the complete Model Tree, it is possible to collect necessary information from other parts of the model and incorporate it. This method must return a mapping of each element together with the value that was generated for it. There is no need to number every element that the method receives. The Numbering Framework will register the generated values internally and set the property value for each element.

**The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.NumberingInfo* support object.**This is an immutable Java Bean object which provides the implementer of the***INumberingAction* interface with all the information as defined in the *AutoNumber* and *NumberingScheme* objects:

- getName() returns the name of the AutoNumber .
- isDefaultNumber() returns the AutoNumber.defaultNumber value.
- getScheme() returns the AutoNumber.numberingScheme value, which is the NumberingScheme used to number this type of element.
- getProperty() returns the AutoNumber.numberedProperty value, which is the Property that will hold the value of the calculated number.
- getTarget() returns the AutoNumber.getTarget() value, which is the type of the elements to be numbered.
- isDisplayedProperty() returns true, if the value of the above getProperty() is displayed in the GUI. An element type can have more than one numbered Property , but only one can be displayed on symbols or in the Element Tree.
- getPrefix() returns the prefix as modified by the user in the Element Numbering dialog, which can be different from the AutoNumber.getPrefix() value (see the following figure).
- getSeparator() returns the separator as modified by the user in the Element Numbering dialog. In the implemented Numbering Framework, this value will override all the NumberParts of a type separator (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information on how to create Numbering customizations with «AutoNumber», «NumberingScheme», and «NumberPart» elements, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with generic numbering mechanism' space='MT'].

###### **Constraints**

A *NumberingScheme* that references an expression which implements this interface, can have one and only one *NumberPart*. Namely a *NumberPart* that references an *INumberingAction*.

###### **Example #1**

We shall now proceed to show a Java class that generates numbers for UML activities depending on their *getStructuredNode()* value. Since this class implements the***INumberingAction* interface, it should be set as the expression in the only *NumberPart* of this *NumberingScheme*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**

We have created a few activities inside a sample *Package*. With the creation of each new activity, an ID is generated and assigned to it. We then add some *StructuredActivityNodes* into two of the given activities. Now, in order to assign *Numbers* to these *Activities* that includes the number of *StructuredActivityNodes* children, we have to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MT']**Element Numbering**dialog and renumber the Activities. This will generate new numbers that reflect the ownership relation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Let's have a look at the source code. This is a very simple example, which differentiates between receiving one or many elements, and calculates the values accordingly. This class can be found in *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample*.

```java
generateIds(List elements, NumberingInfo nInfo) {
            Map idMap = new HashMap();
            if (! elements.isEmpty()) {
 
                // the NumberingInfo object contains the values set in the customization
                String baseId = nInfo.getPrefix() + nInfo.getSeparator();
 
                // when creating an Activity, this will be called with a single element 
                if (elements.size() == 1) {
                    Element e = elements.iterator().next();
                    Class>[] types = new Class>[]{Activity.class};
                    Collection extends Element> acts = ModelHelper.getElementsOfType(e.getOwner(), types, false, true);
                    String id = baseId + (acts.size());
                    idMap.put(e, id);

 
                    // when renumbering in the dialog this will be called with multiple elements
                } else {
                    int counter = 1;
 
                    // we sort so that renumber will get the same order
                    // normally this would ask for a custom comparator class
                    // but it is left out for brevity's sake
                    Collections.sort(elements);
                    for (Element e : elements) {
                        if (e instanceof Activity) { 
                            Activity act = (Activity) e; 
                            String id = baseId + counter; 
                            counter ++;
                            Collection nodes = act.getStructuredNode();
 
                            // we simply attach the suffix '$node_' and the node count 
                            if (nodes != null && !nodes.isEmpty()) { 
                                id += "$node_" + nodes.size();
                            }
                            idMap.put(e, id);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            // returning the mapping of elements and ids, so that the framework can register them
            return idMap;
        }
    }]]>
```

###### **Example #2**

A more complicated example can be found the OpenAPI packages *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.custo*. It shows an implementation that creates numbers for elements connected through UML Relationships. The UML diagram shows the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.custom.DirectedAssociationNumbering* class, which is customized to number element types that are connected by «DirectedAssociation» links.

The interested Reader should consult the source code, in order to learn how such an algorithm has been implemented.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*Example of relationship numbering (fragment)*

**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>What if we want to customize the numbering of elements even further? Let's say we need to number uml:Activities depending on their <em>getStructuredNode() </em>value. By implementing the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.INumberingAction</em> interface, it is possible to create the numbering that follow arbitrary conditions, this comes of course at the cost of increased complexity. The implementation must calculate the values for the numbers all by itself.</p><p>There are two parts to be explained that pertain to this interface. The actual interface that provides access to customized numbering and an immutable support class which encapsulates the values as defined in the <em>AutoNumber</em> and the associated <em>NumberingScheme</em> objects.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_Numberingaction_interface_and_numberingInfo_support_object.png" /></ac:image></p><div class="WordSection1"><p><strong>The <em>INumberingAction </em>Interface</strong>. The interface has one method, which is invoked whenever a new <em>Element</em> is created or when <strong>Create </strong>or <strong>Renumber </strong>buttons are clicked in the <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog. This applies of course only if the Element type equals the type for which the <em>AutoNumber</em> has been customized. So at the time when a new element is created, this method receives a <em>java.util.List</em> with a single entry. On the other hand, when the user navigates the <strong>Element Numbering </strong>dialog, the method receives all elements that are currently listed in the right partition of the dialog. This implies that all elements have the same owner. Since this will give access to the complete Model Tree, it is possible to collect necessary information from other parts of the model and incorporate it. This method must return a mapping of each element together with the value that was generated for it. There is no need to number every element that the method receives. The Numbering Framework will register the generated values internally and set the property value for each element.</p><p><strong>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.NumberingInfo</em> support object. </strong>This is an immutable Java Bean object which provides the implementer of the<em> </em><em>INumberingAction</em> interface with all the information as defined in the <em>AutoNumber</em> and <em>NumberingScheme</em> objects:</p><ul><li><em>getName()</em> returns the name of the <em>AutoNumber</em>.</li><li><em>isDefaultNumber()</em> returns the <em>AutoNumber.defaultNumber</em> value.</li><li><em>getScheme()</em> returns the <em>AutoNumber.numberingScheme</em> value, which is the <em>NumberingScheme</em> used to number this type of element.</li><li><em>getProperty()</em> returns the <em>AutoNumber.numberedProperty</em> value, which is the <em>Property</em> that will hold the value of the calculated number.</li><li><em>getTarget()</em> returns the <em>AutoNumber.getTarget()</em> value, which is the type of the elements to be numbered.</li><li><em>isDisplayedProperty()</em> returns true, if the value of the above <em>getProperty()</em> is displayed in the GUI. An element type can have more than one numbered <em>Property</em>, but only one can be displayed on symbols or in the Element Tree.</li><li><em>getPrefix()</em> returns the prefix as modified by the user in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element Numbering </strong>dialog, which can be different from the <em>AutoNumber.getPrefix()</em> value (see the following figure).</li><li><em>getSeparator()</em> returns the separator as modified by the user in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element Numbering </strong>dialog. In the implemented Numbering Framework, this value will override all the <em>NumberParts</em> of a type separator (see the following figure).</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Modified Separator and Prefix values (fragment of Element Numbering dialog)" ac:alt="Modified Separator and Prefix values (fragment of Element Numbering dialog)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="modified_separator_and_prefix_values.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>For more information on how to create Numbering customizations with «AutoNumber», «NumberingScheme», and «NumberPart» elements, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Working with generic numbering mechanism" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Generic Numbering Mechanism]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><h4><strong>Constraints</strong></h4><p>A <em>NumberingScheme</em> that references an expression which implements this interface, can have one and only one <em>NumberPart</em>. Namely a <em>NumberPart</em> that references an <em>INumberingAction</em>.</p><h4><strong>Example #1</strong></h4><p>We shall now proceed to show a Java class that generates numbers for UML activities depending on their <em>getStructuredNode()</em> value. Since this class implements the<em> </em><em>INumberingAction</em> interface, it should be set as the expression in the only <em>NumberPart</em> of this <em>NumberingScheme</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="ActivityScheme numbering scheme (fragment)" ac:alt="ActivityScheme numbering scheme (fragment)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activityScheme_numbering_scheme.png" /></ac:image></p><p><em><br /></em></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>We have created a few activities inside a sample <em>Package</em>. With the creation of each new activity, an ID is generated and assigned to it. We then add some <em>StructuredActivityNodes</em> into two of the given activities. Now, in order to assign <em>Numbers</em> to these <em>Activities</em> that includes the number of <em>StructuredActivityNodes</em> children, we have to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element Numbering </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and renumber the Activities. This will generate new numbers that reflect the ownership relation.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Activities and Structural Nodes before and after renumbering using Element Numbering dialog" ac:alt="Activities and Structural Nodes before and after renumbering using Element Numbering dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Activities and Structural Nodes before and after renumbering using Element Numbering dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></div><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><p>Let's have a look at the source code. This is a very simple example, which differentiates between receiving one or many elements, and calculates the values accordingly. This class can be found in <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b687e352-9cd5-43f5-80d6-572301180f43"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class ActivityNodeNumbering implements INumberingAction {
        @Override
        public Map<Element, String> generateIds(List<Element> elements, NumberingInfo nInfo) {
            Map<Element, String> idMap = new HashMap<Element, String>();
            if (! elements.isEmpty()) {
 
                // the NumberingInfo object contains the values set in the customization
                String baseId = nInfo.getPrefix() + nInfo.getSeparator();
 
                // when creating an Activity, this will be called with a single element 
                if (elements.size() == 1) {
                    Element e = elements.iterator().next();
                    Class<?>[] types = new Class<?>[]{Activity.class};
                    Collection<? extends Element> acts = ModelHelper.getElementsOfType(e.getOwner(), types, false, true);
                    String id = baseId + (acts.size());
                    idMap.put(e, id);

 
                    // when renumbering in the dialog this will be called with multiple elements
                } else {
                    int counter = 1;
 
                    // we sort so that renumber will get the same order
                    // normally this would ask for a custom comparator class
                    // but it is left out for brevity's sake
                    Collections.sort(elements);
                    for (Element e : elements) {
                        if (e instanceof Activity) { 
                            Activity act = (Activity) e; 
                            String id = baseId + counter; 
                            counter ++;
                            Collection<StructuredActivityNode> nodes = act.getStructuredNode();
 
                            // we simply attach the suffix '$node_' and the node count 
                            if (nodes != null && !nodes.isEmpty()) { 
                                id += "$node_" + nodes.size();
                            }
                            idMap.put(e, id);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            // returning the mapping of elements and ids, so that the framework can register them
            return idMap;
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example #2</strong></h4><p>A more complicated example can be found the OpenAPI packages <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.custo</em>. It shows an implementation that creates numbers for elements connected through UML Relationships. The UML diagram shows the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.custom.DirectedAssociationNumbering</em> class, which is customized to number element types that are connected by «DirectedAssociation» links.</p><p>The interested Reader should consult the source code, in order to learn how such an algorithm has been implemented.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_relationship_numbering.png" /></ac:image></p><p><em>Example of relationship numbering (fragment)</em></p><p><em><br /></em></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437447 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00397: (2026x) JavaScript migration from Nashorn to Rhino engine

- page_id: `254437447`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437447/2026x+JavaScript+migration+from+Nashorn+to+Rhino+engine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:31.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['js', 'javascript', 'nashorn', 'rhino']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The JavaScript *Nashorn* has been removed from the modeling tool ([learn more about deprecated JavaScript Nashorn](https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/335)). Please use JavaScript *Rhino* instead.

To find the usages of the JavaScript *Nashorn* in the project, run the**Deprecated JavaScript**validation suite (**Analyze**>**Validation**>**Validate**).

The key differences are listed below. For more information, please check [Nashorn/Rhino Migration Guide](https://wiki.openjdk.java.net/display/Nashorn/Rhino+Migration+Guide).

###### Class object and .class property

If a java API accepts a `java.lang.Class` object, in *Nashorn* "`.class`" property (similar to Java) is used. In *Rhino* you pass script representation of class "as is".

```js

```

###### Accessing Java packages and classes from script

*Nashorn*'s recommended way to access Java classes (by using `Java.type`) is not supported in *Rhino*.

```js

```

###### Creating Java arrays from script

In *Nashorn*, you can resolve to a Java array class using the same `Java.type` API. And array creation is done using `new` operator. In *Rhino*, you create a Java array using Java reflection from script.

```js

```

###### Java exceptions

In *Nashorn* Java exception objects are thrown "as is". *Rhino* wraps Java exceptions as a script object. If you want underlying Java exception, use "`javaException`" property to access it.

```js
} 
catch (e)
{
    // Nashorn
    e.printStackTrace()
    // Rhino
    e.javaException.printStackTrace()
}]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> has been removed from the modeling tool (<a href="https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/335" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">learn more about deprecated JavaScript Nashorn</a>). Please use JavaScript <em>Rhino</em> instead.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="741ddd1a-e9e5-44f7-bd08-f5442b90bb69"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>To find the usages of the JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> in the project, run the </span><strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong><span> validation suite (</span><strong>Analyze</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Validation</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Validate</strong><span>).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The key differences are listed below. For more information, please check <a href="https://wiki.openjdk.java.net/display/Nashorn/Rhino+Migration+Guide">Nashorn/Rhino Migration Guide</a>.</span></p><h4>Class object and .class property</h4><p>If a java API accepts a <code>java.lang.Class</code> object, in <em>Nashorn</em> &quot;<code>.class</code>&quot; property (similar to Java) is used. In <em>Rhino</em> you pass script representation of class &quot;as is&quot;.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de79df2b-dc41-4e29-96a3-c977d53eb9e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// Nashorn
var types = [com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class]
// Rhino
var types = [com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class]

var project = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.getInstance().getProject()
var Finder = com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder
Finder.byTypeRecursively().find(project, types, false)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Accessing Java packages and classes from script</span></h4><p><span><em>Nashorn</em>'s recommended way to access Java classes (by using <code>Java.type</code>) is not supported in <em>Rhino</em>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa704280-2718-408e-a95a-6ecebdd8c756"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// Nashorn
var Vector = Java.type("java.util.Vector")
var JFrame = Java.type("javax.swing.JFrame")

// Rhino
var Vector = java.util.Vector
var JFrame = Packages.javax.swing.JFrame]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating Java arrays from script</h4><p>In <em>Nashorn</em>, you can resolve to a Java array class using the same <code>Java.type</code> API. And array creation is done using <code>new</code> operator. In <em>Rhino</em>, you create a Java array using Java reflection from script.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd4ea3e8-83a4-47ce-b902-ffd3ab4285b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// Nashorn
var IntArray = Java.type("int[]")
var array = new IntArray(8)

// Rhino
var Array = java.lang.reflect.Array
var intClass = java.lang.Integer.TYPE
var array = Array.newInstance(intClass, 8)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Java exceptions</h4><p>In <em>Nashorn</em> Java exception objects are thrown &quot;as is&quot;. <em>Rhino</em> wraps Java exceptions as a script object. If you want underlying Java exception, use &quot;<code>javaException</code>&quot; property to access it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6221a914-ad37-4036-a565-aec168c5a659"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[try
{
    <...>
} 
catch (e)
{
    // Nashorn
    e.printStackTrace()
    // Rhino
    e.javaException.printStackTrace()
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437332 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00398: (2026x) Jython Scripting

- page_id: `254437332`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437332/2026x+Jython+Scripting
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:22.597+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['jython', 'python']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

679375408

679375410

679375409

Modeling tools allow you to access open API using the Jython scripting language.

Jython is an implementation of the high-level, dynamic, and object-oriented language Python which is seamlessly integrated with the Java platform.

Using Jython you may access all java API and MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) open API. This allows to avoid compilation and to get the same results without a java code. Using scripting you may do everything that you can achieve using a java plugin, and even more: you may change your code without recompiling and restarting an application.

More information about Jython you can find at [http://www.jython.org](http://www.jython.org/). Information about the python language you can find at [http://www.python.org](http://www.python.org/).

On every startup, program checks for scripts in *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/*. If there are subdirectories, each of them are scanned for the *script.xml*file. This file provides information about the script in this directory. A file is similar to a plugin descriptor described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']. If *script.xml*contains valid information, the script file specified in *script.xml*is executed. The script file should contain a valid Jython 2.7.2 script.

679375407

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f934e4e4-78cf-4619-80ce-1a4422fb4630"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">679375408</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a39947-c95a-40d1-a313-174615c5c86e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">679375410</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfd54cca-360e-40be-ab55-920c6f368cc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">679375409</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Modeling tools allow you to access open API using the Jython scripting language.</p><p>Jython is an implementation of the high-level, dynamic, and object-oriented language Python which is seamlessly integrated with the Java platform.</p><p>Using Jython you may access all java API and MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) open API. This allows to avoid compilation and to get the same results without a java code. Using scripting you may do everything that you can achieve using a java plugin, and even more: you may change your code without recompiling and restarting an application.</p><p>More information about Jython you can find at <a style="line-height: 1.4285715;" href="http://www.jython.org/"><u>http://ww</u><u>w</u><u>.jython.org</u></a>. Information about the python language you can find at <a href="http://www.python.org/" style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><u>http://ww</u><u>w</u><u>.python.org</u></a>.</p><p>On every startup, program checks for scripts in <em>plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/</em>. If there are subdirectories, each of them are scanned for the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">script.xml </em>file. This file provides information about the script in this directory. A file is similar to a plugin descriptor described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link>. If <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">script.xml </em>contains valid information, the script file specified in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">script.xml </em>is executed. The script file should contain a valid Jython 2.7.2 script.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33009826-4d09-48d2-93ce-3249dc19e1ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">679375407</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="da9215b1-463e-4299-9093-1f4e3b460f7c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437263 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00399: (2026x) Layouting symbols in the diagram

- page_id: `254437263`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437263/2026x+Layouting+symbols+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We provide an API to layout presentation elements in a diagram using different strategies (layouter), see:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.Layouting

```java

```

```java
symbolsToLayout = ...;
diagramView.setSelected(symbolsToLayout);
Layouting.layout(diagramView);]]>
```

```java

```

Make sure the diagram is opened and the session is closed before doing a diagram layout. These are the current limitation of layout API

****You can find more code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\testframework, com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.testframework.DiagramLayoutTest class

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>We provide an API to layout presentation elements in a diagram using different strategies (layouter), see:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.Layouting</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ac6a0b-6125-4db1-b0a8-114922bf271d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//layout using the default diagram layouter
AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagramView = ...;
diagramView.open();
Layouting.layout(diagramView);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb32900-2972-4178-b314-41e761004ebc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//layout the selected symbols in the diagram using the default diagram layouter
AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagramView = ...;
diagramView.open();

List<PresentationElement> symbolsToLayout = ...;
diagramView.setSelected(symbolsToLayout);
Layouting.layout(diagramView);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25409d16-6ae6-48a7-8d97-7f5d204b5cf6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//layout using any of the available layouters
AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram  = ...;
diagram.open()
Layouting.layout(diagram, Layouting.HIERARCHIC_DIAGRAM_LAYOUTER);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c01ad21-78ba-4b88-b72c-a7879dd4e67e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the diagram is opened and the session is closed before doing a diagram layout. These are the current limitation of layout API</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find more code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\testframework, com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.testframework.DiagramLayoutTest <u>class</u></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7849a87-66ab-4f1a-96c8-df51caad3ac7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437427 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00400: (2026x) LDAP configuration description

- page_id: `254437427`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437427/2026x+LDAP+configuration+description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:29.080+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes the key-values for changing the LDAP configuration via REST API:

| Key-value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| "java.naming.factory.initial" | An optional key-value describing the initial context factory to be used, such as "com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory". |
| "com.sun.jndi.ldap.read.timeout" | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for an LDAP request or a read timeout, e.g., "10000" |
| "weight" | An optional LDAP weight value used to order the LDAP realm for authentication, such as "1", "2", "3". |
| "enabled" | The key-value describing if the LDAP realm is enabled ("true") or disabled ("false"). |
| "com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.timeout" | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the LDAP provider to establish connection, e.g., "5000". If connection is not established within a timeout period, it is aborted. |
| "searchbase" | The starting point of the search in the LDAP directory tree, such as "dc=example,dc=com". |
| "query" | The LDAP search filter value for finding, retrieving, and importing users (used when "authen_dntype" : "query"). The value depends on the LDAP server, e.g., "(uid={0})" or "(&(cn={0})(objectClass=user))". |
| "usergroup_query" | The LDAP search filter value for finding, retrieving, and importing user groups. The value depends on the LDAP server, e.g., "(cn={0})" or "(&(cn={0})(objectClass=group))". |
| "authen_dntype" | The authentication type value. Use the "template" value when one-level search can be used to login. Use the "query" value when sub-level search can be used to login. |
| "userDNTemplate" | The user template value used to search for users by a specific user path in one-level scope. The user name in the user path will be "{0}", e.g., "uid={0},dc=example,dc=com". |
| "anoymousbind" | The anonymous binding allows to connect and search without logging in to the LDAP server. Normally, the value is "false". This value can be used only when the LDAP server allows it. |
| "ldap_realm_name" | The name of the LDAP realm, such as "Apache LDAP" or "AD1". |
| "java.naming.security.protocol" | The protocol for connecting to the LDAP server, such as "ssl" or "none". |
| "key_cer_file_content" | The SSL key file content. If "java.naming.security.protocol" is "none", the value can be "". |
| "protocol" | The LDAP protocol, e.g., "ldap". |
| "port" | The LDAP port, e.g., "10389". |
| "IP" | The server IP address, e.g., "127.0.0.1". |
| "ID" | The ID of the LDAP realm. This value is generated from the Teamwork Cloud server. |
| "authen" | The authentication type. Use the "simple" value for a clear-text password. Use the "sasl_mech" value for a space-separated list of the SASL mechanism names. You can use one of the SASL mechanisms, e.g., "CRAM-MD5" which means that the CRAM-MD5 SASL mechanism described in RFC 2195 will be used. |
| "userName" | The user name used to log in to the LDAP server, such as "uid=admin,ou=system". |
| "password" | The password used to log in to the LDAP server. |
| "url" | The URL constructed from "IP", "port", and "protocol", e.g., "ldap://127.0.0.1:10389". |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following table describes the key-values for changing the LDAP configuration via REST API:</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 65.9239%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.8944%;" /><col style="width: 81.1056%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Key-value</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;java.naming.factory.initial&quot;</strong></td><td><span>An optional key-value describing the initial context factory to be used, such as &quot;com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;com.sun.jndi.ldap.read.timeout&quot;</strong></td><td><p><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for an LDAP request or a read timeout, e.g., <span>&quot;10000&quot;</span></span></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;weight&quot;</strong></td><td><span>An optional LDAP weight value used to order the LDAP realm for authentication, such as &quot;1&quot;, &quot;2&quot;, &quot;3&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;enabled&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The key-value describing if the LDAP realm is enabled (&quot;true&quot;) or disabled (&quot;false&quot;).</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.timeout&quot;</strong></td><td><p><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the <span>LDAP provider to establish connection, e.g., <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">&quot;5000&quot;</span>. If connection is not established within a timeout period, it is aborted.</span></span></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;searchbase&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The starting point of the search in the LDAP directory tree, such as &quot;dc=example,dc=com&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;query&quot;</strong></td><td><p>The LDAP search filter value for finding<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, retrieving, and importing users</span> (used when &quot;authen_dntype&quot; : &quot;query&quot;). The value depends on the LDAP server, e.g., &quot;(uid={0})&quot; or &quot;(&amp;(cn={0})(objectClass=user))&quot;.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;usergroup_query&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The LDAP search filter value for<span> finding</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, retrieving, and importing </span>user groups. The value depends on the LDAP server, e.g., &quot;(cn={0})&quot; or &quot;(&amp;(cn={0})(objectClass=group))&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;authen_dntype&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The authentication type value. Use the <span>&quot;template&quot; value</span> <span>when one-level search can be used to login. Use the &quot;query&quot; value <span>when sub-level search can be used to login.</span> </span></span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;userDNTemplate&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The user template value used to search for users by a specific user path <span>in</span><span> one-level scope</span>. The user name in the user path will be &quot;{0}&quot;, e.g., &quot;uid={0},dc=example,dc=com&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;anoymousbind&quot;</strong></td><td><span><span>The anonymous binding allows to connect and search without logging in to the LDAP server. Normally, the value is &quot;false&quot;. </span>This value can be used only when the LDAP server allows it.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;ldap_realm_name&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The name of the LDAP realm, such as &quot;Apache LDAP&quot; or &quot;AD1&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;java.naming.security.protocol&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The protocol for connecting to the LDAP server, such as &quot;ssl&quot; or &quot;none&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;key_cer_file_content&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The SSL key file content. If &quot;java.naming.security.protocol&quot; is &quot;none&quot;, the value can be &quot;&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>&quot;protocol&quot;</strong></td><td><span>The LDAP protocol, e.g., &quot;ldap&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;port&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The LDAP port, e.g., &quot;10389&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;IP&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The server IP address, e.g., &quot;127.0.0.1&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;ID&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The ID of the LDAP realm. This value is generated from the Teamwork Cloud server.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;authen&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The authentication type. Use the &quot;simple&quot; value for a clear-text password. Use the &quot;<span>sasl_mech&quot; value for a<span> space-separated list of the SASL mechanism names. <span>You can use one of the SASL mechanisms, e.g., &quot;CRAM-MD5&quot; which means that the CRAM-MD5 SASL mechanism described in RFC 2195 will be used.</span></span></span></span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;userName&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The user name used to log in to the LDAP server, such as &quot;uid=admin,ou=system&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;password&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1">The password used to log in to the LDAP server.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>&quot;url&quot;</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>The URL constructed from &quot;IP&quot;, &quot;port&quot;, and &quot;protocol&quot;, e.g., &quot;<a href="ldap://127.0.0.1:10389">ldap://127.0.0.1:10389</a>&quot;.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437449 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00401: (2026x) Licensing information

- page_id: `254437449`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437449/2026x+Licensing+information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:55.208+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Providing licensing information

It is possible to provide the tool with the licensing information using the Java (JVM) system properties.

You can specify either seat or floating license.

- Seat license (license file) properties:

| Property | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| LICENSE_FILE_FORCE_USAGE | true | when specified, the tool tries using the license file specified in LICENSE_FILE property. Allows changing the license. |
| LICENSE_FILE | path to the license file or directory with the license files | when the tool has no license specified yet, or LICENSE_FILE_FORCE_USAGE is specified, the tool uses a license specified by LICENSE_FILE property.Surround the path with double quotes if it contains spaces. |

- Floating license (FlexNet license server) properties:

| Property | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FL_FORCE_USAGE | true | when specified, the tool uses a floating license. |
| FL_SERVER_ADDRESS | FlexNet license server name or IP address |  |
| FL_SERVER_PORT | FlexNet license server port number |  |
| FL_EDITION | tool edition | specify if the tool supports editions; otherwise, omit. |

-D must be prepended to the property name if the Java system property is specified through java command-line arguments/parameters.

E.g., in the tool launcher properties file (bin/<launcher name>.properties), add floating license properties:

JAVA_ARGS=<...> -DFL_FORCE_USAGE\=true -DFL_SERVER_ADDRESS\=myServer -DFL_SERVER_PORT\=1101 -DFL_EDITION\=Enterprise

or specify license file:

JAVA_ARGS=<...> -DLICENSE_FILE\="c:/my licenses/licenseFile.txt"

##### Getting licensing information

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.license.utils.LicenseUtils* to check some licensing information (edition, is it a trial or evaluation version).

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Configure test environment' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Allows changing the license.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">LICENSE_FILE</td><td colspan="1">path to the license file or directory with the license files</td><td colspan="1"><p>when the tool has no license specified yet, or LICENSE_FILE_FORCE_USAGE is specified, the tool uses a license specified by LICENSE_FILE property.</p><p><em>Surround the path with double quotes if it contains spaces.</em></p></td></tr></tbody></table><ul><li>Floating license (FlexNet license server) properties:</li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="width: 95.6522%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 23.7055%;" /><col class="" style="width: 22.7531%;" /><col class="" style="width: 53.5503%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Property</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class="" style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><td>FL_FORCE_USAGE</td><td>true</td><td>when specified, the tool uses a floating license.</td></tr><tr class="" style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><td>FL_SERVER_ADDRESS</td><td>FlexNet license server name or IP address</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr class="" style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><td colspan="1">FL_SERVER_PORT</td><td colspan="1">FlexNet license server port number</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr class="" style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><td colspan="1">FL_EDITION</td><td colspan="1">tool edition</td><td colspan="1">specify if the tool supports editions; -DFL_FORCE_USAGE\=true -DFL_SERVER_ADDRESS\=myServer -DFL_SERVER_PORT\=1101 -DFL_EDITION\=Enterprise</p><p>or specify license file:</p><p>JAVA_ARGS=&lt;...&gt; -DLICENSE_FILE\=&quot;c:/my licenses/licenseFile.txt&quot;</p><h3>Getting licensing information</h3><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.license.utils.LicenseUtils</em> to check some licensing information (edition, is it a trial or evaluation version).</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configure test environment" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437577 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00402: (2026x) Listening to property change events

- page_id: `254437577`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437577/2026x+Listening+to+property+change+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:46.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *PropertyChangeEvents* support provides the ability to listen to the event by different scopes. The scope depends on how the listener is registered:

- If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.
- If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.
- If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.
- If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.

The *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:

- To listen for the whole repository, use:

```java

```

This listener will get all the property change events from any element.

- To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is performed, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.

- To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.

- To listen for property changes defined by the specified config of the specific element type, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.

*EventSupport* could be disabled from the event firing:

- To check if it is enabled, use:

```java

```

- To stop/ start event firing, use:

```java

```

These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.

###### **Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:**

```java

```

###### **Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:**

```java

```

````html

The scope depends on how the listener is registered:</p><ul><li>If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.</li><li>If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.</li><li>If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.</li><li>If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.</li></ul><p>The <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:</p><ul><li>To listen for the whole repository, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffb93836-0efa-4a84-81ac-387a95a341c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, (RefObject)null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will get all the property change events from any element.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ul><li>To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, UML2MetamodelConstants.BEFORE_DELETE);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.</p><p><br /></p><ul><li>To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c70a72de-22c3-4b7a-bae0-1256621e191d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		element.addPropertyChangeListener(listener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.</p><p><br /></p><ul><li><p>To listen for property changes defined by the specified config of the specific element type, use:</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="caa780fd-eb62-494e-adcb-9e57cf81aba5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getSmartEventSupport().register(new Registration(aClass, config, listener));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dce80e5-9c6e-41b1-b514-60eaa771f2dc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>EventSupport</em> could be disabled from the event firing:</p><ul><li>To check if it is enabled, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="971277da-d508-4924-a757-3d984f918130"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().isEnableEventFiring()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>To stop/ start event firing, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3628d536-4402-46cf-a926-c4a752530943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().setEnableEventFiring(...)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.</p><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c9d224a-fac7-4b80-b6f2-e788242ff04e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            // evt.getPropertyName() is changed
        }

    });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection3"><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0763d8c8-9067-4161-97d8-11a5c302c053"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            if (PropertyNames.NAME.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // name is Changed
            }
        }
   });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437583 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00403: (2026x) Listening to related elements in hierarchy events

- page_id: `254437583`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437583/2026x+Listening+to+related+elements+in+hierarchy+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:47.294+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener* class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig*, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.

The***SmartListenerConfig*class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.

Use the *SmartPropertyChangeListener*class*createSmartPropertyListener(...)* method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the *SmartListenerConfig*.

****If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the *removePropertyChangeListener(...**)***method.

****

**Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name**

```java

```

**Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the *"Is Abstract"* property**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener</em> class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig</em>, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.</p><p>The<em> </em><em>SmartListenerConfig </em>class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.</p><p>Use the <em>SmartPropertyChangeListener </em>class<em> createSmartPropertyListener(...)</em> method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the <em>SmartListenerConfig</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f47013c6-52be-40ae-ae38-90aad40b75da"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the <em>removePropertyChangeListener(...<strong>) </strong></em>method.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e455947-8c4a-48c6-b2c2-35fe80d95f6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   		Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); // listen to the element's name 
        config.listenToNested(PropertyNames.OWNER).listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); //listen to the element owner's name
 
        // create the listener
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
               // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the <em>&quot;Is Abstract&quot;</em> property</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90ac467b-5be6-4826-9d81-0decdf8ca156"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    	Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.IS_ABSTRACT); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.OWNER, config);
 
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
                // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437549 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00404: (2026x) Listening to transaction commit events on session closing

- page_id: `254437549`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437549/2026x+Listening+to+transaction+commit+events+on+session+closing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:42.459+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Event support
- labels: ['transaction-commit-listener']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The *com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener* is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.

The *TransactionCommitListener* contains *transactionCommited(java.util.Collection<java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent>)***method, which provides a collection of all *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)* that were executed in a transaction.

****

**Create a custom transaction commit listener:**

```java
events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]>
```

This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.

###### **Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project**

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener</em> is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.</p><p>The <em>TransactionCommitListener</em> contains <em>transactionCommited(java.util.Collection&lt;java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent&gt;)</em><em> </em>method, which provides a collection of all <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)</em> that were executed in a transaction.</p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Create a custom transaction commit listener:</strong></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30d64b3d-3441-4403-a926-9b85f42af2ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class MyTransactionListener implements TransactionCommitListener
    {
        public Runnable transactionCommited(final Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.</p><h4><strong>Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e23f7d2b-8475-417a-8dbc-c98449059aa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    MyTransactionListener transactionListener = new MyTransactionListener(); 
    TransactionManager transactionManager = project.getRepository().getTransactionManager();
    transactionManager.addTransactionCommitListener(transactionListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="491fee21-8713-4bf9-a6c9-2219abb1169b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437439 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00405: (2026x) MagicDraw file format

- page_id: `254437439`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437439/2026x+MagicDraw+file+format
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:30.638+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['zip-based-file-format', 'plain-text-file-format', 'xmi']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0

MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension "mdr".

##### File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up

The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard.

A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.

###### Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)

A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:

- Meta information about entries records.properties.
- Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.
- Project structure information:

*com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project*.

- Shared and not shared uml model information:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model,*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model*

- Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.
- Project options ends with

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.*

- Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.

###### Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)

Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.

****

###### Changes Related to Diagrams

Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension <mdOwnedDiagrams>. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The <mdOwnedDiagrams> extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.

Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named <modelExtension>. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in <mdOwnedViews>. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:

**Diagram > DiagramRepresentationObject > DiagramContentsDescriptor > BinaryObject > BinaryObject. streamContentID**

****

###### Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor

```xml
diagram.ecore
	CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo
	
		
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
		
	
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3>File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0</h3><p>MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension &quot;mdr&quot;.</p><h3>File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up</h3><p>The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard. </p><p>A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.</p><h4>Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:</span></p><ul><li>Meta information about entries records.properties.</li><li>Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.</li><li>Project structure information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project</em>.</p><ul><li>Shared and not shared uml model information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model, </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model</em></p><ul><li>Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.</li><li>Project options ends with </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.</em></p><ul><li>Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.</li></ul><p> </p><h4>Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)</h4><p>Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Changes Related to Diagrams</h4><p>Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt;. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt; extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.</p><p>Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named &lt;modelExtension&gt;. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in &lt;mdOwnedViews&gt;. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Diagram &gt; DiagramRepresentationObject &gt; DiagramContentsDescriptor &gt; BinaryObject &gt; BinaryObject. streamContentID</strong></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><strong style="color: rgb(61,61,61);line-height: 1.6666666;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da45d653-b7b0-4f71-aaa4-06f4252b8180"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<genmodel:GenModel xmi:version="2.0"
xmlns:xmi="http://www.omg.org/XMI" xmlns:ecore="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore"
xmlns:genmodel="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/GenModel" modelDirectory="/com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation/src"
modelPluginID="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation" modelName="Diagram
updateClasspath="false"
rootExtendsInterface="org.eclipse.emf.cdo.CDOObject" 
rootExtendsClass="org.eclipse.emf.internal.cdo.CDOObjectImpl"
reflectiveDelegation="true" importerID="org.eclipse.emf.importer.ecore"
featureDelegation="Reflective"
containmentProxies="true" complianceLevel="5.0" copyrightFields="false"
usedGenPackages="../../com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project/model/binary.genmodel#//binary"
classNamePattern="">
	<foreignModel>diagram.ecore</foreignModel>
	<modelPluginVariables>CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo</modelPluginVariables>
	<genPackages prefix="Diagram" basePackage="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation"
	disposableProviderFactory="true"
	ecorePackage="diagram.ecore#/">
		<genClasses image="false" ecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//
		AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject">
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/ID"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/type"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/umlType"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramProperties"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/initialFrameSizeSet"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/requiredFeature"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//
			AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramContents"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramStyleID"/></genClasses>
			<genClassesecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor">
			<genFeatures property="None" notify="false" createChild="false"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/representation"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterName"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterVersion"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/usedElements"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true" ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/ binaryObject"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/contentHash"/>
		</genClasses>
	</genPackages>
</genmodel:GenModel>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
